Phase 1C deferred work catalogue (set by 1B subagent): (a) trait::start currently uses SessionConfig::default() — Phase 1C must widen signature with RuntimeStartArgs{docker_bridge, mac, config} OR call runtime.manager().start_vm(...) directly via escape hatch. (b) LimaRuntime::ip shells out via 'limactl shell <vm> -- ip -4 -o addr show dev eth1' — Phase 1C should swap for NetworkInfo.vm_ip lookup (already persisted; one less round-trip; works pre-boot). TODO comment left at LimaRuntime::ip impl. (c) LimaRuntime::create is 'create inert VM only' — no clone, no boot, no agent install; the full create→boot→agent flow remains in AppState until Phase 1C decides whether to absorb it onto the trait or keep it on LimaRuntime as Lima-specific inherent methods. (d) GuestConnector still consumes Arc<LimaManager>; Phase 1C may refactor to Arc<dyn GuestTransport>. (e) disk_gb defaulting falls through to SessionConfig::default().disk_gb=20 when SessionSpec::disk_gb is None — Phase 1C may prefer error or daemon-level default upstream.

Phase 1C confirmed: LimaSpec is NOT a separate type. Lima-specific knobs live on BackendSpecific::Lima { hardened, memory_mb, cpus } and SessionSpec.template (Option<String>). The custom Lima YAML template flow now lives in LimaRuntime::create (was inlined in handler before). RuntimeStatus is 5-variant (Running/Stopped/Creating/Error/Unknown); VmStatus is 3-variant. Handlers fold Creating/Error into existing 'leave alone / Unknown' branches for backward compat — Phase 1D may revisit when richer status DTO becomes useful (post-container).

M11-S1 exit criteria fully met. Full E2E suite passed 46/46 in 45:34 against the rethreaded AppState — Lima behavior preserved end-to-end through the Arc<dyn SessionRuntime> dispatch surface. Hermetic 991/991, Lima integration 3/3, clippy -D warnings clean, fmt clean, grep sweep clean (no surviving state.lima refs; Arc<LimaManager> only in expected places: GuestConnector deferred to M11-S2, LimaRuntime internals, comments). kio-reviewer APPROVE with 0 critical and 3 doc-only Issues all fixed in this session.
artifacts: sandbox_core::backend::{SessionRuntime, GuestTransport, RuntimeHandle, RuntimeStatus, RuntimeStartArgs, ExitCode, AsyncReadWrite}

log.title: Route helper, users.conf, daemon startup validation
log.summary: M11-S2 delivered the privileged route helper and users.conf-driven allocation pool. Phase 2A: shared loader sandbox-core/src/users_conf.rs (UsersConfig/SubnetEntry/Cidr4 types, find_subnet_by_*, allows_uid lookups; 22 unit tests). Phase 2B: new sandbox-route-helper crate (6th workspace member) implementing the spec's eight-step authorization flow (caller uid via getuid, gateway-ip subnet match, allow_users membership, pidfd_open+setns into container netns, getifaddrs subnet enforcement, ip route replace default via gateway, single deny exit code 1 with stderr reasons; three deny-branch integration tests with Docker fixture and CappedHelper RAII). Phase 2C: daemon startup loads users.conf, resolves the daemon's uid → subnet, threads CIDR through NetworkManager::new (replacing with_defaults); resolve_allocation_pool extracted as pure helper with 3 unit tests; explicit eprintln!+match shape in main() to surface SandboxError Display rather than Box<dyn Error> Debug; From<UsersConfigError> for SandboxError mapping with test pinning file-path preservation; three integration_users_conf_startup black-box tests (file missing/malformed/no-matching-subnet). Phase 2D: docs/start/installation.md updated with users.conf section, sandbox-route-helper setcap section (NOT setuid), kernel 5.8+ row, and a one-line callout at top of Run tests pointing at the configuration section (resolves kio-reviewer's docs-ordering blocker). Verification gates: cargo nextest --workspace 1017 passed, make test-integration 30 passed (incl. 6 new tests), make test-e2e 46/46 passed in 50 min, clippy clean, fmt clean, no flakes; kio-reviewer NEEDS CHANGES → APPROVED after the one-line callout. Operator artifact: /etc/sandboxd/users.conf created on the dev VM with {"subnets":[{"cidr":"10.209.0.0/24","allow_users":["olek"]}]} root-owned 0644.
decisions: sandbox-route-helper exit-code policy: single deny exit code 1 across all eight steps; stderr reason text is the load-bearing payload. Rationale: distinct codes per step would invite scripts to take ad-hoc dependencies on internal step numbering. Tests assert on stderr substrings, not exit codes.
discoveries: M11-S2 scoping: NetworkManager already accepts arbitrary base CIDR via new(base, prefix_len) — daemon today calls with_defaults() hardcoded to 10.209.0.0/24; users.conf integration just swaps the call site at sandboxd/src/main.rs ~line 4694. nix 0.29 is in-tree (sandbox-deny-logger dep); use it for pidfd_open/setns/getuid/getpwuid_r in the helper rather than rustix. users.conf loader belongs in sandbox-core, fixed path /etc/sandboxd/users.conf, env-var override SANDBOX_USERS_CONF for tests; pattern follows sandbox-cli/src/presets/user.rs but hard-fail instead of warn-skip.

Phase 2A loader landed: 22 unit tests, full workspace 1013 passing. Public surface: UsersConfig/SubnetEntry/Cidr4 types, find_subnet_by_gateway_ip + find_subnet_by_uid + allows_uid lookups, load_users_config{,_from} loaders. nix features split across crates: sandbox-deny-logger has [net,socket,uio]; sandbox-core now has [user] for User::from_{name,uid}. From<UsersConfigError> for SandboxError DEFERRED — Phase 2C must decide between SandboxError::InvalidArgument-with-formatted-path or a typed extension.

Rust 2024 made std::env::set_var and remove_var unsafe — tests that flip env vars need unsafe blocks with save/restore to avoid leaking into sibling tests. Pattern landed in users_conf.rs tests; reuse for any future env-var tests.

9p filesystem on the cargo target/ directory in this Lima VM does NOT back security.capability xattrs — sudo setcap returns Operation not supported against cargo-built binaries. Workaround: copy binary to /tmp (ext4, real xattr backing) before setcap. Pattern landed in sandbox-route-helper integration tests as CappedHelper RAII struct; reuse for any future setcap-needing test. Use /tmp literally, not std::env::temp_dir() — $TMPDIR could be redirected back to 9p.

nix 0.29 does NOT expose pidfd_open in any feature combination (verified by grepping the registry source). Use libc::syscall(libc::SYS_pidfd_open, pid, 0u32) directly and read errno via *libc::__errno_location(). Wrap in OwnedFd via FromRawFd for RAII close. Bumping nix to a future version that exposes pidfd_open is a small follow-up cleanup.

Box<dyn Error>'s blanket From<E> impl short-circuits the  operator's coercion through SandboxError when used at main()'s top level — operators see Debug repr (FileNotFound("...")) instead of Display message. Workaround: explicit match + eprintln!("sandboxd: {err}") + return Err(err.into()) shape for any UsersConfigError or InvalidArgument coming from startup-validation. Pattern landed in sandboxd/src/main.rs.

NetworkManager::restore_from_infos already errors cleanly on out-of-pool persisted-session allocations (case b per Phase 2C handoff): block_index_for returns None for any subnet outside the configured pool; restore_from_infos maps to SandboxError::Network. No additional code needed for the rollback-with-altered-users.conf path; daemon refuses to start. Error message names offending subnet but not configured pool CIDR — minor enrichment opportunity, not blocking.

M11-S2 verification gates all green on combined M11-S1+S2 working tree: cargo nextest --workspace 1017 passed, make test-integration 30 passed (incl. 6 new M11-S2 tests), full make test-e2e 46/46 passed in 50 min wall-clock, clippy clean, fmt clean, no flakes (incl. previously-flaky github-repo test). kio-reviewer NEEDS CHANGES → blocker fixed (docs ordering callout in installation.md). Working tree remains uncommitted — see todo #65 for disentanglement before merge.

log.title: ContainerRuntime, lite image build, GET /backends
log.summary: M11-S3 closed: ContainerRuntime + ContainerTransport (3A), lite image build + ensure_image + first-use warning (3B), GET /backends sub-router endpoint (3C), AppState wiring + container POST /sessions path + SessionDto warnings (3D), and review fixes (uid mapping helper, workspace_modes EnumSet::empty, log demotion). All 4 verification gates green: 1038 unit + 43 integration tests pass, clippy clean, fmt clean. Container backend is now functionally end-to-end behind the existing API surface; CLI flags (--lite/--backend) and rebuild-image come in M11-S4. Deferred: M11-S1+S2+S3 commit disentanglement (todo #65), spec patch for seccomp=builtin (todo #66), cpus precision normalization at HTTP boundary (todo #67), bind-mount plumbing for Shared workspaces (M11-S4/S5).
decisions: M11-S3 Phase 3A network plumbing: SessionRuntime::create signature is fixed (trait surface from M11-S1) and BackendSpecific::Container doesn't carry network info; 3A handoff forbade touching sandboxd/src/main.rs (3D scope). Solution landed: ContainerRuntime::register_session(session_id, ContainerNetwork) public method storing per-session network in Mutex<HashMap>. Phase 3D and integration tests call register_session before create(). Trait stays unchanged; Lima keeps ignoring container fields. Pattern reused by 3B if image-pull metadata needs threading.

M11-S3 Phase 3C: GET /backends endpoint landed via SUB-ROUTER pattern (sandboxd/src/backends_http.rs) instead of inline-in-main.rs the handoff suggested. Justified by existing precedent (events_http.rs, policy_http.rs) and test scaffold reuse — sub-router enables tower::ServiceExt::oneshot HTTP-level integration tests against minimal sub-state without booting Lima/gateway/network/CA-manager. Wire shape unchanged: [{kind: 'lima', capabilities: {...}}]. BackendInfo struct in sandbox-core/src/backend/mod.rs serializes correctly 'for free' from existing serde tags on BackendKind (rename_all=lowercase) and Capabilities. Sort key: BackendKind::as_str() (the persisted lowercase tag) for stable wire order. Net file budget: +1 over handoff (backends_http.rs).

M11-S3 Phase 3D landed. Container backend wired end-to-end: ContainerRuntime registered alongside Lima with resource defaults computed once at startup (host_ram_mb*0.8 floored, host_cpus*0.8 to 1 decimal); runtime_for(state, kind) helper replaces 13 lima_dyn() call sites; POST /sessions container path runs daemon-side SessionSpec::validate, calls ensure_image() before runtime.create(), surfaces Built{warning} via SessionDto.warnings:Vec<String>, calls register_session(session_id, ContainerNetwork) before create. SessionDto.warnings + SessionDto.backend additive with #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = Vec::is_empty)] / #[serde(default)]. Gates 1+3+4 green locally (1037 unit, clippy clean, fmt clean); gate 2 (integration profile) running in background. Non-obvious: hardened=true on container backend rejected pre-projection (Container variant has no hardened field so validate() can't see it; explicit pre-check returns UnsupportedFeature::Hardening). cpus impedance: wire stays u32 (BackendSpecific::Container.cpus); daemon-internal default is f64 to preserve spec 1-decimal precision for Docker --cpus.

M11-S3 review fixes landed cleanly: (1) MAJOR uid degradation fixed via pub map_container_uid_gid(uid,gid)->(u32,u32) helper in sandbox-core/src/backend/container.rs (uid 0→1000:1000 spec floor; uid 1000→1000:1000; uid≠0&&≠1000→passthrough) wired at main.rs:5070; +1 unit test pinning all four spec branches. (2) MINOR workspace_modes mismatch fixed by tightening capabilities_for_container() to EnumSet::empty() with WHY comment that bind-mount plumbing deferred to M11-S4/S5; +1 integration test integration_create_session_container_rejects_shared_workspace asserting UnsupportedFeature::WorkspaceMode(Shared,Container). (3) NIT WARN log demoted to debug! with corrected message about route-helper integration scheduled for M11-S4/S5. All 4 gates green: 1038 unit + 43 integration tests, clippy + fmt clean. Skipping second kio-reviewer pass — fixes were mechanical.
discoveries: M11-S3 scoping: SessionRuntime trait at sandbox-core/src/backend/mod.rs:210 (kind/capabilities/create/start/stop/delete/status/ip/guest_transport/exec_interactive). AppState.runtimes is Arc<HashMap<BackendKind,Arc<dyn SessionRuntime>>> at sandboxd/src/main.rs:269; lima_dyn() helper at line 374 fetches Lima today. Capabilities struct in sandbox-core/src/backend/capabilities.rs:80. V005 migration adds backend column with DEFAULT lima — safe for rollback. Note: Explore claimed M11-S1 'already committed' but it remains uncommitted in working tree (per todo #65); confirm before assuming git state.

M11-S3 daemon flow: POST /sessions handler at sandboxd/src/main.rs:468. Order: parse/validate (474-505) → store.create_session (508) → CA generate (517-534) → network.create_network (536-557) → runtime create+start (Lima today, lines 564-834; ContainerRuntime slots here) → guest.ping agent-ready (837-852) → state Running (855) → setup_session_networking gateway (859-908) → policy apply (910-951) → repo clone (954-1054) → boot cmd (1056-1150) → SessionDto response (1153-1178). NO warnings field on response today — net-new plumbing for the spec-mandated first-use warning.

M11-S3 Docker integration: subprocess-only via std::process::Command::new('docker') in sandbox-core/src/gateway.rs (lines 274 rm, 483 run, 619 stop, 642 force-rm). NO bollard dep. Health checks parse 'docker inspect --format {{.State.Health.Status}}' (gateway.rs:99-115 DockerHealth enum). ContainerRuntime should follow same subprocess pattern. Route-helper invocation: docker inspect -f '{{.State.Pid}}' → sandbox-route-helper <pid> <gateway_ip> → check exit → agent probe. Helper consumes users.conf itself (no extra read needed by ContainerRuntime). Image build: no precedent — net-new ensure_image() in sandbox-core/src/backend/container.rs; Dockerfile location sandboxd/images/lite/Dockerfile (dir is net-new).

M11-S3 Phase 3A spec bug: docker --security-opt seccomp=default is INVALID syntax (Docker reads value as filename and exits 'no such file or directory'). Correct spelling for Docker's default seccomp profile is seccomp=builtin. Spec § Hardening line 542 needs correction. Implementer landed seccomp=builtin and documented the discrepancy in container.rs module docstring. Filed as deferred todo to track the spec patch.

M11-S3 Phase 3A transport choice: spec § container transport says 'docker exec <ctr> socat - TCP:127.0.0.1:5123' — agent binds loopback-only inside container, so the handoff's 'TCP via bridge IP' alternative would not have reached it. ContainerTransport implements docker-exec-socat with kill_on_drop child reaping; ContainerTransportStream wraps stdin/stdout via AsyncRead/AsyncWrite. Type impedance: BackendSpecific::Container has cpus:u32 but spec § Resource defaults wants 1-decimal precision (0.8, 1.5); 3A mitigated with as f64 in resource_ceilings, 3D should normalize at HTTP boundary. Docker stderr variance: 'docker inspect' emits 'error: no such object' (lowercase) while 'docker rm/stop' emits 'Error response from daemon: No such container' (mixed case); status()/delete()/stop() handle both shapes.

M11-S3 Phase 3B delivered cleanly: ensure_image() tag scheme sandboxd-lite:<daemon-version> (env CARGO_PKG_VERSION), container_image_lock as static Mutex<()>, EnsureImageOutcome enum (AlreadyPresent | Built{warning}), LITE_DOCKERFILE via include_str! from sandboxd/images/lite/Dockerfile (verbatim from spec). 3 new integration tests (first-use warning, second-call skips, concurrent serialize) — integration profile 35→38. The 'futex_wait hang' the implementer chased was transient first-run Docker latency (apt-get + ubuntu:22.04 base layer fetch), NOT a deadlock. Future Docker-integration tests on fresh VMs will need the same warm-up patience or a pre-pull step.

M11-S3 reviewer review-fix triage pattern: when kio-reviewer NEEDS CHANGES findings are mechanical (helper extract + capability narrow + log demote), skip second-pass review. Verification = (a) trust subagent's reported gate counts plus (b) ground-truth grep that each fix landed in the expected files. For non-mechanical or large-blast-radius findings, run a second reviewer pass. Reviewer verdict (Blockers vs NEEDS CHANGES) sets the floor; the re-review bar is judgment, not protocol.

log.title: --lite CLI surface, capabilities validation, UX
log.summary: M11-S4 closed. Phase 4D test_lite.py: 8 passed + 2 skipped (todo #68 workspace bind to M11-S5; todo #70 gateway container wiring to M11-S5) in 128.23s. Deliverables: tests/e2e/helpers/__init__.py + host_resources.py + lite_harness.py + test_lite.py. In-flight bug fix: plan_ssh_command -it → conditional -i/-it based on caller's TTY (2 new unit tests pin non-TTY path). Cargo: 1104 unit + 56 integration green, clippy/fmt clean. New deferral todo #70 captures gap #6 gateway integration that Phase 4D-pre's smoke missed. Branch m11-s4 tip remains uncommitted on master per orchestrator policy; next steps: M11-S5 will pick up todos #68 + #70 + parametrise existing Lima E2E tests for both backends. Across all four phases (4A/4B/4C/4D), M11-S4 delivered: --lite + --backend flags with five-tier precedence; CliConfig JSON loader; BackendsCache with one-GET-per-invoke; CLI-side SessionSpec validation; --no-cache rejection for container; BACKEND list column; describe -v capabilities matrix; isolation warning; rebuild-image --backend lima|container|all + --no-cache; docs/guides/lite-mode.md; resolved_cpus/resolved_memory_mb on the wire (gap caveat for cosmetic CPUs:0/Memory:0 display tracked as todo #69); 9 of 10 lite-mode E2E tests with helpers.
decisions: M11-S4 Phase 4A landed cleanly: --backend/--lite flags + BackendKindArg ValueEnum at sandbox-cli/src/main.rs; resolve_backend five-tier resolver in sandbox-cli/src/backend.rs (--lite > --backend > SANDBOX_DEFAULT_BACKEND > config default_backend > Lima); CliConfig JSON loader with #[serde(default)] forward-compat (missing→defaults, malformed→hard error with path); BackendsCache in sandbox-cli/src/backends_cache.rs (one GET /backends per invocation, lazy, in-memory only, 5s timeout, BackendsCacheError variants); cli_xdg::resolve_sandboxd_config_dir shared XDG resolver replacing the duplicate path-builder in presets/user.rs (one-resolver-per-XDG-path rule satisfied); render_feature_mismatch byte-exact spec rendering with backticks around flag names and 3-space help indent; render_no_cache_rejection_for_container three-line spec error pointing at sandbox rebuild-image --backend container --no-cache; dispatch_create_preflight integrates load-config → resolve → no-cache reject (exit 2) → cache → SessionSpec::validate (exit 2 on mismatch) → POST /sessions with backend stamped on body. Crate restructured to lib+bin layout (sandbox-cli/src/lib.rs added) so binary integration tests can drive helpers in-process. Test counts: 1062 unit/binary + 47 integration = 1109 total (+24 unit, +4 integration over Phase 4A baseline of 1038/43); clippy clean; fmt clean.

M11-S4 Phase 4B landed cleanly: BACKEND column added between STATE and AGENT in display_sessions_table (main.rs:873) — refactored to write_sessions_table(&mut dyn Write, &[SessionDto]) for unit testability; describe -v flag added on Describe variant (main.rs:215) with verbose: bool; CapabilitiesLookup enum (main.rs:1171) wraps Available(Capabilities)/Unavailable(String) so cache failures don't fail describe; render_capabilities_block (main.rs:1310) renders 11 spec-listed capability fields with stable enum serialization; handle_describe (main.rs:1805) + fetch_capabilities_for_backends async helper (main.rs:1838) plumb the cache through; isolation warning render_isolation_warning(BackendKind) emits the spec's two-line stderr text byte-exact (em dash U+2014, 6-space indent, lite: prefix) only for Container, integrated into dispatch_create_preflight before SessionSpec::validate. New tests: integration_isolation_warning.rs (subprocess tests pinning stderr.starts_with against spec bytes for both --lite and --backend container) and integration_describe_verbose.rs (default view shows Backend without Capabilities block; -v Lima/Container variants render the matrix). Spec/CLI nomenclature deviation: spec says 'inspect' for the human view but CLI has separate inspect (JSON automation) and describe (multi-block human) — Phase 4B landed functionality on describe to preserve JSON automation contract; documented in handoff. Test counts: 1075 unit (+13 over Phase 4A's 1062) + 53 integration (+6 over 47); clippy clean; fmt clean.

M11-S4 Phase 4C landed cleanly: Command::RebuildImage shape extended to { backend: RebuildImageBackend (Lima/Container/All), no_cache: bool } at sandbox-cli/src/main.rs; dispatch_rebuild_image + run_rebuild_image_dispatch<F> generic over send-closure for hermetic unit tests; best-effort dispatch (Lima→Container stable order) with per-backend error prefix 'rebuild-image[<kind>]:'; non-zero exit if any failed. Daemon-side RebuildImageRequest at sandboxd/sandboxd/src/main.rs:4178 with #[serde(default)] + custom Default(Lima, no_cache=false); handler reads Bytes, empty body→default for backwards-compat with older CLIs, malformed JSON→400 InvalidArgument; both backends dispatched via tokio::task::spawn_blocking. New rebuild_lite_image(daemon_version, no_cache) helper at sandbox-core/src/backend/container.rs:1222 reuses container_image_lock() — concurrent rebuild --backend lima and --backend container run in parallel since locks are independent. Container path explicitly does NOT take state.base_image_lock. Lima --no-cache satisfied by existing rebuild_base_image delete-then-build (no new flag plumbing needed). docs/guides/lite-mode.md authored: 8 sections (what lite mode is, isolation trade-off, what this breaks, what is preserved, resource defaults, UID alignment, prerequisites, when to choose); sidebar wired between Hardening and Integrate-an-agent in site/astro.config.mjs; cross-links from quickstart/sessions/installation. Out-of-scope-but-required: fixed two pre-existing broken anchors (#fail-closed-propagation-for-level-3 → #synchronous-dns-policy-gating) in policy-model.md and network-policies.md to satisfy starlight-links-validator. Test counts: 1090 unit (+15 over Phase 4B's 1075) + 56 integration (+3 new integration_lite_image_rebuild_*); clippy clean; fmt clean; make docs-build clean.

Phase 4D test_lite.py landed: 8 passed + 2 skipped (3.9 todo #68 workspace bind; 3.8 todo #70 NEW gateway wiring). End-to-end run: 128.23s. Three deliverables landed: tests/e2e/helpers/__init__.py + host_resources.py + lite_harness.py + test_lite.py. One in-flight regression caught + fixed: plan_ssh_command was passing -it unconditionally for container backend, breaking every non-TTY caller (pytest, CI) with 'cannot attach stdin to a TTY-enabled container' — added stdin_is_tty parameter, 2 new unit tests pin the non-TTY path drops -t and keeps -i. Cargo gates: 1104 unit (+2 from new TTY tests) + 56 integration tests passing, clippy/fmt clean. NEW DEFERRAL: gap #6 gateway container wiring deferred to M11-S5 (todo #70). The Phase 4D-pre subagent's gap #6 smoke verified sandbox health/exec but did not exercise gateway integration; daemon's container path at sandboxd/main.rs:884-909 explicitly short-circuits before setup_session_networking. Test 3.8 mirrors test 3.9 skip pattern with todo #70 cross-reference. Pre-existing handoff timeouts on tests 3.1/3.2 raised from 120 to 300s to absorb session-scoped _ensure_base_image fixture cost (~112s for Lima base build dominates the first test in a single-file run). Existing E2E unaffected: test_m1_vm_lifecycle.py collects cleanly with helpers package present. Helpers importable as documented.
discoveries: M11-S4 CLI surface mapped: monolithic sandboxd/sandbox-cli/src/main.rs (3200+ lines, all subcommands inline). Key wire-up sites: Cli/Command enum at line 25-239; Create args 41-95 (--no-hardening at 90, --no-cache at 93); build_request body at 465-545 (no_hardening→hardened:false at 495, no_cache→true at 503); send_request_with_timeout HTTP client at 829-888 over hyper+UnixStream (default 600s timeout); display_sessions_table at 769-793 with columns ID|NAME|STATE|AGENT|GATEWAY|CREATED; handle_inspect at 1572-1589 returns serde_json::to_string_pretty (NOT human-readable); render_describe_one at 1090-1159 is the multi-block human view; RebuildImage at 239 currently has no flags. XDG path resolver already implemented at sandbox-cli/src/presets/user.rs:398-421 (resolve_base_dir for ~/.config/sandboxd/presets/) — must share for ~/.config/sandboxd/config.json default_backend per spec 'one resolver, not two'. CLI already imports sandbox-core via use statement at main.rs:12-15; will add BackendKind, Capabilities, BackendInfo, SessionSpec. Tests under sandbox-cli/tests/: preset_cli.rs and events_binary.rs use 'fake axum daemon on temp UDS + real binary via CARGO_BIN_EXE_sandbox' pattern — Phase 4A tests follow same shape. Hand-rolled eprintln!() errors throughout, no anyhow/miette unified type.

M11-S4 spec/CLI nomenclature mismatch: spec § CLI & UX talks about 'sandbox inspect' getting backend prominence + capability matrix on -v, but CLI today has TWO commands: 'inspect' (JSON dump via serde_json::to_string_pretty at main.rs:1572-1589) and 'describe' (multi-block human readable at 1090-1159). The spec's 'inspect' description (default + -v with capability matrix) most closely matches 'describe' shape, not the current JSON-dumping 'inspect'. Phase 4B must either: (a) add backend+capabilities to describe and leave inspect as JSON, (b) merge inspect+describe and adopt -v, or (c) add backend+caps to BOTH. Likely (a) since existing UX has describe as the human path and inspect as automation. Phase 4B handoff must call this out before implementation. ALSO: exit code 2 is already used by 'sandbox policy status --wait' at main.rs:1432 for 'polled once, not yet propagated' — spec wants exit 2 for misuse; should be safe (different code paths) but watch for conflicts.

M11-S4 Phase 4D HANDOFF — surfaced six pre-existing implementation gaps from M11-S2/M11-S3 that block 8 of 10 planned lite-mode E2E tests. Gap #1 (HARD BLOCKER): daemon constructs ContainerRuntime::new with literal DEFAULT_LITE_IMAGE_TAG ('sandboxd-lite:latest') at sandboxd/src/main.rs:5147, but ensure_image builds 'sandboxd-lite:<CARGO_PKG_VERSION>' (currently 0.1.0); the very first sandbox create --lite fails 'Unable to find image sandboxd-lite:latest locally'. Source comments at container.rs:107-109 + 222-223 explicitly flagged this as 'Phase 3B will replace the literal' but Phase 3B never closed the daemon-side wire-up. One-line fix at main.rs:5147. Gap #2: handle_ssh in sandbox-cli/src/main.rs:1900-1956 hardcoded to 'limactl shell' with no backend dispatch — for container sessions it tries Lima VM that doesn't exist. ContainerRuntime::exec_interactive (container.rs:610) wraps docker exec -it but has no caller. Gap #3: GuestConnector at sandbox-core/src/guest.rs:206-251 holds Arc<LimaManager> directly and shells via 'limactl shell sandbox-X -- socat'; container-side ContainerTransport (container.rs:691) has no integration. Comment at main.rs:5124-5126 confirms 'GuestTransport rewire scheduled for M11-S2' — did not happen. Gap #4: resource defaults doubly unreachable; clap default_value_t = 2/4096 always populates wire (cli main.rs:50-54, 526-527), daemon req.cpus.unwrap_or(2)/memory.unwrap_or(4096) at sandboxd main.rs:534-535 falls back to Lima defaults not container's compute_default_resource_limits, host-80% path at container.rs:307-328 is dead code in production. Gap #5: workspace_modes empty() + workspace_host_path: None hardcoded — known deferred via M11-S3 review fix #2 to M11-S4/S5. Gap #6: route_helper_path: None hardcoded; gateway parity for lite unverified end-to-end. Path forward: bundle gaps #1-#4 into single 'Phase 4D-pre' handoff (operational wiring); defer gap #5 to M11-S5 per existing review-fix decision (test 3.9 will skip with that reason); verify gap #6 empirically post-fix. Then resume Phase 4D for 9 of 10 tests.

M11-S4 Phase 4D-pre Tasks 1-4 landed cleanly (gates: 1099 unit + 56 integration tests passing, clippy/fmt clean). Ground-truth verified: lite_image_tag_for_version helper at sandbox-core/src/backend/container.rs:125 used by ensure_image (1097), rebuild_lite_image (1235), and ContainerRuntime::new in sandboxd/main.rs:5182 with daemon_lite_image_tag_matches_ensure_image_for_same_version invariant test pinning the wire (gap #1); plan_ssh_command at sandbox-cli/main.rs:1937 with 4 unit tests for Lima/Container × cmd/no-cmd permutations (gap #2); GuestConnector rewired at guest.rs:218 to {Arc<HashMap<BackendKind, Arc<dyn SessionRuntime>>>, Arc<SessionStore>} with 4 dispatch tests using StubRuntime/StubTransport over tokio::io::duplex (gap #3); cpus/memory flipped to Option<u32> at sandbox-cli/main.rs:55,62 with daemon-side backend-aware unwrap_or at sandboxd/main.rs:565-568 (Lima=2/4096, Container=0 sentinel that resource_ceilings treats as host-80%) (gap #4). However: gap #6 smoke surfaced new HARD blocker — lite image (FROM ubuntu:22.04, glibc 2.35) cannot run sandbox-guest binary built on Ubuntu 24.04 host (glibc 2.39); container exits within 20s of docker start with 'GLIBC_2.39 not found'. This was masked because integration_lite_image_build* only verifies build, never starts; integration_container_runtime_* uses alpine sleep image not the lite image. Phase 4D's test_lite.py would have been the first thing to actually run sandbox-guest in the lite container. Three options: (1) bump Dockerfile to ubuntu:24.04 (one-liner + spec amendment, all listed packages available in 24.04); (2) multi-stage builder on 22.04; (3) static-musl sandbox-guest. Choosing option 1 — lowest-risk, eliminates host/image glibc skew permanently, spec's 22.04 was unmotivated by anything load-bearing. Phase 4D-pre2 task created.

M11-S4 Phase 4D-pre2 landed cleanly. Dockerfile bumped to ubuntu:24.04 with userdel ubuntu mitigation for uid 1000 collision (24.04 ships a default ubuntu user at uid 1000 colliding with spec-pinned agent uid 1000); same edits applied in spec Dockerfile block + prose at line 277 (now '24.04 (noble), same userland as the Lima VM image (also noble)'). docs/internal/milestones/M11.md updated. Verified ground truth: sandboxd/images/lite/Dockerfile lines 9 (FROM ubuntu:24.04) and 17 (RUN userdel --remove ubuntu || true && useradd ... agent). All four cargo gates remain green: 1099 unit + 56 integration tests passing, clippy/fmt clean. Full smoke verified end-to-end: sandbox create --lite test-smoke → State Running, sandbox health → VM running + Agent connected, sandbox exec test-smoke -- echo hello → 'hello', sandbox rm → Session removed. Gap #6 closed; Phase 4D test_lite.py is fully unblocked. Subagent surfaced two minor things to track: (a) CPUs:0/Memory:0 cosmetic display from Phase 4D-pre Task 4 sentinel choice — explicit caveat carried forward; (b) handoff used 'sandbox destroy' but actual subcommand is 'sandbox rm' — minor templating nit.

log.title: E2E parametrization, orphan cleanup, CI policy
log.summary: M11-S5 delivered all four sub-phases: 5A lite container completion (gap #68 workspace bind + gap #70 gateway wiring + cap-aware route_helper resolver + apply_policy(Initial) on container branch + restore_session_networking_lite + inverted stale guard test); 5B orphan cleanup (sandbox-core/src/backend/orphan_reaper.rs with DockerOps trait, 19 hermetic tests + 1 integration_orphan_reaper, hooked at startup); 5C E2E parametrization (94 tests categorized: 7 agnostic files × 2 backends = 62 invocations, 1 mixed file with 3 in-body skips, 3 Lima-only docstring-documented, test_lite.py preserved container-only); 5D CI policy split (Makefile test-e2e-container 48 tests / test-e2e-matrix 94 tests / test-e2e back-compat alias; .github/workflows/ci.yml jobs cargo + e2e-container + e2e-matrix gated on [self-hosted, kvm]). Final state: cargo 1137 unit + 57 integration, clippy/fmt clean, test_lite.py 9 passed / 1 skipped (3.9 rootless docker per spec § Out of scope) / 0 failed. Two follow-ups deferred: #73 KVM runner provisioning, #74 nightly perf benchmarks (blocked on #73).
decisions: Phase 5A fixer: cap-aware route_helper resolver landed. Replaced bare-name PathBuf::from('sandbox-route-helper') with layered resolver: default_route_helper_candidates (sibling-of-current_exe, /usr/local/bin/sandbox-route-helper, then $PATH dirs) + resolve_route_helper_path_from<I,F:is_usable> + has_required_caps -> read_cap_xattr (libc::getxattr on security.capability) -> xattr_has_cap_sys_admin_effective (pure decoder for vfs_cap_data V2/V3, effective flag, cap-bit 21). 13 new unit tests covering candidates builder, priority walk, decoder accept/deny variants. libc=0.2 added. Error message names CAP_SYS_ADMIN+ep + setcap command explicitly so operator gets actionable error rather than EPERM at runtime.

Stale integration test inverted: integration_create_session_container_rejects_shared_workspace -> integration_create_session_container_advertises_shared_workspace_capability. Now positive: workspace_modes contains exactly Shared, not Clone; Shared spec validates ok; Clone spec still rejected with WorkspaceMode(Clone, Container). Documents current contract instead of removed pre-Phase-5A guard.

Phase 5A complete. Final state: cargo 1118 unit + 56 integration, clippy/fmt clean; test_lite.py 9 passed / 1 skipped / 0 failed in 191.89s. test 3.9 (workspace_uid_alignment) gated with @pytest.mark.skipif(is_rootless_docker()) per spec § 'Out of scope' line 1175 + § Workspace lines 572-574 (which forbid userns-remap workarounds). New helper tests/e2e/helpers/docker_info.py exposes is_rootless_docker(). Docs: M11.md Phase 3 gate annotated; deliberately did NOT touch docs/start/installation.md (which refers to gateway-container rootless support, a separate concern). Total Phase 5A scope landed: gap #68 workspace bind, gap #70 gateway wiring, cap-aware route_helper resolver (libc::getxattr+vfs_cap_data decoder), inverted stale integration test, plus two follow-on fixes (apply_policy(Initial) for lite create-session at main.rs:1357-1395 + restore_session_networking_lite for lite stop+start at main.rs:2213-2226+4525-4640) that were necessary mechanical follow-ons to make 3.8/3.10 actually pass.

Phase 5B (orphan cleanup) complete. New module sandbox-core/src/backend/orphan_reaper.rs with: pure parsers (parse_container/home_volume/network_session_id, all leveraging SessionId's strict 12-hex constraint to naturally reject gateway containers, operator-foreign names without a hard-coded skiplist), partition_orphans helper, DockerOps trait + CliDockerOps impl, reap_orphans async fn returning ReaperReport. Hooked into sandboxd/src/main.rs immediately after reconcile() and before reconcile_networking. 19 hermetic unit tests (parsers + partition + best-effort error tolerance) + 1 integration_orphan_reaper_removes_orphans_and_preserves_live_resources covering both reap-and-preserve contracts in one race-free pass. NEW nextest test-group docker-sandbox-namespace (max-threads=1) serializes orphan-reaper tests with integration_container_runtime_* — both families create sandbox-{12hex} resources and would otherwise race against the reaper. Cargo: 1137 unit (+19), 57 integration (+1), clippy/fmt clean. Manual smoke confirmed: pre-planted sandbox-feedface0001 container+volume+net is reaped on first start (containers_reaped=1 volumes_reaped=1 networks_reaped=1), second start is idempotent (0/0/0). Reused lima::parse_session_id_from_name for the container-name parser (single source of truth — lite container handle and Lima VM name share sandbox-{session_id} convention). Discovery: docker --filter name= is substring-match not prefix-anchored, but the strict 12-hex parse downstream filters false positives — reaper occasionally enumerates extra names and discards them. Inlined run_docker_raw in the reaper module rather than making container::run_docker pub(crate) — sibling-concern architecture per spec.

Phase 5C (E2E parametrization) complete. 94 tests categorized across 12 files: 7 agnostic files parametrized (31 fns × 2 = 62 invocations), 1 mixed (test_m3_networking.py — 7 fns parametrized but 3 skip in-body for container due to hardcoded Lima 10.209.x.x/28 subnet pool; agnostic refactor via 'sandbox info' query deferred), 3 Lima-only files (test_m1_vm_lifecycle, test_m6_hardening, test_m85_golden_image — module docstrings updated to explain), 1 container-only (test_lite.py preserved). conftest.py adds @pytest.fixture(params=['lima','container']) backend fixture; helpers updated to honor backend (passes --lite when container). Verified canonical: test_guest_agent_health_check[lima] PASSED in 164.10s, [container] PASSED in 127.46s; test_guest_agent_exec[container] PASSED in 122.77s. test_lite.py baseline preserved (9 passed, 1 skipped in 201.48s). cargo gates unchanged (1137 unit + 57 integration). Discovery: pytest.skipif's string condition evaluates at collection time WITHOUT fixture access — module-level pytestmark with backend reference doesn't work. Pattern used: 'Lima-only files do not take the backend fixture' (mirrors test_lite.py container-only shape) for clean separation. Full matrix (60+ container-side invocations × 3-7min) reserved for CI per spec § 'CI policy'.

Phase 5D (CI policy split) complete. Three workflow jobs: cargo (rename of build-and-test), e2e-container (every PR + push-to-main, ubuntu-latest, runs make test-e2e-container), e2e-matrix (push-to-main only, runs-on [self-hosted, kvm], runs make test-e2e-matrix). Makefile: test-e2e-container selects 48 tests (38 [container] + 10 test_lite.py, 0 [lima]); test-e2e-matrix selects full 94 tests; test-e2e kept as back-compat alias. Handoff followed strictly: NOT silently degraded matrix to container-only — wired to label that doesn't yet exist + filed runner-provisioning handoff. Nightly perf deferred until runner exists. Cargo 1137 unit/57 integration unchanged.
discoveries: M11-S5 Phase 5A scope mapped via Explore: gap #70 gateway wiring is mostly a copy of Lima's setup_session_networking flow (sandboxd/main.rs:3466-3589, 9 steps: register session w/ event bus, bind VM IP, publish gateway_booting, GatewayManager::create_gateway, publish gateway_ready, spawn ingestor, persist NetworkInfo, start DNS-gate listener, attach VM/inject CA) into the container branch at main.rs:884-909 which currently short-circuits with early return at 909. Key findings: (a) GatewayManager::create_gateway is already backend-agnostic — takes NetworkInfo with docker_network_name + gateway_ip; (b) container DNS is already pointed at gateway via --dns at container.rs:441; (c) route_helper_path is hardcoded None at main.rs:843 — must be populated for nft route helper to fire; (d) the agent doesn't initiate outbound before ready so timing window for gateway-up-before-agent-ready is safe. Container path also already attaches container to sandbox-net-<id> bridge (container.rs:430-488). Estimated scope ~150-200 lines, mostly copy from Lima branch with adapted variable names. Gap #68 workspace bind is ~50-100 lines: (1) container.rs:374 capability EnumSet::with(WorkspaceMode::Shared), (2) main.rs:842 thread workspace_host_path from request, (3) docker create's --mount path already wired at container.rs:415-420/471-474. Bundling both gaps into Phase 5A — same function to edit, single test_lite.py run can verify both unskipped tests.

Phase 5A landed: gap #68 workspace bind + gap #70 gateway wiring. Cargo gates green: 1105 unit (+1 new test register_session_round_trips_workspace_host_path), clippy clean (after target/debug/incremental flush — same ICE pattern as 4D-pre2), fmt clean. Key implementation choices: (1) compute_initial_dns_policy(&req) -> String helper extracted, used by both Lima and container branches (one of two intended call sites — restoration path at main.rs:3947 deliberately untouched because it sources from state.session_policies, not req); (2) cleanup_lite_gateway_and_return! is a *local* macro inside container branch closing over session_id/state/backend_kind, parallel to existing cleanup_and_return! and cleanup_net_ca_and_return!; (3) route_helper_path uses bare binary name PathBuf::from('sandbox-route-helper') relying on Command::new() PATH lookup rather than introducing a new daemon-config field — daemon config did not expose one and bare-name approach matches existing helper invocations. Lima step 9 (VM bridge attach + CA injection) intentionally NOT replicated — Lima-only. NOT YET VERIFIED: e2e test_lite.py and integration profile — subagent's signal sent before those background runs completed. Verification deferred to next subagent dispatch.

Cap-aware fix unmasked two pre-existing M11-S5 lite-path gaps that were hidden behind the EPERM-from-setns failure: (1) test_lite_gateway_parity (3.8) failed at allow-path probe — DNS gate logged 'no active policy for session' because the container path returns at main.rs:1370 BEFORE the apply_policy block at ~1651. Lima path applies req.policy at create time; lite path skipped this entirely. Fixed by inserting the apply_policy(Initial) block right after step 8 (state->Running) in the container branch — uses cleanup_lite_gateway_and_return! on failure to mirror the Lima fail_explicit_policy_apply teardown shape. (2) test_lite_home_volume_lifecycle_beta failed on stop+start because start_session calls restore_session_networking unconditionally, which invokes attach_vm_to_bridge -> guest.exec('sudo', ...) — but the lite image has no sudo (and no VM bridge). Fixed by branching start_session on session.backend: BackendKind::Container -> new restore_session_networking_lite (gateway recreate + ingestor + DNS gate listener + reapply policy; skips attach_vm_to_bridge + inject_ca_into_vm), BackendKind::Lima -> existing restore_session_networking. Both fixes verified by individual test runs (gateway_parity passes, home_volume passes).

test_lite_workspace_uid_alignment (3.9) fails on this VM because docker is rootless (docker info: 'Context: rootless', SecurityOptions includes rootless). Under rootless docker, --user 1000:1000 in the container runs as host uid (subuid_base+999) per /etc/subuid mapping (olek:100000:65536). So workspace bind-mount writes hit the host as uid ~100999, not host uid 1000 — assertion st_uid==os.getuid() cannot hold without spec violation. Spec § 'Out of scope' line 1175 explicitly excludes rootless docker. Spec § Workspace lines 572-574 forbids userns-remap workarounds. Test was designed for default-hardened (rootful) docker. Two valid paths forward: (a) add rootless detection + uid:gid remap to map_container_uid_gid (substantive scope expansion beyond original handoff), (b) gate the test with @pytest.mark.skipif(rootless) (small, matches spec out-of-scope statement, but changes the test contract from '10 passed 0 skipped'). Going with (b) is out-of-scope from the original handoff (which only asked for route-helper fix + integration test inversion). Both fixes I already landed (apply_policy + restore_lite) were also out of original scope but were necessary mechanical follow-ons to make the route-helper fix testable. The rootless decision is a design call the user should make.

GitHub-hosted ubuntu-latest runners do NOT expose /dev/kvm, so Lima parametrization cannot run on stock runners. tests/e2e/conftest.py:226-236 already skips on missing /dev/kvm — meaning a green test-e2e-matrix on a stock runner would silently skip ALL Lima tests and still report green, defeating the policy. The runs-on:[self-hosted,kvm] label-gate keeps the gap loud: until the runner exists the job queues unscheduled rather than passing-with-zero-Lima-coverage. docs/guides/integrate-agent.md:156 — project already accepts the same KVM/self-hosted constraint upstream-of-CI.

log.title: Spec-delivery verification (lite-mode container backend)
log.summary: M11-S6 spec-delivery verification: closed with verdict DELIVERED. Built 238-claim verification map at .tasks/specs/2026-04-22-lite-mode-container-backend-design/2026-04-22-lite-mode-container-backend-design-delivery.md (1009 lines). Initial verification gate FAILED at make test-e2e-container 18/44; triaged into 3 defect classes (A: policy propagation, B: daemon restart, C: 3 fixture gaps). Fix subagent root-caused all classes plus discovered Class D (per-session MITM CA bind-mount — real M11 spec gap that escaped Phase 5A's DNS-deny-only verification). Class A real cause was bind9-host package shipping only 'host' not 'nslookup' (one-line Dockerfile fix); Class B was Lima-only startup reconcile() force-flipping container sessions to Error (added BackendKind::Container early-skip); Class C was 3 fixture gaps (skipif Clone unsupported, skipif shared_mount Lima-only, conditional ping for CAP_NET_RAW-dropped container); Class D shipped via ContainerNetwork.ca_host_path field + build_ca_mount_args helper rendering bind-mount + 4 env-var injections (CURL_CA_BUNDLE, SSL_CERT_FILE, REQUESTS_CA_BUNDLE, NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS). Final cargo: 1138 unit (+1 new CA-mount test) / 57 integration / clippy / fmt clean. Final e2e: 42 passed / 6 skipped / 0 failed in 741s (skips: 1 rootless-Docker, 3 Lima-pin, 1 Clone-unsupported, 1 Lima-only path). Closeout subagent landed two commits per Path B-hybrid: 31caddd 'feat(container): lite-mode session backend (M11)' (75 files +16967/-732) bundling Phases 5A-5D + Class A/B/C/D fixes + M11.md milestone narrative + closeout note, and 273bda7 'docs(verify): M11 spec-delivery verification map' (1 file +1009). Two M10-S10 link-fix files left intentionally unstaged per 'no non-M11 changes' constraint. Phase 5C verification gap also confirmed: M11-S5 Phase 5C only ran ONE canonical test ([container] guest_agent_health_check) end-to-end before declaring done; the full backend-agnostic suite was run for the first time in M11-S6 and exposed 18 failures. Lesson logged: 'one canonical test verification' is insufficient for parametrization shape changes.
decisions: Class D (NEW, beyond original triage) — L3 MITM CA trust delivered. Surfaced after A/B/C unmasked 6 surviving failures all with curl: (60) SSL certificate problem: unable to get local issuer certificate. Per-session sandbox CA from CaManager::generate_session_ca was never trusted inside container; Lima's update-ca-certificates path is incompatible with spec-mandated --read-only rootfs. Fix landed: ContainerNetwork.ca_host_path: Option<PathBuf> field; pure helper build_ca_mount_args renders --mount type=bind,src=<host>,dst=/etc/ssl/certs/sandbox-ca.pem,readonly + 4 --env vars (CURL_CA_BUNDLE, SSL_CERT_FILE, REQUESTS_CA_BUNDLE, NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS) at SANDBOX_CA_CONTAINER_PATH. Daemon wires ca_host_path: Some(<base_dir>/sessions/<id>/ca/cert.pem) at main.rs:1185 (CA already on host disk from generate_session_ca, pure plumbing). New unit test container_runtime_create_includes_ca_mount_when_path_set pins arg vector, register/lookup round-trip, and empty-vec contract when ca_host_path=None. This was a real M11 spec gap that escaped Phase 5A (test_lite_gateway_parity passed because it exercised DNS-deny path, not real TLS interception). Final state: cargo 1138 unit (+1)/57 integration/clippy/fmt clean; e2e [container] 42 passed/6 skipped/0 failed in 741s. Suggested commit split: fix(container) for A/B/C + feat(container) for D + new test.
discoveries: M11-S6 verification gate FAIL: make test-e2e-container 18/44 failed. Cargo gates all pass, test_lite.py 9/9. Three defect classes in parametrized [container] tests (M11-S5 phase 5C added them; only one canonical test was run end-to-end then). Class A: gateway-config policy propagation never advances for container sessions, ~10 tests fail with 'propagated=false actual=- age=Ns' over 60s — likely missing watcher subscription in ContainerSessionRuntime or actual=<gen> not surfaced back to SessionStore. Class B: daemon-restart leaves container session in Error state, 3 tests — orphan-cleanup reconcile (Phase 5B) handles cleanup but not re-attach to live containers; restore_session_networking_lite (Phase 5A) may miss policy-watcher resubscription. Class C: 3 mechanical fixture gaps — C1 nslookup not in lite image (add bind9-host to Dockerfile), C2 test_clone_repo[container] needs skipif (Clone unsupported per Capabilities), C3 sandbox cp target not visible inside container in test_shared_mount. Triage at .tasks/handoffs/20260427-080000-implementer-m11-s6-e2e-container-defects.md. Verification map at delivery.md is updated with NOT delivered verdict but NOT committed (waiting for fix). Discovery validates Phase 5C verification was insufficient — only one canonical test run.

M11-S6 e2e gate fix delivered. Original triage hypothesis for Class A WRONG: not a missing gateway-config-sync watcher — root cause was bind9-host package only ships 'host', not 'nslookup'. test_m10_s5_presets._warm_dns() and inline nslookup calls silently no-oped, so daemon's DNS-propagation cache never advanced. One-line fix: bind9-host -> bind9-dnsutils in sandboxd/images/lite/Dockerfile. Class B real bug: startup reconcile() at sandboxd/src/main.rs:5004-5070 was Lima-only (looked up sessions against lima_runtime.manager().list_vms() and force-flipped any session not in that list to Error). Container sessions universally Error after restart. Fix: early-skip 'if session.backend == BackendKind::Container { continue; }' + skipped_container counter on info! summary; container reconcile is independently handled by reconcile_networking/restore_session_networking_lite. Class C: 3 fixture gaps as expected (C2 cp invisibility was actually Lima-only assertion path; container binds /workspace per spec, lite-specific bind contract gated by test_lite_workspace_uid_alignment).

M11-S6 closeout committed cleanly. Path B-hybrid: single bundled M11 implementation commit (31caddd, 75 files +16967/-732, 'feat(container): lite-mode session backend (M11)') + separate verification map commit (273bda7, 1 file +1009, 'docs(verify): M11 spec-delivery verification map'). Closeout subagent bundled M11.md closeout-note into commit 1 (deviation from plan, justified per M10-S7 precedent — milestone narrative belongs with implementation, not with verdict map). Two M10-S10 link-fix files (docs/concepts/policy-model.md, docs/guides/network-policies.md) intentionally left unstaged per handoff constraint 'DO NOT bundle non-M11 changes' — confirmed via git log --grep='DNS-policy gating' that they are leftover M10-S10 follow-ons unrelated to M11. Final cargo regression check: build clean / 1138 unit (+1 new CA-mount test) / 57 integration / clippy / fmt all clean. Verdict in delivery.md: 'Delivered. All claims delivered. Gate green.'

log.title: Residual quality polish + e2e skip triage
log.summary:
M11-S7 'Residual quality polish + e2e skip triage' — closed clean.

Final e2e gate (make test-e2e-container): 47 passed, 2 skipped, 0 failed in 13:45.
Both skips are spec-aligned rootless-Docker contracts: test_lite_workspace_uid_alignment (pre-existing) and test_shared_mount[container] (added this session).

Delivered (organized by wave):

* Wave 1 — small isolated fixes:
  - route-helper polish (todos #61 NetworkManager error enrichment, #62 RUST_LOG warn, #63 i32::try_from for pidfd_open, #64 comment cleanup) -> 6822a0d
  - spec text corrections (seccomp=builtin, /home/agent/workspace/) -> 6822a0d
  - test_m10_s4 DNS skip → hard failure -> 7d39bb0
  - test_concurrent_sessions RAM precondition scoped to Lima-only -> 15e78c2
  - test_lite_orphan_cleanup_on_daemon_restart (todo #71, SIGKILL+sessions.db DELETE synthesizer) -> 169c7ea

* Wave 2 — larger Rust changes:
  - Clone-on-container (capabilities matrix advertises {Shared, Clone}; docker exec git clone reusing fail_explicit_repo_clone envelope from Lima) + unified workspace bind target /home/agent/workspace/ on container -> 5fadccf
  - Backend-neutral SessionDto.network/mounts plumbing via 'sandbox inspect'; SessionConfigDto.cpus: f32 with cpus_decimal sibling for forward/rollback compat -> 651a635
  - cpus 1-decimal precision + 'Some(0.0) → None' sentinel normalization in describe -> 6dd5808
  - --boot-cmd symmetry on container -> 13d5dbe
  - Bundle Y test refactor: 4 networking tests now read gateway_ip from 'sandbox inspect' instead of in-VM ip-addr regex (test_gateway_traffic_flow, test_stop_start_with_networking, test_concurrent_sessions, test_gateway_crash_recovery) -> 651a635 + 929726f

* Fold-in polish (post-discovery, per 'resolve small todos inline' feedback): todos #76, #78, #79, #80 -> e06b2da

* TCP-probe replacement for ping (post-e2e-gate diagnosis):
  - _probe_gateway_tcp helper using bash builtin </dev/tcp/IP/53> wrapped in 'timeout 5' (no extra package needed; works under cap-drop=ALL because TCP connect doesn't require CAP_NET_RAW)
  - 4 ping call sites in test_m3_networking.py swapped
  - test_concurrent_sessions cross-session negative-isolation step gated to Lima-only with full architectural rationale (gateway DNAT prerouting at sandbox-core/src/gateway.rs:1462-1486 makes cross-session L4 isolation invisible from inside container sessions)
  -> 929726f

* test_shared_mount[container] rootless-Docker skipif (in-body conditional pytest.skip; mirrors test_lite.py:495-507 pattern); spec-aligned exit story: M11-S8 will move this to daemon-side refusal -> 929726f

* Two kio-reviewer passes:
  - 1st (mid-session): 6576430 fix(m11-s7): kio-reviewer findings on the S7 diff
  - 2nd (post-TCP-probe): fd63787 (4 reviewer follow-ups: 2 delivery-doc line-citation drifts + 'Docker bridges' wording NIT + helper shell-layering NIT) + 1be18c5 (docstring parity follow-up surfaced by the fix agent)

* Delivery doc (.tasks/specs/.../delivery.md): augmented with new claim rows LM4.37, LM6.20-23, LM8.30-31 in cec6808; existing-row augmentation for the TCP-probe coverage in 929726f; line-citation drift fixes in fd63787. Summary table grand total 246/231/15/1/0 (231 a-shipped, 15 b-out-of-scope, 1 c-tracked-todo, 0 blockers).

Todo accounting: 60 completed, 18 dropped, 4 deferred (#29 UDP IP_ORIGDSTADDR conntrack lookup pre-M10, #60 nix pidfd wrapped form, #81 Lima fractional --cpus warn/reject, #82 gateway nftables-integration cross-session L4 isolation coverage — newly added this session).

Architectural discovery worth carrying forward (already in current_log + delivery doc): gateway DNAT prerouting in sandbox-core/src/gateway.rs:1462-1486 rewrites every TCP/UDP packet from a session's vm_subnet to that same session's gateway-IP sinks (CoreDNS, Envoy, deny-logger). The 'ip saddr {vm_subnet}' selector is per-session, but rules match the session's OWN saddr — so any outbound TCP/UDP from session A targeting session B's gateway gets answered by A's own gateway. Cross-session L4 isolation is invisible from inside the session; only ICMP (untouched by prerouting) lets a guest observe the actual L3 routing outcome. Container-backend cross-session isolation is best tested at the gateway nftables-integration level; tracked as todo #82.

Net: 9 new commits since M11-S6 close; cargo build/clippy/fmt clean throughout; integration_users_conf_startup green; both kio-reviewer passes APPROVE.
decisions: M11-S7 ping/cap-drop conflict resolved via path #2 (TCP probe), not path #1 (skip). Replaces ping <gateway_ip> with bash-builtin </dev/tcp/IP/53> reachability probe in test_m3_networking.py's three sites. Rationale: (a) preserves cross-backend reachability coverage that Bundle Y's network-info refactor was building toward, (b) aligns with directional pressure of S7 (DNS-skip→hard-fail, fewer skips not more), (c) modest scope — one helper + 3 call-site swaps. CoreDNS on port 53/TCP is always listening on the gateway so the probe is reliable. The cross-network negative-isolation check in test_concurrent_sessions becomes cleaner with TCP probe (clean fail) than with ping (hangs).
discoveries: Workspace bind target unified to /home/agent/workspace/ on container backend (M11-S7). Both backends now share the same in-session workspace path; capabilities matrix advertises {Shared, Clone} for both. Container Clone uses docker exec git clone reusing fail_explicit_repo_clone error envelope from Lima. The lite Dockerfile's tini/sandbox-guest entrypoint is what makes container side of state.guest.exec work — same TCP-over-SSH protocol as Lima.

Lima injects per-session MITM CA via guest agent into the system trust store (/usr/local/share/ca-certificates/sandbox-ca.crt + update-ca-certificates) — not via a bind mount. Container backend uses a read-only rootfs so it bind-mounts at /etc/ssl/certs/sandbox-ca.pem + env-var trust (CURL_CA_BUNDLE etc., per M11-S6 closeout). Asymmetry is structural: Lima can write trust dirs and rebuild the bundle; container cannot. SessionDto's mounts.ca_bundle_path is None for Lima as a result.

Lite container ping failures (3 of test_m3_networking.py [container] params) are NOT a missing-package gap — they are structural: spec § Hardening line 561-562 forbids ping under --cap-drop=ALL, iputils-ping on Ubuntu 24.04 needs cap_net_raw (inert under cap-drop=ALL), kernel ping_group_range fallback is 65534 65534 inside container, rootless Docker rejects --sysctl net.ipv4.ping_group_range at create time. ping in the e2e suite is a Lima-era L3-reachability proxy that does not survive the lite hardening posture.

Lite Dockerfile is shipped to the daemon via include_str! at sandbox-core/src/backend/container.rs:145, and the cached sandboxd-lite:0.1.0 image tag is keyed off CARGO_PKG_VERSION — bumping the Dockerfile alone does not bust the image cache. Forcing a rebuild requires: (a) cargo build --workspace AND (b) docker rmi sandboxd-lite:0.1.0.

Lite container backend's gateway nftables prerouting (sandbox-core/src/gateway.rs:1462-1486) DNATs every TCP/UDP packet from a session's vm_subnet to the SAME session's gateway IP — CoreDNS, Envoy, or deny-logger sink. The 'ip saddr {vm_subnet}' selector scopes per-session, but because rules match the session's OWN saddr, any outbound TCP/UDP from session A targeting session B's gateway IP gets answered by A's own gateway. Cross-session isolation is therefore invisible at L4 from inside the session — only ICMP (untouched by prerouting) lets a guest observe the actual L3 routing outcome. This is well-justified for production (every byte mediated) but limits what cross-session isolation tests can express via TCP. Container-backend cross-session isolation is best tested at the gateway nftables-integration level, not via e2e.
artifacts: 9 commits 6822a0d..1be18c5 on master; final e2e gate log /tmp/m11-s7-e2e-container-final.log (47P/2S/0F in 13:45); delivery doc verification map updated

log.title: Rootless-Docker enforcement at the daemon
log.summary:
M11-S8 ("Rootless-Docker enforcement at the daemon") — DONE.

**Scope delivered.** Daemon-side rootless-Docker probe (cached for daemon lifetime, container-backend only) + spec-shaped SandboxError::RootlessDockerRefused → HTTP 400 mapping + `--force-rootless-docker` CLI flag (container-only, per-create, programmatically rejected on `--backend lima` with exit 2) + `SessionDto.rootless` visibility field. Replaces the prose-only § Non-goals statement with code-enforced refusal at session-create time, keeping the deliberate-override escape hatch.

**Commits (6 logical + 1 reviewer fold-in).**
- b292527 — rootless-Docker probe + RootlessDockerRefused error + PATH-stub helper
- 193494d — --force-rootless-docker CLI flag + daemon enforcement + SessionDto.rootless field
- 5c11848 — 4 integration tests (refused, force flag overrides, force flag rejected on lima, default-hardened proceeds)
- 2b780c3 — drop polarity-inverted rootless-Docker skipifs from tests/e2e/test_lite.py + test_m5_workspace.py (now daemon-enforced)
- cf72ffb — spec amendment: § Non-goals 1175 → 1195 + new § Container specifics → Rootless Docker subsection (Hardening-table style)
- 2b5cb07 — delivery doc: LM12.11–LM12.16 cluster + LM12.2 (b)→(b)+(a) + summary table 246→252 + comprehensive line-citation drift fix
- d626160 — kio-reviewer fold-in: 4 MINORs (delivery summary table 11→10, CLI comment polarity, api/mod.rs comment polarity, docker_info.py docstring intent)

**Final gate state.**
- cargo build/clippy/fmt clean
- nextest: 1179/1179 default + 61/61 integration
- make docs-build clean
- make test-e2e-container intentionally NOT run on this rootless-Docker host (would produce 2 expected ERRORs at session-create — that IS the loud-failure signal the M11.md plan calls for); gate-run on default-hardened-Docker CI is the orchestrator's call

**Discoveries logged.** Spawned-binary daemon+CLI is the right harness pattern (vs in-process axum router) — duplicating main() startup choreography in test fixtures is wasted effort. CARGO_BIN_EXE_sandbox not visible cross-crate; resolve as sibling of CARGO_BIN_EXE_sandboxd. docker-sandbox-namespace nextest filter must be extended whenever new integration_* tests touch the sandbox-{id} namespace.

**Decisions logged.** Probe gate sits in container branch BEFORE artifacts (avoid leaving partial state). force_rootless_docker is per-create and not persisted to SessionConfig (deliberate override is for one session at a time, not a daemon-wide mode). New SessionDto.rootless substruct uses Option + #[serde(default)] for forward/backward compat.

**Deferred.** Todo #82 (cross-session L4 isolation untestable through DNAT prerouting) — architectural, separate from S8. Todo #83 (probe-failure integration test for NIT 3) — kio-reviewer fold-in cleanup, separate scope.

**Reviewer verdict.** kio-reviewer APPROVE-WITH-FOLLOWUPS resolved inline per "resolve small todos inline" feedback.
discoveries: M11-S8 W3 harness: spawned-binary daemon + spawned-binary CLI is the right pattern (vs in-process axum router). Reasoning: AppState construction needs LimaManager + NetworkManager + GatewayManager + event bus + live tasks — replicating that in test fixtures = duplicating main()'s startup choreography. integration_users_conf_startup.rs uses the same spawned-binary pattern. Cargo gotcha: CARGO_BIN_EXE_sandbox is NOT visible cross-crate from sandboxd/tests/; resolve sandbox CLI path as sibling of CARGO_BIN_EXE_sandboxd in target/<profile>/. The docker-sandbox-namespace nextest test-group filter in sandboxd/.config/nextest.toml MUST be extended whenever new integration_* tests touch the sandbox-{id} Docker namespace, otherwise concurrent runs collide and surface as flaky 500s — affected groups today: container-runtime, orphan-reaper, rootless-docker.

log.title: Route-helper auth hardening, dev-environment setup, test stability
log.summary: Landed three coupled threads: (1) route-helper auth hardening — install path moved to /usr/local/libexec/sandboxd/, SANDBOX_USERS_CONF env-var consultation gated behind test-env-override Cargo feature, defensive stat check on canonical users.conf (uid 0 + no group/world-write), dual-entry privilege boundary in sandbox-core/users_conf. (2) Daemon resolver collapsed to 2-step env+canonical lookup, both arms fail-closed. (3) TCP listen backlog pinned in sandbox-deny-logger so over-cap connections always reach accept(); flake on tcp_respects_concurrency_cap eliminated, conservation invariant deny+dropped_total==BURST is now a hard assertion. Plus: setup-dev-env Makefile orchestrator with 5 stamp-tracked idempotent sub-targets, contrib/users.conf.example template, /20-vs-/24 doc reconciliation, integration test #5 (probe-failure → HTTP 500), is_secure_mode pure helper extracted with 8-case matrix test. Verified GREEN: cargo build clean, 1185/1185 hermetic tests pass, clippy clean. Reviewer pass identified 1 Major (test coverage gap on world-writable arm) folded in; 4 follow-ups deferred as todos #87-#90. Landed as 5 commits: 5cb1167 sec(route-helper), 34cb4dc refactor(daemon), a698c5e fix(deny-logger), 6364f33 build(make), b1a8756 docs(m11-s9). Deferred M11-S10 (CIDR-scoped orphan reaper) is now the terminal session for the lite-mode container backend design spec.
decisions: Plan changed: Added M11-S10 (CIDR-scoped orphan reaper) — defense-in-depth filter for cross-daemon collision, deferred from M11-S9 to keep S9 cohesive around route-helper/dev-env theme. (total sessions: 78 → 79)

M11-S8 integration_rootless_docker.rs tests #2 and #4 set SANDBOX_ROUTE_HELPER_PATH=/usr/local/libexec/sandboxd-test/sandbox-route-helper on spawned daemon. Rationale: S9's privilege boundary makes production helper ignore SANDBOX_USERS_CONF, but those tests rely on tempfile users.conf via that env var. Pointing the daemon at the test-cap'd helper (which honors the env var) keeps tests independent of /etc/sandboxd/users.conf content while preserving production helper's security boundary.

Privilege-boundary code pattern for capability-bearing helpers: dual entry points in sandbox-core/users_conf — load_users_config (daemon, env-var honored) vs load_users_config_route_helper (helper, env-var only honored when test-env-override Cargo feature is on). Production builds of cap-bearing binaries unconditionally read the canonical config and ignore SANDBOX_USERS_CONF. Codifies 'if a process holds an extra privilege, it must not be redirectable by user-controlled state.' Use this same shape for any future capability-bearing helper or daemon component.

Dev-env Makefile pattern (setup-dev-env): stamp-tracked sub-targets under sandboxd/target/.dev-env-stamps/. Each sub-target does cmp + getcap pre-checks before invoking sudo so a no-op rerun issues zero sudo calls. install-route-helper-test-cap must use --workspace --tests --features sandbox-route-helper/test-env-override to match the binary nextest builds (checksum check). Future host-setup work follows this pattern.
discoveries: make install-route-helper-test-cap had to switch from --release --target-dir route-helper-test to --workspace --tests --features sandbox-route-helper/test-env-override writing to target/debug/. Reason: integration test's checksum check requires the installed binary bit-identical with CARGO_BIN_EXE_sandbox-route-helper, which nextest builds in debug+workspace+tests scope. Any narrower cargo invocation produces a structurally different binary and the test panics 'installed route helper is stale' immediately after a fresh install. Prod (release) and test (debug) binaries don't clobber each other because they live in different profile dirs under the same target/.

Subnet defaults split: NetworkManager in-code default is 10.209.0.0/24 (256 addresses); contrib/users.conf.example dev-install template is 10.209.0.0/20 (4096 addresses). Both work because users.conf overrides the in-code default. Documented in concepts/networking.md and M3.md so future readers reconcile correctly.

log.title: CIDR-scoped orphan reaper
log.summary: M11-S10 (CIDR-scoped orphan reaper) landed as 3 commits on top of b1a8756: 6d8f6dd feat(reaper) dual-anchor model, 34f3001 test(reaper) CIDR integration tests + nextest serial group, c9dac98 docs(m11-s10) LM14 cluster + concepts/networking.md dual-anchor enforcement promotion. This is the terminal session of M11 and closes the lite-mode-container-backend-design spec. Hard-gate verification (per session-planning skill spec-delivery rules): (a) claim-to-code verification map committed in c9dac98 (LM14.1-LM14.6 covering IPAM probe, filter point, transitive ownership, daemon-startup wiring, docstring extension, integration tests; cross-reference from LM9.x). (b) Conjunctive exit criteria: cargo nextest workspace GREEN (1200/1200 hermetic, was 1199 + 1 from new IPv6-only parser test); make test-integration GREEN; three new integration tests pass deterministically across 6 consecutive runs (integration_reaper_skips_out_of_pool_network, integration_reaper_reaps_in_pool_network, integration_reaper_skips_network_with_missing_ipam); legacy integration_orphan_reaper green under new pool param; cargo clippy clean; make docs-build GREEN with starlight-links-validator clean; reaper module docstring + concepts/networking.md prose match. KNOWN ENVIRONMENT GAP: make test-e2e-container could not run on this dev VM because rootless Docker is in use and post-M11-S8 daemon rejects lite-session creation on rootless docker by default; verified via git stash that pre-S10 master shows identical 47-fail outcome with the same daemon-side rejection — not an S10 regression. S10 changes touch only the orphan-reaper code path on daemon startup, which is upstream of any session-create call; no behavioral overlap with the rootless-docker rejection. Future host-setup must either run on default-hardened Docker or extend the e2e harness to flip --force-rootless-docker. kio-reviewer ran over the diff: 1 Major + 4 Minor + 2 Questions + 2 Nits. Major (in-pool integration test attached container to network being reaped) fixed inline by dropping --network from create_stopped_container helper, all calls now attach to bridge. Minor m-1 (saturating_add defensive intent) and m-4 (IPv6-only parser test) and Q-1 (transitive-ownership scope limitation docstring) fixed inline. Remaining 4 deferred as todos #91-#94: m-2 unique_session_id collision risk (parity with legacy), m-3 missing_ipam test name vs behavior, n-1 module-tag style consistency, n-2 Cidr4::parse #[doc(hidden)]. Decisions logged: two-pass reap_orphans with out_of_pool_sids skip set, transitive-ownership scope (single-anchor fallback when sandbox-net-{sid} torn down). Discoveries logged: Docker integration test convention (attach to bridge not custom network).
decisions: Orphan reaper dual-anchor design: two-pass reap_orphans (networks → containers/volumes) with out_of_pool_sids HashSet<SessionId> populated during network pass. Container and volume passes consult the skip set, achieving transitive ownership without coupling to docker-network-inspect calls. The CIDR anchor only protects siblings of out-of-pool networks observed during the network pass — a container/volume whose sandbox-net-{sid} has already been torn down falls through to single-anchor (name-prefix) protection. Acceptable given 12-hex sid randomness; revisit if multi-daemon deployment runbook lands. Documented in orphan_reaper.rs module docstring.
discoveries: Docker integration test convention: test fixtures should attach containers to bridge, NOT the test's custom sandbox-net-{sid} network. Some Docker engine versions (20.x+) register a created-state container as an active endpoint of its custom network, causing 'docker network rm' to fail with 'network has active endpoints'. The legacy integration_orphan_reaper.rs already followed this pattern; new integration_orphan_reaper_cidr.rs aligned via the create_stopped_container(name) helper. Apply this rule to any future Docker-backed integration test that exercises network teardown.

log.title: UDP audit + pre-DNAT deny-event fix
log.summary: Replaced IP_ORIGDSTADDR-only deny-logger UDP path with conntrack netlink lookup; UDP deny events now carry pre-DNAT 5-tuple. Hand-rolled netlink encoding via netlink-sys (published netlink-packet-netfilter v0.2.0 lacks conntrack). Phase 8 test 2 rewritten to assert pre-DNAT; new integration_udp_load_pre_dnat_attribution_holds_under_concurrent_flows exercises 12 concurrent flows. NLM_F_ACK pitfall locked in by comment + unit test (kernel ack-frame leaked into next pipelined recv). NLA_F_NET_BYTEORDER mask added per review (kernel-ABI hardening). Lookup miss → post-DNAT fallback + tracing::warn!. Init failure → hard-fail startup. Audit notes at docs/internal/m12-s1-udp-audit.md surface 3 S2-scope issues (dead allow-path, networking-design.md drifts) and 6 S9 candidates as deferred todos. Tests: 1206 hermetic + 66 integration all green; clippy/fmt clean. Commits: c3bc626 + 08c1d3e.
decisions: M12-S1 conntrack lookup uses hand-rolled netlink encoding via netlink-sys (not netlink-packet-netfilter): published v0.2.0 lacks conntrack support, only nflog. ~250 LOC stable kernel ABI emit/parse with full hermetic test coverage. Cargo.toml comment locks rationale + 'revisit when crates.io ships conntrack' marker.

On conntrack lookup miss (entry GC'd / race), deny-logger falls back to post-DNAT bind_addr + tracing::warn!, not drop. Losing deny attribution is strictly worse than emitting with less-accurate destination. CONNTRACK_LOOKUP_MISSES counter exposed via pub fn (not yet wired to /health). On netlink-socket init failure, deny-logger hard-fails startup so Docker HEALTHCHECK flips unhealthy + sandboxd restarts.

All review findings (1 MAJOR + 2 minors fold-inline) resolved before commit: NLA byte-order mask + comprehensive byte-order tolerance test, Phase 8 docstring rewritten, misleading miss-counter test claim corrected. Reviewer's m3-m6 deferred to M12-S9 as explicit todos.
discoveries: UDP allow-path is structurally dead, not just under-tested: nft DNATs policy_allow_udp to Envoy:10000, but gateway input chain rejects udp dport 10000 and Envoy has no UDP listener. M12-S2 'fully working UDP at transport' is bigger than planned — needs design decision (add Envoy UDP, reshape nft to skip Envoy for UDP, or remove dead path).

No conntrack/netlink crate in workspace today. M12-S1 implementation needs new dependency: rust-native (netlink-packet-netfilter / nfnetlink_conntrack-rs) vs C-linked (nfct via libnetfilter_conntrack). Decision pending implementation phase.

networking-design.md lives at repo root, not docs/internal/. Plan references and docs that point at docs/internal/networking-design.md are stale; future doc-reconciliation work should fix the path or move the file.

NLM_F_ACK on netfilter CT_GET corrupts pipelined lookups: kernel sends successful entry reply followed by separate NLMSG_ERROR(errno=0) ack frame which next lookup's recv() consumes. Fix: drop NLM_F_ACK; on miss kernel sends NLMSG_ERROR(-ENOENT) regardless. Single-flow tests miss this; load tests catch it. Locked in via comment + unit-test assertion.

log.title: Fully working UDP at transport level
log.summary: M12-S2 'Fully working UDP at transport level' complete. Restructured the UDP datapath end-to-end: (a) extracted sandbox-event-emitter shared lib crate; (b) renamed sandbox-deny-logger -> sandbox-nft-deny-logger, replaced conntrack-driven UDP attribution with kernel NFLOG (group 1) + drop, deleted src/conntrack.rs entirely; (c) added new sandbox-nft-allow-logger driven by NFCT NFNLGRP_CONNTRACK_NEW subscription, writing nft-allow.jsonl; (d) extended daemon ingest with strict layer/event pairing parser, DenyLoggerAllow envelope variant, allow_body_dto mapper, EventName::Allow filter; (e) gateway container ships dual binaries with launch-both-then-probe-both pattern (:10003 deny + :10004 allow health), dual-PID process monitor; (f) JSONL filenames flipped to spec-mandated nft-deny.jsonl + nft-allow.jsonl; (g) UDP allow-path skips Envoy entirely (TCP-only proxy); (h) reconciled 5 user-facing docs (networking.md, networking-design.md, network-policies.md, policy-model.md, troubleshooting.md) to silent-drop + five-process model. Test parity: 4 new e2e UDP allow-path tests in test_m4_policy.py (NTP allow, bidirectional echo NEW-only, multi-port-same-host, CIDR-edge), test_m3_networking.py UDP-skip removed and replaced with real deny-event assertion, synthetic-JSONL integration_nft_allow_logger_ingest.rs (2 tests). Verification: 1221 hermetic tests pass, integration profile clean, clippy clean, fmt clean, e2e Lima-backend 5/5 pass. Review verdict APPROVE with 0 blockers / 0 majors / 3 minor citation nits (folded in). Landed as 4 commits c1f069f..0336342 on master (not pushed; awaiting user authorization).
decisions: M12-S2 spec open questions resolved: (1) NFLOG group=1; (2) silent drop, no ICMP unreachable (reconcile networking-design.md:1221 to silent drop); (3) per-source rate cap deferred to S9; (4) delete src/conntrack.rs entirely after UDP-deny moves to NFLOG (TCP-deny uses SO_ORIGINAL_DST, no remaining caller); (5) /health field names preserved through rename, new fields added for allow-logger; (6) JSONL filenames nft-deny.jsonl + nft-allow.jsonl; (7) allow events fire on NFCT_T_NEW only, flow-end deferred.

EventEmitter::open signature changed: now takes layer: &str so each binary can set its own layer tag (deny-logger vs allow-logger). Line enum's layer field is now &'a str (borrow from emitter's owned String) instead of &'static str. JSONL on-disk shape preserved byte-for-byte because callers pass the original literal.

AllowRecord field set identical to DenyRecord (orig_dst_ip, orig_dst_port, protocol, src_ip, src_port). Same envelope, distinguished by event discriminator only ('allow' vs 'deny'). flow_id deferred (Resolution 7 NEW-only — no second event to correlate); forward-compat via Option<T> + #[serde(default)] when needed.

JSONL 'layer' value pinned at 'deny-logger' across the rename — daemon-side ingest parser keys on this string literal. Crate name and binary name both flipped to sandbox-nft-deny-logger but the wire-shape layer tag stays. Allow-logger will use a distinct layer value (TBD in Phase 3, likely 'allow-logger').

Daemon-side ingest layer-tag asymmetry: on-bus DTO layer always 'deny-logger' (single nft-logger family per Decision 5); discriminator is event tag (deny/allow/rate_limited). On-disk JSONL retains producer-specific layer tags ('deny-logger' vs 'allow-logger'); parser enforces layer/event pairing. nft_logger.rs (renamed from deny_logger.rs) handles both producers with parse_5tuple helper.

Allow-logger /health on port :10004 with nfct_socket and allow_events_emitted_60s fields. health::run is now polymorphic with body_builder (deny_logger_body / allow_logger_body); each binary plugs in its own shape. netlink-sys reused for NFCT subscription — netlink-packet-netfilter v0.2.0 doesn't cover conntrack subsystem.

Phase 4 launch pattern: launch-both-then-probe-both. Gateway entrypoint launches sandbox-nft-deny-logger + sandbox-nft-allow-logger in parallel after mitmproxy/Envoy/CoreDNS, then sequential readiness probes (:10003 deny -> :10004 allow). Process-monitor iterates DENY_LOGGER_PID + ALLOW_LOGGER_PID; either exiting takes container down per Decision 4.

Phase 6 doc reconciliation completed across five files (179+/25-): networking.md (five processes, TCP/UDP-split datapath), networking-design.md (UDP subsection rewritten + ICMP-unreachable mandate reconciled to silent drop), network-policies.md (UDP destinations section with NTP/UDP-123 example), policy-model.md (UDP-specific caveats H4 block), troubleshooting.md (UDP traffic section, 4 sub-entries).

Phase 5 e2e test pattern: bash /dev/udp/ for one-shot UDP sends (universal), socat for bidirectional (read-back required). All assertions hit the on-bus DTO (layer='deny-logger' on bus, event tag discriminates allow/deny) — agnostic to on-disk JSONL filename. RFC-5737 TEST-NET-3 (203.0.113.1) used for negative-tuple deny assertions to avoid collision with idle VM traffic. NTP (time.cloudflare.com:123) chosen as bidirectional probe — universally reachable, real protocol response to validate.

M12-S2 commit split landed as 4 commits instead of suggested 6: c1f069f feat(nft-loggers)! collapses lib-extract + deny rework + allow-logger + daemon ingest because file-level interleaving (events/ingest/{nft_logger,watcher,envelope,mod}.rs all carry both deny and allow content). Restructure authorized by spec/plan; resulting commits all compile mid-sequence. Followed by f301c36 gateway, 514fb3e e2e tests, 0336342 docs.
discoveries: NFLOG bind is a three-step handshake on NETLINK_NETFILTER: (1) PF_BIND cmd=3 [NOT 1, that's CMD_BIND], (2) per-group CMD_BIND cmd=1 [WITHOUT this kernel never routes packets to socket], (3) CFG_MODE COPY_PACKET. Locked in nft-deny-logger nflog.rs.
