NFLOG recv buffer can pack multiple NFULNL_MSG_PACKET frames in one recvmsg(); parser must drain all of them (parse_all loop), not return after first. Single-msg parse loses all but the first event in a burst — caught only by load-style integration test.

Allow-logger uses synthetic-JSONL integration test approach (no gateway image dep): tests/sandboxd/integration_nft_allow_logger_ingest.rs writes nft-allow.jsonl + asserts ingest dispatch. Mirrors existing events_ingest_integration.rs pattern. Real-binary live e2e covered in Phase 4+5.

deny-logger JSONL filename still 'deny-logger.jsonl' on disk after Phase 4 (deliberate). Spec Resolution 6 mandates 'nft-deny.jsonl'. Coordinated rename touches: networking/gateway/entrypoint.sh DENY_LOGGER_OUT default, sandbox-nft-deny-logger main.rs clap default, sandbox-core watcher.rs DENY_LOGGER_JSONL constant. Phase 6 docs already written for spec-mandated name.

SIGTERM doesn't promptly kill nft-loggers because spawn_blocking netlink-recv threads aren't woken by tokio cancel. Pre-existing behavior (matches the deny-logger before split). Entrypoint's 10s SIGKILL escalation handles cleanup. Cosmetic; flagged as latent S9 cleanup.

JSONL rename completed across 7 files (5 tracked + 2 untracked from earlier in session). Watcher.rs gained a strengthened negative test asserting legacy 'deny-logger.jsonl' is no longer in KNOWN_FILES — turns the rename into a tested invariant. 2 files (sandbox-nft-deny-logger/src/main.rs and integration_nft_allow_logger_ingest.rs) are untracked-but-modified — Phase 7 commit split must keep these atomic with the tracked rename so it doesn't get split across commits.

Gateway image must be rebuilt before integration tests when sandboxd loggers change; make test-integration does NOT auto-rebuild. Phase 5 first run failed 3/5 m10_s3_end_to_end tests with stale image (old sandbox-deny-logger expecting DNAT-to-:10002 datapath); make gateway-image then re-run flipped to 5/5 green. Worth either: hardening Makefile dep chain (gateway-image stamp triggers integration profile rebuild), or callout in CONTRIBUTING.

Forward-chain invariant changed in M12-S2: pre-S2 'every saddr-accept also has daddr' assumption no longer holds because gateway.rs admits VM-subnet UDP wholesale at FORWARD (PREROUTING NFLOG+drop already filtered denied UDP). test_denied_traffic relaxed to skip the wholesale UDP-accept rule when checking the daddr invariant for TCP-class lines. Live nft list ruleset prints L4 protocol numerically: UDP=17, TCP=6 — e2e regex assertions need numeric form.

Review M12-S2 verdict: APPROVE with 3 mechanical Minor findings (no Blockers, no Majors). All seven Resolutions and six Decisions from the spec faithfully implemented. Wire-protocol code (NFLOG + NFCT) well-disciplined: NLA flag-bit masking, multi-message recv drain, group=0 rejection at bind, NEW-only NFCT subscription. Strict layer/event pairing parser rejects all four cross-pairs. Doc set internally consistent. Full review in .tasks/handoffs/20260501-161400-kio-reviewer-m12-s2-review.md

log.title: ECHConfig stripping fix + policy-version doc clarification
log.summary: M12-S3 'ECHConfig stripping fix + policy-version doc clarification' complete. Two commits landed on master: (1) f61a3ee fix(coredns-plugin) — reverted M10-era blanket-deny of SVCB/HTTPS DNS queries to original strip-not-deny intent: CoreDNS forwards queries upstream and strips only the ECH SvcParam from the response, records and other SvcParams pass through. Empty-param records preserved. Old _Blocked tests renamed to _StripsECHParam, new _NonECHPassesThrough + _DeniedDomain coverage. networking-design.md, docs/concepts/networking.md, and 2026-04-25 sync-DNS-gating spec wording sharpened to specify param-level semantics. (2) b7400cf docs — one-paragraph clarification in policy-model.md and network-policies.md that 'version' is the schema discriminator (currently 2.0.0; minor 2.x.x accepted, major must match), not a per-policy revision counter; pointed readers at policy_hash on policy_propagated/policy_updated lifecycle events and expected_hash/propagated_hash on GET /sessions/{id}/policy/propagation-status. Verification: go test ./... -count=1 -race clean, 1221 hermetic Rust tests pass, clippy clean, fmt clean. Two follow-ups deferred: drop unused hasECHParam helper (S5 candidate), optional ECH e2e coverage (no-priority).
decisions: M12-S3 #98 ECHConfig fix landed at param-level (not record-level). Spec wording sharpened: networking-design.md:493/800 + docs/concepts/networking.md:205 now say 'strips the ECH SvcParam ... records and other SvcParams pass through' instead of the M10-era ambiguous 'strips records that carry ECHConfig'. The M10-S10 sync-dns-gating spec at lines 178-180/385/857 reconciled: 'AAAA denied at request time; SVCB/HTTPS forwarded with ECH SvcParam stripped from response; gate is A-only'. New helpers stripECHFromSection/stripECHFromRR/filterECHParams replace the old filterECHRecords. hasECHParam retained but currently unused in production (in-scope bullet asked for wiring, not deletion).
discoveries: Policy version field clarification (#99) landed: policy-model.md and network-policies.md gain one paragraph each clarifying 'version' is the schema discriminator (currently 2.0.0; minor 2.x.x accepted, major must match), not a per-policy revision counter. Per-policy identity surface: policy_hash on policy_propagated/policy_updated lifecycle events and expected_hash/propagated_hash on GET /sessions/{id}/policy/propagation-status (sandbox-core/api/mod.rs:79-89 + lifecycle.rs:94-145). +4 lines, no code touched.

Empty-param HTTPS records (only ECH param, nothing else) are kept in the response with an empty Value slice rather than dropped. Locked in by TestStripECH_HTTPSOnlyECHEndsWithEmptyParams. Rationale: dropping would re-introduce record-level removal semantics, which is exactly what S3 reverts. Operators should expect to see HTTPS RRs with empty SvcParams in cases where the upstream sent only an ECH-bearing record.

log.title: Daemon validation & gateway hardening + ubuntu preset
log.summary: M12-S4 daemon validation + ubuntu preset shipped via 5 commits (37d74e1..d383e62) on master. (1) 37d74e1 reject --no-cache on container caps + reject fractional --cpus on Lima at HTTP boundary (PerSessionNoCache UnsupportedFeature variant; SessionSpec.no_cache: Option<bool> threaded as per-invocation flag, NOT persisted). (2) 9581e7c hermetic cross-session L4 forward-chain isolation tests against pub generate_forward_allow_ruleset (3 tests: positive admit, absence-of-accept walk, per-session generator hygiene). (3) 01e0494 retargets the orphan M11-S1 TODO at lima.rs:298 to the container-runtime side-map pattern; LimaRuntime::ip migration filed as deferred todo #123. (4) 14984d5 LM2.10 wording fix — drops 'stateless over RuntimeHandle' phrasing. (5) d383e62 ubuntu preset (NTP UDP/123 to ntp.ubuntu.com+time.ubuntu.com Transport-level + apt HTTPS/443 to archive+security.ubuntu.com Tls-level; udp_transport_rule helper added; Lima-only e2e smoke). All four verification gates green: cargo build clean, 1229/1229 nextest pass, clippy clean, fmt clean. Local-only — no push per session-tracking constraint.
decisions: M12-S4 #82 test file named cross_session_l4_isolation_integration.rs (not integration_*-prefixed) per repo convention: integration_* prefix is reserved for tests needing Docker/Lima/nft CLIs (selected by integration profile); this test is hermetic (calls pub generate_forward_allow_ruleset and pattern-matches emitted nft text). 3 tests added: positive-admit shape; absence-of-accept walk (load-bearing); per-session generator hygiene.

M12-S4 ubuntu preset shipped: 4 rules — NTP UDP/123 to ntp.ubuntu.com + time.ubuntu.com (Transport level — only valid for UDP per PolicyCompiler::validate), apt HTTPS/443 to archive.ubuntu.com + security.ubuntu.com (Tls level). HTTP/80 omitted (Ubuntu 22.04+ uses HTTPS mirrors by default). snap/livepatch/changelogs omitted (no measurement available; preset can grow if real workloads need them). udp_transport_rule helper added next to existing http_rule/tls_rule. e2e variant delivered (test_ubuntu_preset_smoke, Lima-only — apt-update + UDP/123 allow event).

M12-S4 #95+#81 daemon validation landed: no_cache: Option<bool> field added to SessionSpec (per-invocation flag, NOT persisted to SessionConfig — passed to session_spec_from_config as a parameter), validate() returns UnsupportedFeature::PerSessionNoCache(caps.kind) on Some(true) against caps with per_session_no_cache=false. Lima fractional cpus rejected at HTTP daemon boundary (sandboxd/src/main.rs create_session) with 400 — hard-reject not soft-warn because SessionDto.warnings is for post-success operator notices, not malformed-sizing accept. Pattern mirrors existing rejects_hardened test. 5 tests added (3 unit + 2 integration).
discoveries: M11-S1 Phase 1C deferred work item (b) was missed at the close — LimaRuntime::ip() still shells out via 'limactl shell <vm> -- ip -4 addr show eth1' instead of reading from a per-session side map. ContainerRuntime (M11-S3 Phase 3A) implements the right pattern: sessions: Mutex<HashMap<SessionId, ContainerNetwork>> populated by register_session before SessionRuntime::create. M12-S4 #97 retargeted the TODO from 'M11-S1 Phase 1C' to a concrete pointer at the container-runtime side-map pattern; deferred-todo filed for the actual lima migration.

Forward-allow generator (gateway.rs:1601 generate_forward_allow_ruleset) is single-session by design — each session has its own gateway container with its own nft table state, so no multi-session compile surface exists. M12-S4 #82 cross-session L4 isolation is provable structurally against the single-session generator: compile A's ruleset, walk every accept rule, prove no accept admits a daddr outside A's own (subnet,gw_ip) — by disjoint-subnet construction, B's subnet/gateway/vm_ip are unreachable. Trailing reject catches the rest.

log.title: Reviewer-nit round-up + cleanup renames
log.summary: M12-S5 reviewer-nit round-up shipped via 9 commits (79fb3d2..c678329) on master. (1) 79fb3d2 inlines ROUTE_HELPER_BINARY_NAME, lifts SANDBOX_ROUTE_HELPER_PATH from shared Daemon::spawn into per-test extend_env closures (only tests #2 and #4 actually invoke the helper), adds 2 hermetic outer-wrapper tests for resolve_route_helper_path env-read (#86p1+p3, #87). (2) 2c5e94e changes sandbox-nft-deny-logger tcp::bind signature to u32 with saturating clamp to i32::MAX (the u32 signature IS the wrapper); adds drop(outcomes) drain-window comment (#86p2+p4). (3) 0f251db adds metadata-follows-symlinks docstring sentence + lib.rs intentional-asymmetry comment for helper-side users-conf functions (Option B per caller-graph analysis: only sandbox-route-helper uses them via explicit path) (#88, #90). (4) 6d183e9 marks Cidr4::parse #[doc(hidden)] (Option A; caller graph fully internal to sandbox-core) (#94). (5) 4e1092c mixes std::process::id() & 0xff into unique_session_id (PID tiebreaker > atomic counter for cross-process disambiguation) + comprehensive rename to integration_reaper_skips_resources_when_sibling_network_has_no_ipv4_ipam (#91, #92). (6) 9c7eda1 drops single (M11-S10) tag from orphan_reaper module-doc heading; remaining 8 inline references explicitly deferred to M12-S6 (#93). (7) 4f519ad drops unused hasECHParam helper from coredns-plugin/strip.go (#121). (8) 145e844 adds nix-rust/nix#2748 reference comment above libc::syscall fallback in sandbox-route-helper (#60 follow-up). (9) c678329 clarifies sudo timestamp_timeout behavior in installation.md One-shot setup section (#89). All 9 verification gates green: cargo build clean, 1231/1231 nextest pass (+2 vs M12-S4 baseline of 1229 due to #87's two new tests), clippy -D warnings clean, fmt clean, Go build/test/vet clean, and three grep gates zero hits (ROUTE_HELPER_BINARY_NAME, integration_reaper_skips_network_with_missing_ipam, hasECHParam). Two deferred follow-ups filed: #124 (validate_canonical_users_conf_security symlink_metadata hardening — deferred half of M11-S9 reviewer Nit #6) and #125 (51 pre-existing rustdoc warnings in sandbox-cli/src/main.rs surfaced during F's gate). Local-only — no push per session-tracking constraint.
decisions: M12-S5 batch C #90 lib.rs re-export asymmetry resolved as Option B (deliberate omission with rationale comment). Helper-side users-conf functions (load_users_config_route_helper, route_helper_users_conf_path) have a single caller — sandbox-route-helper/src/main.rs — that imports via the explicit sandbox_core::users_conf::... module path, not through the crate-root re-export. Re-exporting them at the root would broaden the API surface for zero benefit and dilute the privilege-asymmetry signal. Comment block above pub use users_conf::{...} now documents the asymmetry as intentional.

M12-S5 batch B #86 part 4 wrapper: changed sandbox-nft-deny-logger tcp::bind signature itself from i32 to u32 (saturating to i32::MAX before socket2 listen, which is c_int by external constraint) rather than introducing a parallel bind_u32 wrapper. Production caller is the only non-test consumer; test sites are trivial to update; parallel API would leave dead surface. The u32 signature IS the wrapper. Saturation chosen over try_into().expect(...) since the whole point was eliminating the .expect panic path — a u32 backlog is intentional behavior, not an error.

M12-S5 batch F #94 Cidr4::parse marked #[doc(hidden)] (Option A). Caller graph is fully internal: every call site lives in sandbox-core (users_conf.rs, backend/orphan_reaper.rs, integration_orphan_reaper{,_cidr}.rs). No external workspace crate calls parse; sandboxd consumes Cidr4 only as a value type. Function is pub rather than pub(crate) purely so integration-test binaries (external crates from rustc's POV) can import it — the textbook 'internal API' branch. Visibility unchanged so existing callers keep compiling; only the doc-surface signal changes. lib.rs Cidr4 re-export untouched (the value type stays public).

M12-S5 batch E #93 chose Option A (drop inline (M11-S10) tag from orphan_reaper.rs section heading) but scoped narrowly to the single tag the reviewer flagged. Eight other inline M11-S10 references in the same file are explicitly deferred to M12-S6's broader milestone-reference cleanup (todo #102) — expanding the scope here would conflict with M12-S6. #92 rename: integration_reaper_skips_resources_when_sibling_network_has_no_ipv4_ipam (sweep boundary stops at code/test/in-tree-doc; preserved in frozen historical records under docs/internal/milestones/M11.md, .tasks/specs, .tasks/progress.json, .tasks/history.json — those describe past state). #91 PID-byte tiebreaker (std::process::id() & 0xff) chosen over atomic counter — atomics are per-process and can't disambiguate two parallel CI processes both starting from 0.
discoveries: M12-S5 batch A #86 part 3 was an architectural reshape, not a per-test deletion: the SANDBOX_ROUTE_HELPER_PATH env-var setting lived in shared Daemon::spawn (integration_rootless_docker.rs), not in per-test bodies. Lifted to module-level TEST_ROUTE_HELPER_PATH constant + per-test extend_env closures for tests #2 and #4 only (which actually invoke the route helper). Tests #1 and #5 (which reject before setup_session_networking) now leave the env var unset. #87 outer-wrapper unit-test hermetic strategy: real has_required_caps reads file caps and runners can't setcap fixtures (no CAP_SETFCAP), so the env-set test asserts via error-message content using a non-existent /tmp/... path; the env-unset test tolerates both Ok(canonical) (dev hosts with route-helper installed) and Err(message naming canonical) (clean CI) — proves the env-read seam without requiring a specific host state.

log.title: Milestone-reference cleanup
log.summary: M12-S6 milestone-reference cleanup completed via 2 commits on master (c44077f, 5effe40). 668 milestone-tag occurrences (matching regex Mn-Sn or Mn-Sn Phase X) reduced to 2 deliberate-keep residuals across the production tree (sandboxd/, networking/, tests/e2e/, CLAUDE.md). Sweep covered 107 files spanning sandbox-core (api/backend/events/session/store/policy.rs/gateway.rs/lib.rs + integration tests), sandbox-event-emitter wire crate (event/health/lib/limits.rs + Cargo.toml), sandbox-nft-{deny,allow}-logger (incl. Cargo.toml dep rationale), sandboxd daemon (main.rs/lib.rs/events_http.rs/Cargo.toml + 7 integration test files), sandbox-route-helper, sandbox-cli (main.rs/backend.rs/presets/{builtin,mod,user,param,merge}.rs + tests/{preset_cli,events_binary,integration_no_cache_rejection}.rs + cargo_fetch_trace.json fixture), tests/e2e (8 .py files), networking/mitmproxy (4 .py files), networking/gateway (entrypoint.sh, healthcheck.sh), networking/coredns-plugin (6 .go files), and orphan_reaper.rs (the 8 inline M11-S10 references explicitly deferred from M12-S5). Substantive narrative preserved by rephrasing to describe behavior rather than history; symbol names and test FUNCTION names baking milestone in were kept verbatim (external references); test FILE names like m10_s2_end_to_end.rs and m10_s3_end_to_end.rs preserved with explicit doc-comments at the top. CLAUDE.md gains a 'No milestone tags in code or tests' bullet under 'Key conventions' (todo #102, M12.md exit criterion). Dispatched 6 parallel W-subagents (W1-W6) in first wave; concurrent git-stash incidents in W2/W6 silently destroyed ~250 sites of W1/W3/W4 work. Recovered by committing surviving W2/W5/W6 output as intermediate checkpoint c44077f, then re-dispatching 3 R-subagents (R1/R2/R3) with explicit no-stash prohibition. Final 11 strays cleaned inline. Verification: cargo build clean, 1231/1231 nextest pass, clippy -D warnings clean, fmt clean, Go build/test/vet clean, Python/shell syntax clean. Local-only — no push per session-tracking constraint.
decisions: M12-S6 commit shape: 2 commits on master (c44077f intermediate checkpoint + 5effe40 final), not the 'single PR with categories' the M12.md plan suggested. The intermediate checkpoint was forced by the stash-pop loss recovery — anchoring surviving W2/W5/W6 work before re-dispatching W1/W3/W4 needed a real commit. The two-commit shape is fine for review: c44077f covers sandbox-core/nft-loggers/event-emitter, 5effe40 covers daemon/cli/e2e/mitmproxy/coredns/gateway/orphan_reaper + CLAUDE.md convention bullet. Both messages enumerate categories in the body.
discoveries: M12-S6 hazard: parallel cleanup subagents that ran 'git stash / git stash pop' to verify pre-existing baselines silently destroyed each other's in-flight edits. The pop reads from a stash created with the entire dirty tree (including sibling agents' work) and then pops into a tree the siblings have moved on from — net effect is silent edit loss reported as 'Dropped'. ~250 sites of cleanup work were lost in the first M12-S6 wave from W1/W3/W4 and had to be re-swept (R1/R2/R3) after committing surviving work as an intermediate checkpoint c44077f. Lesson: when delegating parallel sweep agents touching the same workspace, prohibit 'git stash' explicitly and direct them to 'git show <commit>:<path>' for pre-existing-state checks.

log.title: Native cp via limactl cp / docker cp
log.summary: Refactored sandbox cp from in-tree base64-chunk byte pump to native dispatch: plan_cp_command(backend, session, direction, host, remote) returning (limactl cp -r ...) or (docker cp ...). Established the dispatch surface M12-S8 will extend with plan_sync_command. Side benefits: directory copy now works (was broken), attributes preserved (mode/sparse), error messages reach operator verbatim, container cp works against stopped sessions. Dropped base64 dep from sandbox-cli. 6 unit + 7 integration tests pin invocations on both backends; full hermetic suite 1237/1237 + clippy clean. Filed two deferred follow-ups: #126 (deprecate dead daemon /upload /download endpoints) and #127 (E2E test_cp_native_attributes for sparse + mode round-trip). Committed as f3fc855.
decisions: M12-S7 cp dispatch shape: parallel free-functions (plan_ssh_command + plan_cp_command), not a trait. Rationale: matches existing backend-keyed dispatch idiom in the CLI; trait object adds no polymorphism we use. M12-S8 sync lands a sibling plan_sync_command on the same surface.
discoveries: Pre-refactor sandbox cp had broken directory-upload (std::fs::read fails on dirs, despite docs/guides/workspaces.md claiming dirs were 'transferred as chunked base64'). Native dispatch fixes this transparently — limactl cp -r and docker cp recurse by default.

limactl cp -r is harmless on plain files (scp ignores -r for non-directories); docker cp recurses by default and rejects unknown flags. Hence asymmetric flag set in plan_cp_command: limactl gets unconditional -r, docker gets none.

log.title: rsync-like directory sync
log.summary: Shipped 'sandbox sync' as a separate command (chosen over a 'cp --rsync' flag for clearer semantics). plan_sync_command planner sibling of plan_cp_command returns (rsync, [-a, --delete, -e 'limactl shell' or 'docker exec -i', src, dst]). rsync is a hard requirement; provisioned into both base images (Lima cloud-init apt-get baseline + lite Dockerfile, with the apt step renamed to apt-baseline). 6 unit + 7 integration tests pin invocations on both backends; full hermetic 1243/1243 + clippy clean. E2E placeholders added but not run (todo #130). Filed deferred follow-ups #128 (rsync flag pass-through), #129 (sync-against-stopped session troubleshooting bullet), #130 (E2E execution). Committed as acaac2f.
decisions: M12-S8: ship 'sandbox sync' as a separate command (not 'sandbox cp --rsync'). Rationale: cp and sync carry distinct semantics (one-shot retransfer vs incremental mirror with --delete); folding both into cp puts two mental models behind one verb. Each command's contract is now obvious from its name.

M12-S8: rsync remote-shell uses 'limactl shell' on Lima and 'docker exec -i' on container — both as the -e arg to rsync. Rejected 'ssh -F ~/.lima/<vm>/ssh.config' because it's an internal Lima artefact. Rejected '-it' for docker exec because TTY corrupts rsync's binary wire protocol.

M12-S8: rsync required (not optional). Provisioned in both base images (Lima cloud-init apt-get baseline + lite Dockerfile). No runtime pre-flight probe — native error from rsync when missing is clearer than a Rust-emitted check. Establishes new contract for session images going forward.
discoveries: bash builtin echo silently consumes -e as 'interpret escapes' flag, corrupting argv recorders. The cp shim happened to never see -e; the sync shim cannot. Use 'printf %s\n' in shim recorders.

Lima base-image content hash automatically picks up generate_base_template() changes — adding a package to the apt baseline transparently triggers a rebuild on next sandbox create via existing staleness-detection. No extra plumbing needed.

log.title: Backlog buffer: surface and resolve any new M12 todos
log.summary: Drained M12 deferred backlog from 21 → 4. Resolved 10 todos this session (#106 docs reconcile, #110 #122 #127 E2E test additions, #112+#117+#119 nft-loggers parser counters + SIGTERM clean exit, #120 Makefile no-change disposition, #126 dead /upload /download endpoint removal, #128 sandbox sync trailing-args). Plus three fix-here items from earlier in S9: #124 (users.conf canonical-path symlink rejection), #125 (51 rustdoc warnings → 0), #129 (sync-against-stopped troubleshooting bullet). Dropped 7 todos as obsoleted by M12-S2's UDP datapath rework: #105 #111 (allow-path real and kernel-resident now), #109 #113 #114 #115 #116 (all targeted code in deleted conntrack.rs). Remaining 4 deferred all have clear 'something big' or execution-only reasons: #107 ICMP-unreachable spec/impl reconciliation, #108 per-source UDP rate limit feature, #123 LimaRuntime::ip side-map refactor, #130 E2E sync execution. 6 commits landed. Full hermetic suite 1232/1232; integration cp+sync 15/15; clippy clean; rustdoc clean. Clean hand-off to M12-S10 verification.
decisions: M12-S9 #125 fix policy: demote private-item doc links to backtick-quoted code text rather than promoting items to pub. The constraint was doc-only edits; items are private for sound reasons (module-internal helpers/validators).
discoveries: Rustdoc resolves a module's '//!' inner doc-attribute against the PARENT module's scope, not the module's own. self::/super:: workarounds don't resolve; only fully-qualified crate::... paths work — even for items defined in the same file. Discovered during the M12-S9 #125 sweep when fixing inner-doc links in container_rootless_probe.rs and test_support/mod.rs.

log.title: Claim-to-code verification (terminal session for M12)
log.summary: M12-S10 terminal verification gate PASSED. Built claim-to-code map at docs/internal/milestones/M12-delivery.md covering every concrete claim across S1–S9: each claim maps to (a) code+test locator, (b) Explicitly deferred bullet verified absent, or (c) tracked follow-on with defer reason. Verification: make test 1232/1232, make test-integration 85/85, clippy clean, rustdoc 0 warnings. Every deferred bullet verified absent from tree. 4 follow-ons retained with clear reasons (#107 #108 #123 #130). M12 status flipped to completed in session-plan.md. M12 — closing notes block appended to M12.md. make test-e2e remains the single pre-merge gap, tracked as #130 (~80 min wall-clock). Committed as 2b46173 (fmt) + ad5348c (verification + close).

log.title: M12 backlog cleanup + deferred sync E2E
log.summary: M12-S11 cleanup landed across 5 commits. Source tree (43539a3): route-helper rootful-Docker fix (CAP_NET_ADMIN ambient raise via two-step Permitted→Inheritable→Ambient promotion); test fixture passes --user uid:gid so setns(pidfd, NEWNET) clears ptrace_may_access; lite Dockerfile pre-creates /home/agent/workspace; E2E cp/sync paths moved off read-only /tmp; install paths and docs refreshed for cap_net_admin,cap_sys_admin=eip. Earlier: Runtime::ip() trait deletion (c1420bb), milestone-tag rename sweep (6c8b6b6), milestone-plan post-close-session entry (e03d208), end-to-end docs refresh (120d02a), CLAUDE.md crate/migrations/transport corrections (f4d3bcb), Lima cp -r and sync trail-slash fix (3e888c4), and time.ubuntu.com / SVCB→HTTPS preset and test fixes (9f07740). Verification: 1236/1236 hermetic, 87/87 integration, 3/3 route-helper integration, 60/60 container E2E from earlier in session. Full Lima E2E re-run was blocked by two pre-existing daemon issues (partial-Lima-dir cleanup #136 and silent base-image provision failure #137) — both filed as deferred todos with reproducers; both are out of S11 scope. Selected todos (#131, #133, #134, #135) all completed; #130 (sync E2E both backends) deferred as post-M12 long-running validation.
decisions: Dropped todos #107, #108, #123 after one-by-one review. #107: no spec/impl divergence (silent UDP drop is the design per networking-design.md:1121,1229); converted into GH issue Koriit/sandboxd#3 to revisit deny-ICMP posture for UX. #108: category error - RateCap is ingest-pipeline back-pressure, not per-source fairness, and there is no cross-tenant masking surface (one gateway = one VM). #123: trait method Runtime::ip() was structurally unused (daemon owns the IP via NetworkInfo); deleted trait method, both impls, test stub, and integration sanity-check. Workspace builds clean, 1230 hermetic tests pass, clippy clean.

M12-S11 #131 landed in 6c8b6b6: 13 milestone-tagged test files renamed via git mv, 12 reference sites updated, CLAUDE.md exemption removed. Rust m10_s2/s3_end_to_end.rs became event_ingestion_pipeline.rs / gateway_deny_pipeline.rs. .tasks/handoffs/ left as historical record. make test (1230 pass) and make test-integration (85 pass) clean.

M12-S11 #134 (docs/ refresh) landed in 120d02a: 14 files updated. Notable corrections: --memory-mb→--memory, --layer=allow-logger→--event=allow, gateway 'four-process'→'five-process' model, sandbox-deny-logger→sandbox-nft-deny-logger crate paths, lite-mode default cpus 4GB (not 2GB), HTTP API now documents /policy/propagation-status, /backends, source_presets, backend, force_rootless_docker, no_cache rejection.

Groups A+B fix landed in 3e888c4: 'fix(sandbox-cli): condition lima cp -r on dir source; auto-trail-slash on sync'. plan_cp_command and plan_sync_command grew a source_is_dir parameter; the call sites (handle_cp, handle_sync) stat the host-side source for Upload and pass false for Download. -r only emitted on Lima cp when host source is a directory (rsync-backed limactl cp 2.x ENOTDIR fix). Trailing slash auto-appended on directory-source sync upload (rsync contents-mirror idiom). Planners stay pure functions; hermetic + integration tests cover both branches. 1236 hermetic, 17/17 cp+sync integration green.

Route-helper rootful-Docker fix: chose ambient-cap raise (PR_CAP_AMBIENT_RAISE on CAP_NET_ADMIN before ip(8) exec) over direct rtnetlink. File caps now cap_net_admin,cap_sys_admin=eip. ~3 LoC + caps crate dep, vs 40+ transitive deps for rtnetlink-rs.
discoveries: CLAUDE.md was wrong on two structural facts before #133: (1) crate count was 4, actual is 8 (sandbox-core, sandboxd, sandbox-cli, sandbox-guest, sandbox-route-helper, sandbox-event-emitter, sandbox-nft-deny-logger, sandbox-nft-allow-logger). (2) Guest-agent transport described as 'TCP-over-SSH' but actual mechanism is socat over limactl shell / docker exec (no SSH). Refinery-embedded SQL migrations under sandbox-core/migrations/ are the real schema-evolution surface, not inline Rust in SessionStore. Fixed in f4d3bcb.

E2E full matrix run 52/66/1 in 65 min. Lima half: 38 pass / 8 fail. 6 of 8 are real M12 regressions: (A) sandbox cp Lima — limactl 2.1.1 internally uses rsync and -r flag breaks file copies (rsync change_dir ENOTDIR). plan_cp_command docstring assumed scp semantics that no longer hold. (B) sandbox sync — tests use no-trailing-slash on src dir, impl forwards verbatim to rsync, files land at synced/<basename>/ instead of synced/. Design call between impl auto-trail vs test fix. Other 2 Lima failures (ubuntu_preset_smoke nslookup NXDOMAIN, svcb empty response) need separate investigation. Container half and test_lite.py: by design — rootless Docker on this dev VM rejected by lite spec § Non-goals 1195.

After Docker reinstall to rootful (29.4.0, containerd snapshotter), integration_route_helper_denies_when_netns_ip_outside_caller_subnet fails with 'setns failed: EPERM' on Docker container netns. cap'd helper (cap_sys_admin+ep on /usr/local/libexec/sandboxd-test/sandbox-route-helper) cannot setns into root-owned container PIDs even though container userns == host init userns. setns to own pid works; setns to PID 1 (root-owned) also fails. Likely Ubuntu 24.04 unprivileged_userns AppArmor profile or Linux 6.8 hardening wrt cross-uid setns. Pre-existing environmental issue, NOT a code regression: last green test-integration on 6c8b6b6 was under rootless Docker. Other 84/85 integration tests pass (cp/sync dispatch all green after this session's planner fix).

Group C E2E failures were both real upstream issues, not gateway/code regressions. (1) Canonical removed time.ubuntu.com from authoritative DNS — apex returns SOA-only for this name across ns1.canonical.com/8.8.8.8/1.1.1.1; only ntp.ubuntu.com resolves. (2) cloudflare.com publishes HTTPS (TYPE65) at apex, not SVCB (TYPE64); test_svcb_record_without_ech_reaches_vm asked TYPE64, upstream returned NOERROR-zero-answers correctly. Both fixed in 9f07740: drop time.ubuntu.com from UBUNTU_NTP_HOSTS preset (was a 2-host list, now 1-host) and update SVCB test to query TYPE65. SVCB/HTTPS share wire format and stripECHFromRR handles both arms identically, so HTTPS exercises the same code path.

Container backend (--lite) is broken under rootful Docker 29.4.0 — sandbox create fails with 'sandbox-route-helper denied (exit 1): RTNETLINK answers: Operation not permitted' / 'ip route replace failed: exit 2'. Root cause: under rootless Docker the container's netns lives in a child user namespace, so cap_sys_admin in the parent userns implicitly grants cap_net_admin in the child (Linux userns rule); under rootful Docker the netns sits in the init userns directly, so the helper's spawned ip(8) process has no caps and cannot RTM_NEWROUTE. Adding cap_net_admin to the helper file caps doesn't fix it — file caps are dropped at exec, the spawned ip(8) needs ambient caps (PR_CAP_AMBIENT_RAISE) or the helper should issue the netlink op directly. NEW regression from the rootful Docker switch, not from S11 cleanup work. Out of S11 scope; needs follow-up to make container backend rootful-Docker compatible. Container-backend E2E entirely blocked on this; Lima-backend E2E unaffected.

Rootless Docker masked the route-helper bug via userns cap promotion: CAP_SYS_ADMIN in parent userns implicitly grants all caps in child userns when operating on objects in child userns, so a cap-less ip(8) child still succeeded at RTM_NEWROUTE. Rootful Docker has no such promotion.

PR_CAP_AMBIENT_RAISE under setcap requires the helper to first raise CAP_NET_ADMIN from its Permitted to its Inheritable set. File caps =eip put the cap in the FILE's inheritable set, but on exec a thread's Inheritable is taken from the parent's Inheritable (the unprivileged caller's, which is empty). The helper must call caps::raise(Inheritable, NET_ADMIN) before caps::raise(Ambient, NET_ADMIN).

Docker cp can't write to a --read-only container's tmpfs (/tmp) and can't read from it either; it only sees the rootfs overlay. /home/agent (mounted as a Docker volume) is visible to docker cp because volumes are not part of the rootfs. Test paths under /home/agent/ work on both backends; /tmp/ paths only work on Lima.

Route-helper test setns(pidfd, CLONE_NEWNET) under rootful Docker is gated by ptrace_may_access(PTRACE_MODE_READ_REALCREDS), which checks BOTH real uid AND real gid of the target task. Production passes --user 1000:1000; the test fixture passed only --user 1000, leaving gid at busybox image default (root/0), so gid mismatch denied setns. Fixed by passing uid:gid in test fixture.

Pre-existing daemon bug surfaced during full Lima E2E re-run: when 'limactl clone' fails after writing disk/cidata.iso but before lima.yaml, the partial Lima dir poisons every subsequent limactl invocation across all instances — every limactl op fatals on the missing lima.yaml. Daemon does not rm -rf the partial dir on clone failure. Manifests as cascading failure of every Lima E2E test downstream of the first failure. Manual fix: rm -rf the broken Lima dir. Out of S11 scope but worth a follow-up todo.

Lima base image build silently accepts a failed docker install: cc_scripts_per_boot allows Lima provision.system scripts to exit non-zero without aborting cloud-init, so the daemon's 'golden base image build complete' message fires even when docker-ce apt install failed mid-run. Each cloned session VM then re-runs provision.system on its own first boot, hits the same network flakiness, and fails again — this is the upstream cause of the Lima E2E cascade. Verified by inspecting /home/olek.guest/.lima/sandbox-base/serial.log: 'step=docker start' present, 'step=docker done' missing. Pre-existing, out of S11 scope.

log.title: Fix Lima daemon bugs (#136 partial-dir cleanup, #137 base-image provision validation) + run deferred sync E2E (#130)
log.summary: Resolved all three remaining M12-S12 deferred todos: (1) #136 partial-clone cleanup — added cleanup_partial_lima_instance with limactl-delete-then-rm-rf fallback wired into both clone_vm error arms; (2) #137 base provisioning validation — added validate_base_provisioning probing socat/git/rsync/docker via 'limactl shell command -v' between base VM start and golden-image stamping; (3) #130 sync E2E — ran test_sync_full_tree, test_sync_incremental_no_op, test_sync_delete_mirroring, test_sync_attribute_preservation against both lima and container backends, all 8 passed in ~12 min. Also hardened provision templates against apt EAGAIN flake (Acquire::Queue-Mode access + Pipeline-Depth 0 + retry loops around apt-baseline and curl|sh docker install). 1236 hermetic + 87 integration tests green, clippy clean, sandbox-base rebuilt and validated end-to-end. Committed as 092693d.
discoveries: Lima SLIRP networking surfaces apt EAGAIN ('Could not wait for server fd - select (11: Resource temporarily unavailable)') under concurrent fetches. Acquire::Queue-Mode 'access' alone is insufficient — Pipeline-Depth=0 (forces serial requests within each connection) is the load-bearing fix. With it, base-image apt-baseline succeeds first try in ~85s.

limactl clone is non-atomic: writes disk + cidata.iso BEFORE lima.yaml. Mid-clone interruption leaves a dir with no lima.yaml, which then poisons every subsequent limactl invocation host-wide ('open lima.yaml: no such file or directory'). Cleanup must try 'limactl delete --force' first then fall back to direct 'rm -rf $LIMA_HOME/<vm>' since limactl refuses to operate on the corrupted instance.

Cloud-init's cc_scripts_per_boot does NOT abort the boot when a per-boot script exits non-zero — it logs a warning and proceeds. So 'limactl start' returning success is necessary but NOT sufficient for declaring a base image golden. Must probe in-VM tools post-start (command -v socat git rsync docker) before stamping. Without the probe, downstream cloned VMs re-run the same flaky provisioning at session-create time.

log.title: SANDBOX_BASE_VM_NAME knob + drop conftest preflight sweep
log.summary: Isolated e2e from production daemon state via SANDBOX_BASE_VM_NAME (sub-section A) and SANDBOX_USERS_CONF dual-pool layout (B); deleted the conftest preflight sweep (C) safely under the new pool isolation; landed zero-skip e2e marker convention (D), docs sweep (E), and a defensive eth0 MTU clamp on Lima base/session templates (F) that closed the libslirp PMTU blackhole exposed when (A)'s rename forced fresh apt-update during base-image build. Folded in an end-to-end stability sweep once the suite was actually exercised: connect()-based daemon-readiness probe (b8a123c + 10909e4 covering the six inline respawn paths missed by b8a123c), graceful-then-force base VM stop (a02ac9e), nextest filter loosening for module-path-prefixed unit tests (fafcbb8), per-test byte-window daemon-log auto-dump (24f49ca + c62eaac), Makefile green ✓ (e655d38), and a manual-iterate-path doc note for the venv ABI trap (c84bfce, resolving the rediscovered #138). #140 (cargo always-rebuild on sandbox-guest + sandbox-route-helper) dropped as not reproducible — host fingerprint trace showed only up-to-date markers. Operator smokes #1-#3 (SANDBOX_BASE_VM_NAME validation, pool isolation while production live, make setup-users-conf upgrade idempotency) all passed. Full make test-e2e runs to completion under the cumulative fix set; bind→listen race and libslirp PMTU were the dominant flake sources. M12 closes with this session.
decisions: SANDBOX_BASE_VM_NAME is set on the e2e harness's own os.environ at conftest module-import time (via os.environ.setdefault), not just on the spawned daemon's env. This lets test_golden_image.py read it as a module-level constant before any session-scoped fixture runs, and keeps Popen(env=...) calls simple — children inherit the parent env. Future env-var-driven test config should follow the same pattern.
discoveries: S13's CIDR + base-VM-name isolation does NOT isolate ~/.lima/ itself: neither e2e conftest nor Rust integration tests set LIMA_HOME, so test and production daemons share the global ~/.lima/ directory. Test cleanup operates by name pattern (sandbox-*) over the shared store. Full ~/.lima/ isolation would require per-test LIMA_HOME=<tempdir> on the spawned daemon and integration tests — out of scope for S13.

S13 sub-section (F) — Lima base/session templates clamp eth0 (SLIRP NIC) MTU to 1280 via netplan dropin in a new step=net-tune provision block. Root-caused to libslirp not propagating host-learned PMTU into guest TCP state; surfaced because (A)'s base-VM rename forced a fresh apt-update during base-image build instead of reusing operator's pre-built image. Universal-safe IPv6 floor (RFC 8200 §5); same choice as Cloudflare WARP/Tailscale. Session runtime egress on eth1/gateway-bridge keeps default 1500 — agent workloads unaffected. Commit 83b6cb1.

nextest filter 'not test(/^integration_/)' in sandboxd/.config/nextest.toml only anchors against bare test names — it filters integration tests in tests/ directories but does NOT filter unit tests inside src/ whose qualified name has a module prefix (e.g. 'tcp::tests::integration_foo'). The CLAUDE.md convention works for unit tests too only after the regex is loosened to '(^|::)integration_'. Surfaced when renaming sandbox-nft-deny-logger src/-mod tcp tests; not previously visible because every prior integration_* test lived in tests/.

tests/e2e/conftest.py sandbox_daemon readiness probe was racing the daemon bind/listen window: socket file appears at bind, connect returns ECONNREFUSED until listen. The old socket_path.exists() check returned prematurely, causing intermittent ECONNREFUSED on the first CLI call under load. Fixed in commit b8a123c by replacing the existence poll with an actual AF_UNIX connect probe (FileNotFoundError plus ConnectionRefusedError = keep polling, success = ready, anything else propagates).

Base-image stop step 5 (build_base_image_inner): single graceful limactl stop with 120s budget timed out under e2e suite I/O contention (in-guest systemd shutdown + qcow2 fsync of dirty pagecache exceeds budget). Replaced with graceful-then-force fallback (60s graceful + 60s force, total ceiling unchanged at 120s). Warn! on fallback preserves diagnostic signal — operators correlate recurring fallbacks with host load vs. genuine guest-side regressions. Force-stop is safe for the base image because it is a build artifact (qcow2 flushes via QEMU SIGTERM exit handler; metadata file write at step 6 is what finalizes the build). Commit a02ac9e.

E2E test failures now auto-dump the last 100 lines of sandboxd stderr/stdout to pytest's failure section via pytest_runtest_makereport + autouse fixture in tests/e2e/conftest.py. Removes the manual /tmp/pytest-of-*/sandboxd0/sandboxd.{stderr,stdout}.log step that was previously the first diagnostic move on every flake.

Three e2e test files deliberately SIGKILL the session-scoped sandboxd and Popen a fresh one on the same --socket and --base-dir, reopening the existing stderr/stdout log paths in append mode: test_networking.py:test_daemon_restart_recovery (and its fallback at test_networking.py:1058), test_lite.py (~:755), and test_policy_persistence.py (~:241). The respawned Popen handle is swapped back into sandbox_daemon["process"] (conftest.py:545-552, 574-584). This means a single pytest session can have multiple sandboxd 'sandboxd starting' lines in the same log file — entirely expected, not a crash signal. Future log-tailing diagnostics must take this into account.

The bind→listen race in sandboxd's startup window also affected SIX inline daemon-respawn paths in three e2e tests — the b8a123c fix landed only in the session-scoped fixture, missing test_lite.py:768/878, test_networking.py:981/1069, and test_policy_persistence.py:254/351, all of which polled os.path.exists() instead of attempting a connect(). When any of those tests hit their respawn path, the next test in the suite raced ECONNREFUSED into the bind→listen window. Fixed in 10909e4 by extracting wait_for_daemon_ready(socket_path, proc, timeout) into tests/e2e/conftest.py and routing all seven call sites (the fixture + the six inline ones) through it. Future inline-daemon-respawn code MUST use this helper, not os.path.exists.

End-to-end suite (make test-e2e) now runs to completion under the cumulative S13 fix set (commits up to 10909e4) — confirms the bind→listen race and libslirp PMTU blackhole were the dominant flake sources. Operator-confirmed validation.

tests/e2e venv version-mismatch trap is fully addressed: Makefile guard via VENV_STAMP target with python-minor-version-stamped filename was already in place from commit ccd0fd7 (April 25, 2026, M10-S8) — make-driven path auto-rebuilds the venv when host python3 minor version differs from the stamped one. The remaining gap was the manual 'source tests/e2e/.venv/bin/activate && python -m pytest' fast-iteration path documented in CLAUDE.md, which bypasses make and surfaces the trap as a bare ModuleNotFoundError. Commit c84bfce adds a one-paragraph warning + rebuild command directly under that snippet.

Cargo always-rebuild symptom on sandbox-guest + sandbox-route-helper does not reproduce. CARGO_LOG=cargo::core::compiler::fingerprint=trace shows only 'up-to-date' / 'filesystem up-to-date' lines for sandbox-guest, no 'dirty' markers. Workspace has zero build.rs files, ruling out the original primary hypothesis. No env-baked stamps either. Original report likely captured transient behavior (parallel make targets touching files, or initial-cold-cache phase). Dropping #140 as not reproducible; if the symptom recurs the diagnostic command captured in .tasks/handoffs/20260506-133833-todo-140-cargo-cache-investigation.md identifies the dirty reason in one line.

log.title: users.conf schema version + V001 pure transform
log.summary: Added _schema_version Option<u32> field to UsersConfig (deny_unknown_fields preserved) and pure migrate_v001(Value)->Value transform that stamps version 1 and prepends "sandbox" to each pool's allow_users idempotently. 11 unit tests: 7-row § 8.2 transform table (matches § 5.5 examples row-for-row) + 4 § 8.3 schema-field tests including verbatim '_shema_version' typo assertion. cargo nextest 1254/1254 pass, clippy + fmt clean. Single-file change in sandbox-core/src/users_conf.rs (+364 lines). Committed c0d937f.
decisions: migrate_v001 treats non-Object inputs as pass-through rather than panic. Spec 5's framework owns post-transform validation against UsersConfig (typed parse error there). Future migrations (V002+) should mirror this: pure value→value, no rejection.
discoveries: serde_json preserve_order is NOT enabled in this workspace — Value::Object is BTreeMap-backed, keys serialize alphabetically. Future migration tests must rely on structural assert_eq! over Value (order-insensitive), not textual key-order comparisons.

serde deny_unknown_fields error format wraps the offending key in backticks (e.g. 'unknown field `_shema_version`'). Verbatim-key assertions should substring-match the bare token, not include backticks, to stay robust if serde formats change.

log.title: Route-helper pair-check, --for-user, audit log
log.summary: Route-helper now enforces pair-membership: both caller (getpwuid_r) and --for-user (getpwnam_r) must be in the target pool's allow_users. Strict identity resolution replaces the prior soft ok().flatten(); every invocation appends a JSON-Lines audit record (ts/decision/caller/for_user/pool/gateway_ip/pid; reason on denies). Allow-path log-write failures are swallowed; deny-path failures escalate to stderr but still exit DENY_EXIT. Hand-rolled argv parser supports --for-user NAME and --for-user=NAME with strict rejection of malformed forms; default-no-flag treats for_user=caller for older daemons. New modules: src/audit.rs (153L), src/pair_check.rs (175L). Tests: 8-row pair_check unit table + 7 integration tests (the 8th § 8.4 test, integration_route_helper_uid_without_passwd_denies_cleanly, deferred to M15). 1262/1262 default + 101/101 integration green; clippy clean in both feature configs; fmt clean. Committed 3ac4294.
decisions: Route-helper argv parser is hand-rolled (no clap) — keeps the cap'd binary minimal-dep. Accepts both '--for-user NAME' and '--for-user=NAME'; rejects missing-value, duplicate flag, value-looking-like-flag, unrecognised flags. Default-no-flag path treats for_user = caller (lets older daemons keep working pre-M13-S3).
discoveries: Audit-log path resolution: /run/user/1000/sandboxd/route-helper-audit.log → ~/.local/share/sandboxd/route-helper-audit.log → /tmp/sandboxd/route-helper-audit.log. Production-only paths are hardcoded; env override SANDBOX_ROUTE_HELPER_AUDIT_LOG exists ONLY under the test-env-override Cargo feature (mirrors SANDBOX_USERS_CONF privilege story). M14 systemd unit and any future log consumers must align with this chain.

Audit record wire shape per Spec 1 § 3.5: 'caller' and 'for_user' are bare username strings, not objects-with-uid+name. Initial delegation prompt described them as objects; spec wins. M13-S6 review track 1 and M13-S7 verification must assert the bare-string form.

Flaky integration test under parallel load: integration_create_session_container_backend_round_trip (sandboxd crate, pre-existing, unrelated to M13). Flaked once during the integration profile run; passed on isolated re-run. Future sessions running the integration profile should expect occasional retries here and not over-investigate when it self-clears.

log.title: Daemon SO_PEERCRED acceptor + RuntimeStartArgs::for_user threading
log.summary: Daemon-side SO_PEERCRED plumbing complete: custom axum::serve::Listener (PeerCredListener) reads peer_cred() per connection, resolves uid→name via nix User::from_uid, refuses-and-warns on failure without crashing the server. OperatorIdentity { uid: u32, name: String } now flows as Extension on every request via into_make_service_with_connect_info + operator_identity_layer (a from_fn middleware). RuntimeStartArgs::for_user: Option<String> threaded through all four runtime.start() sites (D1 container, L1/L2 Lima, D2 backend-dispatched); Container backend invoke_route_helper emits --for-user <name> before positional args (wire spec § 6.5); Lima accepts-but-ignores. Container errors with SandboxError::Internal if for_user is None and route_helper_path configured (§ 6.3 programming-error contract). Three new propagation integration tests (argv-snapshot, Lima fall-through structural, missing-for-user guard). docs/start/installation.md describes _schema_version + sandbox convention for operators editing users.conf by hand. 1264/1264 default + 104/104 integration green; clippy + fmt clean. Committed 956ca92.
decisions: OperatorIdentity { uid: u32, name: String } lives in sandbox-core::caller_identity (re-exported at crate root), not in sandboxd. Spec 2 (M13-S4) consumes it for owner_username stamping and per-caller SessionStore filtering; placing in sandbox-core avoids a code-move when Spec 2 lands. The orphan-rule workaround for axum's Connected trait is a local PeerCredAddr(OperatorIdentity) newtype in sandboxd::main, unwrapped back in middleware so handlers see the clean Extension<OperatorIdentity> shape.

RuntimeStartArgs::for_user is populated at ALL four runtime.start() call sites (D1 container, L1/L2 Lima fast/slow paths, D2 backend-dispatched), even though Lima accepts-and-ignores. Uniform population is a defensive invariant: it avoids a regression if a third backend lands or if a runtime.start() site moves across branches. The Container backend errors with SandboxError::Internal if for_user is None AND a route_helper_path is configured — the spec § 6.3 'programming error' contract.
discoveries: axum 0.8 per-connection state pattern: implement custom Listener with Addr = PerConnExt type, use into_make_service_with_connect_info::<Listener::Addr>(), then a from_fn middleware reads ConnectInfo<...> and inserts Extension<...> for handlers. Connected<IncomingStream<'_, L>> trait impl glues the listener's Addr into axum's per-conn plumbing.

Integration profile gotcha: any change to sandbox-core triggers a sandbox-route-helper rebuild via transitive dep, which invalidates the cap'd test binary's SHA256 staleness check. Must run 'make install-route-helper-test-cap' before nextest --profile integration. The matching cargo invocation requires '--features sandbox-route-helper/test-env-override' so the cargo binary matches the installed test binary's feature set.

log.title: V006 migration, SessionStore per-caller filtering, handler wiring
log.summary: V006 migration (DELETE FROM sessions + 3 NOT NULL columns) lands with a three-pass SessionStore::new restructure to keep V004's orphan-sweep firing before V006's FK cascade. Every SessionStore read/mutation method now requires caller_username and folds 'AND owner_username = ?N' into its WHERE; foreign sessions are 404-indistinguishable from nonexistent (Ok(None)/Err(SessionNotFound)). update_state_forced → update_state_reconcile rename; static-analysis test (update_state_reconcile_allow_list.rs) pins authorized callers exactly + rejects any caller carrying Extension<OperatorIdentity>. Internal-only callers (gateway monitor, orphan reaper, explicit-fail handlers) use *_unfiltered helpers. SessionDto gains owner_username (additive, serde-default). All 12 HTTP handlers + 2 module handlers (events_http, policy_http) extract Extension<OperatorIdentity> and thread operator.name. create_session stamps owner_username; guest_protocol_version=0, guest_binary_version="" placeholders pending M13-S5. Tests: 4 V006 migration tests, 9 SessionStore filter tests, allow-list static-analysis test, foreign-session-404 integration test. 1280/1280 default + 104/104 integration green; clippy + fmt clean. Committed bfad1ad.
decisions: M13-S4 spec 2 structural core complete: V006 migration + orphan scan; per-caller filter on every SessionStore method (5-arg create_session, caller_username on get/list/update_state/delete/policy methods); update_state_reconcile rename with allow-list static-analysis test; 404-on-foreign-id via Ok(None) at storage boundary; SessionDto.owner_username field; events_http/policy_http thread Extension<OperatorIdentity>; 1280 tests pass, clippy + fmt clean.

Internal-only SessionStore callers (gateway monitor, orphan reaper, explicit-fail handlers) bypass per-caller filtering via *_unfiltered helper methods (list_sessions_unfiltered, get_network_info_unfiltered, etc.). These are pub but rustdoc-marked internal-only. The update_state_reconcile_allow_list.rs static-analysis test pins the exact <path>:<fn> set of authorized callers; a secondary check rejects any caller whose signature includes Extension<OperatorIdentity> (HTTP handlers must never use the reconciler escape hatch).
discoveries: V006 migration ordering: 'DELETE FROM sessions' cascades through session_policies via FK, which would drop orphan rows BEFORE V004's orphan-sweep can fire. The fix was a three-pass restructure of SessionStore::new: pass 1 (V001-V003) snapshots pre-V004 rule counts; pass 2 (V004-V005) sweeps orphans BEFORE V006 cascade-deletes them; pass 3 (V006+) applies the rest. Any future migration that adds destructive DELETEs must consider FK-cascade interactions with prior migrations' sweep passes.

Per-caller filter wire shape: SessionDto.owner_username is additive with #[serde(default)] to keep older clients deserializing responses without the field. Foreign-session probes return 404 indistinguishable from nonexistent (read methods Ok(None), mutations Err(SessionNotFound)) — never 403, so attackers cannot enumerate session IDs by probing.

log.title: Guest version compat + refresh machinery
log.summary: Guest version compat infrastructure complete. DAEMON_GUEST_PROTO_VERSION=1 + SANDBOX_GUEST_VERSION (wired via build.rs reading sandbox-guest/Cargo.toml) constants in sandbox-core::guest; create_session stamps real values (replaces M13-S4 placeholders). Predicates is_protocol_compatible + can_refresh_in_place per spec § 3.3/3.7. GuestRequest::Version + GuestResponse::VersionResult wire variants; guest-side handler. SessionRuntime::refresh_guest_binary trait method; Container impl (docker stop -t 5 → stage binary → docker cp → drop tempfile), Lima impl (limactl start → copy → sudo mv → sudo chmod → systemctl restart → limactl stop), wrapped in spawn_blocking. GuestProtocolIncompatible → HTTP 409 with load-bearing tokens 'refresh is not viable' and 'recreate the session'. SessionStore::update_guest_versions with per-caller filter. start_session compat gate per § 3.4 decision tree (fast/refresh+stamp/refuse). 5 integration tests (§§ 7.4-7.5) + 6-row predicate unit table (§ 7.3). 1290/1290 default + 109/109 integration green; clippy + fmt clean. Committed 38ce8c1. Two flagged gaps: spec § 3.8.1 docker cp incompatible with --read-only rootfs (todo #141, recommended path b: bind-mount on create); stage_embedded_guest_binary reads disk vs spec § 3.6 include_bytes! (todo #142, M15 transition).
discoveries: Spec 2 § 3.8.1's docker cp refresh mechanism is empirically broken on modern Docker (29.4+ with containerd snapshotter): 'docker cp' into a --read-only container rootfs is REJECTED by dockerd with 'container rootfs is marked read-only', regardless of destination path. The lite image (images/lite/Dockerfile) ships /usr/local/bin/sandbox-guest baked into a read-only rootfs — production refresh would fail. M13-S5 integration tests pass via a workaround Dockerfile declaring VOLUME [/usr/local/bin]. Three resolution paths exist: (a) add VOLUME directive to production lite Dockerfile (accept volume-orphan lifecycle); (b) bind-mount host-side sandbox-guest into the container at create time + refresh becomes host-side file replace + recreate (cleanest, agent recommended); (c) docker commit per session (heavy). Requires spec amendment to § 3.8.1.

M13-S5 stage_embedded_guest_binary reads sandbox-guest from disk via lima::guest_agent_path() (sibling-of-daemon lookup), not via Spec 2 § 3.6's prescribed include_bytes! embedding. Works in dev where daemon and guest co-build in target/debug/; production binary distribution (Spec 4 / M15 release-and-install) requires the switch to compile-time include_bytes! plus a build.rs that builds sandbox-guest first. The const SANDBOX_GUEST_VERSION is already wired via build.rs reading sandbox-guest/Cargo.toml — that's the half that landed.

Spec 2 §7.5 names 3 such tests; none exist. (2) No integration_v006_orphan_scan_logs_each_found_orphan despite spec §7.5 calling it out. cargo nextest --profile integration on the route-helper crate requires --features sandbox-route-helper/test-env-override; without the flag the freshly-built binary's sha256 differs from the cap'd install and integration_route_helper_* tests panic with 'installed route helper is stale'. Makefile test-integration target wires this correctly. Verifier docs should match.

Daemon create_session handler persists the session row early (right after spec validation, before any network/Docker/Lima work) — so owner_username stamp survives every downstream failure mode. This is why an SO_PEERCRED integration test can poll the DB without waiting for backend boot.

log.title: /version endpoint + CLI version-equality check
log.summary: M14-S2 implementation: /version endpoint + CLI version-equality handshake. Commit 94fd4ac. Daemon now serves GET /version returning {"version": "<CARGO_PKG_VERSION>"} on the unix socket (no authentication). CLI fetches /version after connect in send_request_with_timeout, refuses on skew with verbatim stderr (version mismatch, CLI is, daemon is, both must match) and exit code 2. Stub Command::Doctor and Command::Version variants added; sandbox version answers locally without a daemon connection. --version output pinned to '<name> <semver>' on both binaries via unit tests. 7 existing mock-daemon tests extended to serve /version. 6 unit tests + 3 integration tests added (including bonus integration_cli_version_subcommand_bypasses_handshake). Followup todo #155 about CLI path symmetry (ssh/cp/sync/logs/events/inspect/describe don't fire handshake — spec only pins send_request). All gates green: 1310 default passed, 119/120 integration passed (one pre-existing flake recovered on rerun-alone).
decisions: Doctor-subcommand version-bypass implemented via real Command::Doctor stub (prints 'not yet implemented'), not parse-level. M14-S3 inherits final bypass plumbing (command_bypasses_version_check is single source of truth for the bypass set). Spec § 11.3 doctor-bypass test asserts against the real variant.
discoveries: Sandboxd binary was missing 'version' attribute on clap #[command] — sandboxd --version errored with 'unexpected argument'. Now pinned by unit tests on both binaries. Spec 4 § 4.4.5's awk '{print }' parse would have broken before this fix.

log.title: sandbox doctor + GET /diagnostics endpoint
log.summary: M14-S3 commit 8d01d58: sandbox doctor + GET /diagnostics. New sandbox-cli/src/doctor.rs (1662 LOC) with Check trait, C1-C13 registry, serial-then-parallel two-phase execution, colored output formatter, --verbose flag, exit codes 0/1/2. Dev-mode degradation via informational Skip (C4/C5/C10 when sandbox system user absent). Cascade: C2 failure skips daemon-dependent checks rather than failing them. New daemon /diagnostics endpoint returns system-level fields (daemon_uid/user, kvm_readable/writable, gateway/lite_image_present, users_conf_pool) plus per-operator scoped fields (guest_version_drift, substrate_orphans) filtered by caller_username. installation.md updated with system-service deployment + doctor section. 5 new integration tests + ~10 unit tests. All gates green: 1322 default pass, 124/125 integration pass (1 known pre-existing flake). Smoke test against live daemon: 10 passes, 0 fails, 3 informational skips, exit 0.
decisions: Doctor C1 systemctl logic is permissive: only 'active' returns Pass directly; inactive/failed/etc. fall through to socket-connect probe so dev daemons (started by hand) still pass. Fail hint only fires if both systemctl AND socket probe fail. Dev-mode degradation per Spec §12.2 implemented as informational Skip (not Pass) for C4/C5/C10 when sandbox system user absent — skips don't flip exit code to 1.
discoveries: Doctor cascade behavior: if C2 (/version handshake) doesn't Pass, every daemon-dependent check (C3,C5,C6,C7,C8,C11,C12,C13) reports Skip rather than spuriously failing. Cascade test branches on C2's outcome rather than C1 so it's robust on hosts where the unit is genuinely active. DiagnosticsPayload.daemon_uid/daemon_user carry #[allow(dead_code)] — wire-level forward-compat hooks not yet consumed by any C-check.

log.title: Spec 3 delivery verification
log.summary: M14-S5 (Spec 3 daemon productionization delivery verification, terminal session of M14). Produced claim-to-code map at .tasks/specs/2026-05-11-daemon-productionization-design/2026-05-11-daemon-productionization-design-delivery.md (191 claims / 170 (a) / 15 (b) / 6 (c) / 0 blockers). Initially 1 BLOCKER (P5.11 socket mode 0660 not pinned at bind) — resolved by factoring bind_socket helper + UMask=0117 systemd directive + spec § 4.1 amendment + falsifiable unit test. Example replays 4/4 pass: sandbox doctor --verbose, sandbox version (no daemon connect, awk-parseable), version-skew tokens verbatim (exit 2), make gateway-image produces sandbox-gateway:0.1.0. All gates green: 1325 unit tests, fmt+clippy clean. M14 complete (104/114); next is M15 (Spec 4 release/install pipeline).
discoveries: Spec 3 replays 4/4 pass on every load-bearing assertion. Replay 4 caveat: live gateway container's image tag not docker-ps-confirmed due to host route-helper at /usr/local/bin (Apr 27, cap_sys_admin=ep only) rejecting current daemon ABI; tag-pinning verified via daemon startup log + 3 call-site code inspection at sandboxd/src/main.rs:1220,6006,6983 instead. Re-install prod route-helper to get docker-ps-grade observation.

Spec 3 delivery map written: 191 claims | 169 (a) | 15 (b) | 6 (c) | 1 BLOCKER (P5.11 socket mode 0660 not pinned at UnixListener::bind, daemon would land 0644 under production umask 022 and doctor C5 would false-negative). Three (c) rows reference todo #157 (/diagnostics ALL-fields-gated more conservative than spec § 13.2). Fix agent dispatched.

M14-S5 conjunctive hard gate PASS: (1) delivery map committed at 1a3a033; (2) 0 BLOCKERs (P5.11 socket-mode resolved at 6f44559 via bind_socket helper + UMask=0117 + spec § 4.1 amendment); (3) every concrete claim of Spec 3 §§ 1-13+15 mapped — 191 claims | 170 (a) | 15 (b) | 6 (c) → todos #153/#157/#158/#159; (4) example replays 4/4 (Replay 4 docker-ps caveat is environmental, not spec deviation); (5) 1325/1325 unit tests pass, fmt clean, build clean. Spec 3 closed; M14 milestone complete; ready for M15 (Spec 4 release/install).

log.title: GitHub Actions release workflow + CI shellcheck
log.summary: M15-S1 GitHub Actions release workflow + ci shellcheck. release.yml: two-arch matrix (x86_64 ubuntu-22.04 + aarch64 ubuntu-22.04-arm, fail-fast: false), per-arch tarball assembly (bin/, libexec/, systemd/, images/sandbox-gateway-<ver>.tar, attestations/, MANIFEST via inline Python from spec § 3.3), cosign-installer@v3.7.0 + cosign v2.4.1 sign-blob, attest-build-provenance@v1, softprops/action-gh-release@v2. Triggered on push.tags. ci.yml: new shellcheck job (POSIX-sh + style) short-circuits when scripts not yet present. Spec internal contradiction (§ 3.3 vs § 4.1 on publish-install-script) resolved by following § 4.1 (hosting authoritative); tracked as todo #160 for M15-S5 spec cleanup. yamllint clean; actionlint clean modulo info-level SC2086 inside the verbatim spec block.
discoveries: Spec 4 contains internal contradiction: § 3.3 references publish-install-script job in release.yml, § 4.1 says release workflow does NOT republish scripts (they ride docs.yml). Followed § 4.1 as authoritative hosting source. release.yml contains a comment block documenting the absence. Tracked as a spec-cleanup todo for M15-S5 delivery verification.

log.title: install.sh + uninstall.sh + GitHub Pages hosting
log.summary: M15-S2 install.sh + uninstall.sh + Pages hosting. install.sh (972 LOC POSIX-sh) implements all 24 spec § 4.4 steps; idempotent; embeds cosign v2.4.1 sha256 trust root; writes /var/lib/sandbox/.install-state.json per § 4.5; logs to /var/log/sandbox-install.log per § 4.6. uninstall.sh (535 LOC) implements all 12 § 5.2 steps; mirrors install via state file. Two deviations tracked: (1) state writer dropped python3 prereq via POSIX-printf, byte-equivalent output; (2) cosign step 8 falls back to /usr/local/bin/cosign on network failure (trust root unchanged). docs.yml: build-time cp into site/public/ before npm run build (symlinks rejected as Astro-fragile). installation.md: two-path rewrite (operator curl|bash primary, developer make setup-dev-env secondary). shellcheck clean (-s sh -S style, zero suppressions). Both scripts: --help exits 0, --unknown exits 2.
discoveries: M15-S2 install.sh/uninstall.sh deviations from spec: (a) state file writer switched python3 heredoc → POSIX printf (drops python3 prereq, byte-equivalent output, jq-validated against § 4.5 schema); (b) step 8 cosign fallback to /usr/local/bin/cosign on network failure (trust root sha256 verification unchanged, more lenient than spec). Both ride on the spec's snippets being illustrative-not-binding. Tracked under todo #161 and #162 for M15-S5 verification.

log.title: Lima E2E test harness for install/uninstall
log.summary: M15-S3 Lima E2E test harness shipped. tests/install-e2e/ scaffolding (conftest.py 619 LOC, build-local-tarball.sh 270 LOC, 8 test files, pyproject.toml, requirements.txt) plus .github/workflows/install-e2e.yml ([self-hosted, kvm] runner). 12 test cases: 4 happy-path (ubuntu-22.04, debian-12, fedora-41 RHEL paths, idempotent double-run), 1 partial-failure recovery, 2 refusal (wrong-arch + preexisting different version), 1 air-gapped, 3 uninstall (clean, purge, double-run), 1 systemd unit smoke. All 12 PASS in 43 min on local KVM host. Three install.sh bugs caught by the harness: (a) setcap getcap parser for libcap 2.30+ output format, (b) preexist version capture when binary --version fails to invoke (state.json fallback), (c) glibc portability via docker build inside ubuntu:22.04. shellcheck clean, yamllint clean. Closes the integration_systemd_unit_smokes gap deferred from M14-S3.
discoveries: M15-S3 Lima harness caught a real bug on first VM run: install.sh setcap_route_helper sed pattern assumed libcap (less than 2.30) getcap output format (path equals caps plus ep). libcap 2.30+ (Ubuntu 22.04+, Fedora 36+) drops the equals-space delimiter. Old pattern fell through to full line, making the verification check always fail. Replaced with awk NF. Without M15-S3 this would have shipped to the first operator on a modern distro.

M15-S3 Lima matrix 12/12 PASS in 43 min after two rounds of fixes: (1) preexist same-version skip (install.sh state.json fallback for empty --version output), (2) systemd is-active race (wait helper with journalctl dump on failed); (3) build-portability (build-local-tarball.sh defaults to ubuntu:22.04 docker build, glibc-2.34 floor, opt-out via SANDBOX_RELEASE_PORTABLE_BUILD=0). Matrix also surfaced two latent smoke-test bugs: sudo traversal for /run/sandbox (0750) and doctor-as-sandbox-user instead of root for operator-group check.
