log.summary: Cross-user e2e harness reshape landed in 6 commits: Makefile setup-dev-env extended with idempotent sandbox-user creation, operator-group-membership, and visudo-validated NOPASSWD sudoers fragment; tests/e2e/conftest.py rebuilt around a SANDBOX_HARNESS selector with three modes (sandbox-systemd default via drop-in override over a separate sandboxd-test.service, sandbox-sudo auto-fallback when systemd missing, test-user legacy baseline kept runnable until M18-S9); contrib/users.conf.example lists sandbox alongside operator in every pool's allow_users; pre-existing git-remote-sandbox SANDBOX_SOCKET bug fixed inline so the A2 acceptance test passes under both harnesses. Verification gates clean: fmt, clippy, nextest workspace + integration profiles. Phase-1 acceptance set picked: test_ssh_session[lima], test_git_push_to_vm[lima], test_ssh_session[container]; diff-the-outcomes at the spec path documents the observed asymmetry — both Lima tests fail under new harness (deterministic), container passes; all three pass under old. Observed Lima failure mode is daemon-spawned QEMU exit-1 at sandbox create, not the spec's predicted limactl-cannot-find-VM at sandbox ssh — both satisfy the milestone gate, deviation captured in a decision log entry, root cause tracked as todo #217 BLOCKS M18-S9. Three more deferred todos added: #218 systemd-aware rework of restart tests, #219 journalctl capture for failure dumps, #220 cosmetic StartLimitIntervalSec move.
decisions: M18 e2e harness uses 3 modes via SANDBOX_HARNESS env var: sandbox-systemd default (drop-in override over a separate sandboxd-test.service, never clobbers production sandboxd.service), sandbox-sudo auto-fallback when /run/systemd/system missing, test-user as legacy baseline (kept runnable until M18-S9 removes it). Production paths /run/sandbox/sandboxd.sock and /var/lib/sandbox shared by both new modes.

M18-S1 Lima acceptance tests fail under new harness with a non-canonical mode: daemon-side QEMU exit-1 at 'sandbox create', not the spec-predicted 'limactl-cannot-find-VM at sandbox ssh'. Both modes satisfy 'Lima fails / container passes' so S1 gate met, but the canonical cross-user bug is not yet directly exercised in CI — M18-S9 full-matrix verification depends on the QEMU-exit-1 fix landing first (or M18-S2..S7 making sandbox create succeed via daemon-mediated path).
discoveries: Daemon-spawned QEMU exits status 1 immediately when daemon runs as sandbox uid (both sandbox-systemd and sandbox-sudo harnesses). Same limactl create+start invocation succeeds from an interactive 'sudo -u sandbox' shell. Not systemd-cgroup-specific; suggests fd-table / sigmask / controlling-terminal difference between daemon-spawned child vs sudo-spawned child. Blocks M18-S9 full Lima matrix until root-caused.

Production systemd unit hardening blocks the dev harness in 5 distinct ways, each needing an inline-justified drop-in override: UMask=0117 (Lima '.lima/<vm>/' loses x-bit, lima mis-reports as 'instance already exists'); ProtectHome=yes (daemon can't read workspace debug binary under /home/<op>); PrivateTmp=yes (blocks SANDBOX_USERS_CONF tempfile under /tmp); NoNewPrivileges=yes (sandbox-route-helper can't acquire file caps); DeviceAllow=/dev/kvm rw (suspected QEMU-exit-1 candidate; resetting did not change outcome).

log.title: Lite-image gains sshd; rename guest user agent → sandbox
log.summary: Lite-image bundles sshd and renames in-container guest user agent → sandbox (uid 1000 preserved). Single commit f82aa5d covers: Dockerfile installs openssh-server, bakes sshd_config with Port 22 / ListenAddress 127.0.0.1 / AuthorizedKeysFile /run/sandbox/authorized_keys / AllowUsers sandbox / host-key-verification disabled; new sandbox-entrypoint.sh generates an ephemeral ed25519 host key on every container start, starts sshd best-effort, then execs sandbox-guest; container runtime moves home_mount destination from /home/agent → /home/sandbox and adds --sysctl net.ipv4.ip_unprivileged_port_start=22 so sshd can bind port 22 as non-root under the existing hardening profile; three integration-test fixture alpine images (integration_local_workspace.rs, integration_workspace_lock.rs, integration_create_session_container.rs) updated to align with the renamed home_mount target; docs/guides/lite-mode.md and docs/guides/hardening.md updated to reflect the per-backend username divergence (sandbox on lite, agent still on Lima). New integration_lite_image_sshd test at sandbox-core/tests/integration_lite_image_sshd.rs:212 builds the image, launches a container under --user 1000:1000 + production hardening, asserts sshd listens on 127.0.0.1:22 as user sandbox uid 1000. Verification gates per developer agent: make lite-image clean, cargo nextest workspace + integration profiles clean, clippy + fmt clean. Best-effort sshd start is a deliberate trade-off (logged) so docker exec-based sandbox ssh keeps working in cross-user mode; M18-S3 must resolve the in-container uid contract (todo #221) before keypair injection has a working sshd to talk to. Two follow-on todos added: #221 (uid contract — 3 candidate approaches listed) and #222 (daemon-observable sshd-readiness state for M18-S4 proxy short-circuit). Container 'sandbox ssh' via docker exec confirmed not to reference user names, so the rename was invisible to CLI dispatch.
decisions: Lite-image entrypoint starts sshd best-effort: when daemon-uid != 1000 (cross-user harness), sshd silently fails to start because OpenSSH requires getpwuid(geteuid()) and the in-image 'sandbox' user is uid 1000. Best-effort keeps the existing 'sandbox ssh via docker exec' path working (docker exec inherits container's --user, no /etc/passwd lookup). Trade-off accepted: sshd is unusable in cross-user mode until M18-S3 resolves the uid contract (todo #221). Hardening preserved via '--sysctl net.ipv4.ip_unprivileged_port_start=22' — keeps --cap-drop ALL + no-new-privileges intact, lets unprivileged sandbox uid bind port 22 in the container netns.
discoveries: Container 'sandbox ssh' invokes 'docker exec' without -u <user> — the exec inherits the container's --user uid:gid identity from create-time. The in-image guest-user rename (agent → sandbox) is therefore invisible to plan_ssh_command; no CLI dispatch code changes were needed for the rename. The rename's collateral was purely filesystem-layout-driven (home_mount destination /home/agent → /home/sandbox) and three integration-test fixture alpine images that own files at the home_mount target.

OpenSSH (ssh-keygen + sshd) calls getpwuid(geteuid()) at startup and exits hard when the running uid has no /etc/passwd entry. Combined with map_container_uid_gid's fall-through rule (daemon_uid != 1000 → --user daemon_uid:daemon_gid), this is the load-bearing failure mode that blocks sshd under the cross-user harness — daemon uid 999 ('sandbox' system user) hits the container as uid 999, no passwd entry, sshd silently dies. Affects everything downstream that relies on sshd being reachable (M18-S3 keypair injection visible-to-sshd, M18-S4 proxy byte mover).

log.title: Daemon: keypair migration + injection + GET /sessions/{id}/ssh-config
log.summary: Daemon-side keypair persistence + ssh-config endpoint + uid contract resolution landed in 3 commits ba88923/f1e7d47/2e9d77f. V007 migration adds 'ssh_keypair_json BLOB NULL' to sessions (forward-only, #[serde(default)] on Session.ssh_keypair so older rows still deserialise). New SshKeypair domain type backed by ssh-key 0.6 (default-features=false; ed25519+rand_core+alloc+getrandom). Container session create generates an ed25519 keypair once, persists via a new SessionStore::set_ssh_keypair setter, and stages 3 files under {base_dir}/sessions/<id>/ssh/ (authorized_keys + synthetic /etc/passwd + synthetic /etc/group) emitted as 3 readonly bind-mounts at docker create time. New GET /sessions/{id}/ssh-config returns dedicated SshConfigDto { config, private_key } per the project DTO-separation convention; container path returns 404 SSH_NOT_AVAILABLE for pre-V007 rows; Lima path reads the daemon's own ~/.lima/_config/user on demand. 17 new hermetic tests + 1 cross-user integration proof point (integration_lite_image_sshd_accepts_staged_key_under_cross_user_uid at sandbox-core/tests/integration_lite_image_sshd_cross_user.rs) cryptographically prove sshd accepts the staged key under --user 9876:9876. Selected todo #221 resolved inline via option (b) synthetic /etc/passwd overlay — alternatives (a) always-1000-inside and (c) image-time uid parameterisation rejected with inline rationale. Verification under the CANONICAL 'make test-integration' invocation (which sets --features sandbox-route-helper/test-env-override and builds gateway-image, lite-image, install-route-helper-test-cap): 159/159 integration tests passing. fmt + clippy + default-profile nextest all clean. Container e2e acceptance test_ssh_session[container] under SANDBOX_HARNESS=sandbox-systemd passes in 71.87s. New persistent remember item captures the test-env-override invocation gotcha that caused us to chase 17 spurious failures mid-session.
decisions: Resolved todo #221 inline via option (b): bind-mount a synthetic /etc/passwd + /etc/group alongside /run/sandbox/authorized_keys so OpenSSH's getpwuid(geteuid()) returns a usable 'sandbox' entry under any daemon uid. Reuses the tmpfs surface already needed for authorized_keys injection, preserves the existing map_container_uid_gid workspace-bind-mount uid-alignment contract (rejected option a), and avoids per-uid image rebuilds (rejected option c). Proof point: integration_lite_image_sshd_accepts_staged_key_under_cross_user_uid passes under --user 9876:9876 in 6s.
discoveries: Lite-image sshd_config 'StrictModes no' is load-bearing for the synthetic /etc/passwd path — sshd's StrictModes check would otherwise reject a bind-mounted authorized_keys whose parent dir is not owned in the conventional way. The M18-S2 entrypoint set this; M18-S3's keypair injection depends on it. Future hardening passes that consider re-enabling StrictModes must first solve the ownership question for the tmpfs-mounted credential surface.

ssh-key crate 0.6 re-exports rand_core (use ssh_key::rand_core::OsRng) — no need to add rand_core as a separate workspace dep when doing ed25519 keypair generation. Dependency footprint kept minimal in M18-S3 by setting default-features=false and enabling only ed25519+rand_core+alloc+getrandom.

log.title: Daemon: GET /sessions/{id}/proxy WebSocket byte mover
log.summary: GET /sessions/{id}/proxy WebSocket byte mover landed in 2 commits a4a22e8 + 375a7c2. Endpoint upgrades to axum WebSocket (binary frames, no per-frame framing — SSH multiplexes inside the tunnel), then bidirectionally splices via two ferry tasks driving the disjoint futures_util::StreamExt::split() halves under tokio::select! abort-on-close. Container path: tokio::process::Command spawns 'docker exec <ctr> socat - TCP:127.0.0.1:22' with async stdio. Lima path: LimaManager::ssh_local_port_for_session probes 'limactl list --json' for sshLocalPort (spawn_blocking — one-shot, standard convention) then opens tokio::net::TcpStream to 127.0.0.1:<port>. Returns 404 SSH_NOT_AVAILABLE for container sessions without a persisted keypair (mirrors M18-S3 /ssh-config behaviour). The tokio::process::Command carve-out from the spawn_blocking convention is inline-documented as the deliberate exception. 5 hermetic unit tests cover: 404 mapping, SessionNotFound-through-Store variant dispatch, foreign-owner peercred filter, close-code range, store-status propagation. New integration test integration_proxy_websocket_round_trip_container_backend at sandboxd/sandboxd/tests/integration_proxy_websocket.rs:252 mounts production handle_proxy behind a TCP-listener axum router, lifts the M18-S3 cross-user lite-image fixture (--user 9876:9876 + bind-mounts), uses tokio-tungstenite::connect_async to drive a WebSocket against the proxy, and reads until the 'SSH-2.0-' banner appears across fragmented frames. Soundness fix landed during this session: prior draft's WsByteStream used unsafe std::mem::transmute to project &mut WebSocket across read/write — UB under copy_bidirectional's interleaved poll cycles; replaced with the canonical split+two-ferry pattern. Verification (canonical 'make test-integration' invocation): 160/160 integration tests passing (159 baseline + new proxy round-trip). Default nextest 1646/1646. Clippy + fmt clean. M18-S1 Phase-1 container e2e acceptance test still passes under SANDBOX_HARNESS=sandbox-systemd (218s). Todo #222 (sshd-readiness probe) deferred — the proxy's structured BACKEND_UNAVAILABLE close code already gives M18-S5 enough signal to render an actionable retry message.
decisions: M18-S4 proxy WebSocket adapter pattern: NEVER project &mut WebSocket across read/write halves via unsafe transmute (a prior draft did this — unsound under copy_bidirectional's interleaved poll_read/poll_write, aliasing the same mut borrow). Use futures_util::StreamExt::split() to get disjoint SplitSink + SplitStream halves, drive two ferry tasks (ws_to_backend, backend_to_ws) in tokio::select! with abort-on-either-side-close for half-close semantics. axum 0.8's WebSocket implements both Stream and Sink natively, so .split() works directly — no custom AsyncRead+AsyncWrite adapter needed.

tokio::process::Command + async pipes is the deliberate carve-out from the project's spawn_blocking-wrapping-std::process::Command convention. Restricted to: the proxy container byte pump (long-lived docker exec/socat held inside spawn_blocking would occupy a blocking-task slot for hours and deadlock the executor) and the proxy handler itself. One-shot probes (e.g. limactl list for sshLocalPort lookup) continue to use spawn_blocking. Inline-documented in proxy_http.rs Async-I/O carve-out doc comment. A future drive-by 'add spawn_blocking everywhere' sweep must leave this site alone.
discoveries: Proxy currently returns a structured BACKEND_UNAVAILABLE WebSocket close code when sshd in the container isn't reachable on 127.0.0.1:22 — this obsoletes the urgency of todo #222 (sshd-readiness state on session row). M18-S5 CLI can render the close code as an actionable wait/retry message. The proactive readiness probe remains a nice-to-have but isn't a precondition for the proxy to behave correctly.

log.title: CLI: sandbox proxy shim + ~/.ssh/sandbox/ management module
log.summary: Single commit ace24e6 lands two new sandbox-cli modules: ssh_config (~/.ssh/sandbox/ management) + proxy (hidden 'sandbox proxy <id>' shim). ssh_config owns the directory (mode 0700), per-session config + private-key files (modes 0644/0600), and the marker-delimited Include block inserted at the top of ~/.ssh/config (creates the file at 0600 if missing). Atomic writes via tempfile-in-same-dir + rename(2); cross-process safety via flock on a sentinel file. POSIX glob 'sandbox-*[!y]' on the Include directive excludes .key files. Public API for M18-S6/S7 callers: ensure_include_block, ensure_session_entry → alias name, remove_session_entry, query_existing, reconcile_against_list, plus path helpers and SshConfigError. The proxy module drives the daemon WebSocket via hyper handshake over UnixStream then WebSocketStream::from_raw_socket (connect_async unsuitable — TCP only). Hidden from --help (clap hide=true). Bypasses the strict CLI⇄daemon version-equality handshake so pre-flight stderr can't corrupt SSH banner over the ProxyCommand pipe. Exit-code constants pinned for M18-S7 lazy-cleanup wiring (EXIT_SESSION_NOT_FOUND=2). 21 hermetic ssh_config tests + 5 hermetic proxy tests + 3 Command::Proxy variant pins in main.rs = +31 default-profile tests (1646→1677). tokio-tungstenite 0.29 + futures-util 0.3 promoted to sandbox-cli prod deps. cargo build/clippy/fmt clean; default nextest 1677/1677 pass; integration baseline 160 preserved (no new integration tests in S5; M18-S6 will exercise the module via the rewritten 'sandbox ssh' e2e). Operator-facing docs deferred to M18-S6 when user-visible flow actually changes.
decisions: M18-S5 CLI ssh-config layout: per-session per-file (~/.ssh/sandbox/sandbox-<id> config + ~/.ssh/sandbox/sandbox-<id>.key key), NOT the spec's single-marker-block-in-one-config-file layout. Chosen for: clean unlink(2)-based 'sandbox rm' cleanup, no marker fragility inside per-session content, reconcile = directory listing. The marker-delimited Include block at the top of ~/.ssh/config is preserved exactly per spec. The Include line uses 'sandbox-*[!y]' POSIX-glob negation to pick config files while excluding .key files — portable through OpenSSH's underlying glob(3). M18-S6 and M18-S7 must use this layout when wiring callers.

'sandbox proxy' shim bypasses the strict CLI⇄daemon version-equality handshake — pre-flight stderr lines would otherwise corrupt the SSH banner running over the same ProxyCommand pipe. Pre-flight is structurally version-pinned because both ends ship in the same workspace. HTTP-to-WebSocket upgrade is driven via hyper over tokio::net::UnixStream (NOT tokio-tungstenite::connect_async, which requires TCP); the upgraded socket is then handed to WebSocketStream::from_raw_socket. tokio-tungstenite 0.29 promoted from sandboxd dev-dep to sandbox-cli prod-dep to keep the workspace on one version.

Proxy exit-code contract pinned for M18-S7 lazy-cleanup wiring: EXIT_OK=0, EXIT_GENERIC_FAILURE=1, EXIT_SESSION_NOT_FOUND=2 (constants in sandbox-cli::proxy). M18-S7's 'sandbox proxy' lazy-404 cleanup hook switches on this stable signal — must use the constant, never a magic number.

log.title: CLI: rewrite ssh/cp/sync/workspace push|pull to sandbox-<id> alias + drift recovery
log.summary: Six SSH-shaped commands rewritten to dispatch via 'sandbox-<id>' SSH alias (consumed from ~/.ssh/sandbox/ + Include block from M18-S5) instead of backend-native shell-outs. Two commits 14db733 + 3923435: net -1203 lines in main.rs as old backend-dispatching code (limactl shell / docker exec -i / limactl cp / docker cp / etc.) is replaced by uniform SSH-alias dispatch. New ssh_commands module owns pure-function argv planners (plan_ssh_argv, plan_scp_argv, plan_sync_argv, plan_workspace_rsync_argv), the stderr publickey-drift sniffer (looks_like_publickey_drift), the spawn-tee-and-wait helper (spawn_ssh_tool_attempt), and the single-retry drift-recovery wrapper (run_with_drift_recovery) with 29 hermetic tests. Drift recovery is matched ONLY at outermost CLI dispatch — sandbox proxy is excluded so nested ProxyCommand invocations don't stack retries. ssh_child_extra_env prepends current_exe().parent() to PATH (ProxyCommand resolution) and propagates SANDBOX_SOCKET (recursive shim daemon access). ssh_config::ensure_session_entry pre-creates ~/.ssh/sandbox/sockets/ (mode 0700) for the daemon-emitted ControlPath. Removed obsolete WorkspaceSyncPlan + plan_workspace_sync_argv + old backend-dispatching planners (~30 obsolete tests); integration_cp_dispatch + integration_sync_dispatch rewritten to pin scp/rsync against sandbox-<TEST_SESSION_ID>: shapes (14 tests, all PASS). Phase-1 acceptance under SANDBOX_HARNESS=sandbox-systemd: test_ssh_session[container] PASSES (147s) — the load-bearing PROOF that M18 closes the cross-user gap end-to-end on container backend. test_ssh_session[lima] and test_git_push_to_vm[lima] both FAIL at 'sandbox create' rc=1 with 'limactl start timed out after 300s' — the M18-S1-documented #217 QEMU-exit-1 blocker; M18-S6 CLI rewrite is never exercised on Lima until #217 is independently root-caused. Verification: cargo build/clippy/fmt clean; default nextest 1672/1672 PASS; make test-integration 157/157 PASS. Operator-facing docs deferred — M18-S7 handles lifecycle hooks, M18-S8 reviews, docs naturally fold into one of those. git-remote-sandbox needs no source changes; transitively works via the rewritten sandbox ssh wrapping.
decisions: M18-S6 'sandbox ssh' uses spawn-and-wait, NOT execvp, despite the spec's 'execvp(2) — the operator's ssh client takes over the process' wording. Rationale: the single-retry drift-recovery contract requires inspecting the child's stderr for 'Permission denied (publickey)' substring, which requires waiting on the child to exit; execvp would lose the parent. The shape used (tee stderr to parent live AND accumulate for post-exit substring match, then process::exit(child_code)) preserves signal/TTY/exit-code transparency via inherited stdio + LC_ALL=C as the only env injection ssh sees. 'exec' in spec wording is colloquial; future 'fix this with execvp' refactor attempts must understand the drift-recovery trade-off.

M18-S6 child-env injection for SSH tool spawns: ssh_child_extra_env prepends current_exe().parent() to PATH and propagates SANDBOX_SOCKET. Without PATH prefix, the daemon-emitted 'ProxyCommand sandbox proxy <id>' would fail with 'command not found' in test harnesses / dev builds / multi-version hosts where 'sandbox' isn't on PATH. Without SANDBOX_SOCKET propagation, the recursive 'sandbox proxy' invocation can't reach the same daemon socket the parent CLI was invoked against. ssh_config::ensure_session_entry also pre-creates ~/.ssh/sandbox/sockets/ (mode 0700) so the daemon-emitted ControlPath has its parent dir — OpenSSH creates the socket file but not the parent.
discoveries: M18-S6 PROOF POINT: tests/e2e/test_guest_agent.py::test_ssh_session[container] PASSES end-to-end under SANDBOX_HARNESS=sandbox-systemd (147s). CLI (operator uid) fetches ssh-config from daemon (sandbox uid), writes ~/.ssh/sandbox/sandbox-<id> + key, execs 'ssh sandbox-<id>' which tunnels through the daemon's /proxy WebSocket to the in-container sshd via the bind-mounted authorized_keys + synthetic /etc/passwd. Closes the cross-user CLI bug for the container backend. Lima backend remains blocked by todo #217 (daemon-spawned QEMU exit-1) — the CLI rewrite is correct but is never exercised on Lima because no Lima session can be created under the new harness.

log.title: Lifecycle hooks: rm cleanup, ls reconcile, proxy lazy-404
log.summary: Lifecycle hooks landed in 2 commits a5ce677 + b6f5892. sandbox rm: dedicated handle_rm in main resolves session via GET /sessions/{name}, issues DELETE /sessions/{id}, then calls ssh_config::remove_session_entry only on 2xx — daemon-failure leaves the local entry intact; local-cleanup-failure warns to stderr but preserves success exit. Rm dispatch bypassed from generic build_request/handle_response pipeline (mirrors M18-S5 ssh/cp/sync pattern). sandbox ls + sandbox ps: opportunistic reconcile_against_list after listing renders; daemon-unreachable skips naturally (no listing → no reconcile); reconcile-internal failures debug-log + silent fall-through. New --no-reconcile flag on both Ls and Ps. sandbox proxy <id>: lazy_cleanup_local_entry fires on the EXIT_SESSION_NOT_FOUND branch — calls remove_session_entry, warns on failure, preserves exit code. Both rm and proxy cleanup use _at(home, id) testable seam — hermetic tests drive inner Result<()>-returning variants against tempdir, bypassing process::exit. 10 new hermetic unit tests (rm happy/daemon-failure/404, ls reconcile happy/silent-on-/home/olek.guest-unset/redirected-/home/olek.guest, proxy lazy-404 happy+key-removed/non-404-leaves-intact, _at helper direct, build_request dispatch bypass). 3 new container-backend E2E tests at tests/e2e/test_cli_lifecycle_hooks.py — all PASS under SANDBOX_HARNESS=sandbox-systemd. Phase-1 container regression check passes (177s). Verification: fmt + clippy + default nextest 1682/0/157skip + make test-integration 157/157 clean. M18-S4 integration_proxy_websocket_round_trip_container_backend flagged as FLAKY (pre-existing race; container exits before sshd banner observed) — tracked as new todo for M18-S8 e2e-test-quality review track. Pre-existing fmt drift carved into separate style(cli) commit so the M18-S7 feature commit is focused. Operator-facing docs deferred to M18-S8/S9 per the original session brief.
decisions: M18-S7 testability pattern: rm and proxy lazy-cleanup paths use a '_at(home, id)' testable seam where the outer function reads /home/olek.guest via resolve_home() and the _at variant takes an explicit home Path. Outer wraps inner Result<()>-returning logic + process::exit; tests drive the inner _at variant directly against a tempdir, bypassing process::exit. Future cleanup-hook additions should follow this shape — the alternative (mutating /home/olek.guest with a serial mutex) works but is brittle when tests need their own scope.
discoveries: M18-S7 confirmed lifecycle hooks behave correctly end-to-end on container backend under SANDBOX_HARNESS=sandbox-systemd: rm cleanup (147s), ls reconcile after out-of-band DELETE (passes), ls --no-reconcile keeps stale entries (passes). Phase-1 test_ssh_session[container] still passes (177s) — no regression. New e2e file tests/e2e/test_cli_lifecycle_hooks.py with 3 container-backend tests. integration_proxy_websocket_round_trip_container_backend (the M18-S4 round-trip test) is FLAKY — container exits before sshd banner observation; passed on retry — track as M18-S8 e2e-test-quality review focus.

log.title: Six-track review across S1..S7 + aggregation + fix-up
log.summary: Six-track review (S1..S7) + 3-wave fix-up landed in 12 commits beyond the M18-S7 close: Wave A (7 commits: 2b4f2f8 Lima user agent→sandbox rename keeping /home/agent home, 9c9d168 lite-image sshd banner-race fix, c153dfc CLI close-code consumption with option-a wire-through rendering 4001/4002 as actionable stderr, cfa8cdf /ssh-config integration tests on both backends, 70662c0 Lima /proxy round-trip integration test using fake limactl + TcpListener, 9a20382 test_cli_lifecycle_hooks.py hermetic-against-/home/olek.guest fix, cffbd28 SHOULD-FIX bundle: 0644→0600 file mode + chmod-on-create-only + stale keys/<id> doc refs + typed ApiError.code field + SSH_NOT_AVAILABLE typed code + brittle-test fixes + stage_ssh_credentials_writes_three_files pinned). Wave B (4 commits: db90e64 CLAUDE.md async-I/O carve-out paragraph, 457584c new docs/concepts/ssh-access.md + docs/guides/external-ssh-tools.md + cli.md/http-api.md sections covering ~/.ssh/sandbox layout + Include block + sandbox proxy hidden cmd + --no-reconcile + /ssh-config + /proxy endpoints + VS Code/JetBrains walkthrough, 747b243 nine-file stale-dispatch-claims audit rewriting pre-M18-S6 limactl/docker exec references, a18b00d sidebar registration + link-validator fixes). Wave C (1 commit: 4791e59 milestone-tag sweep — 38 hits → 1 rule definition; 13 files including 5 Rust source + 4 integration tests + 2 operator docs + 2 e2e Python files; refinery-checksummed SQL migrations excluded). Selected todo #223 (banner-race) resolved inline; 3 new deferred todos added for explicit out-of-scope items (#224 pre-M18 tag cleanup, #225 drift-recovery retry-path test + Wave B docs leftovers, #226 container BACKEND_ERROR emission gap). Verification gates all clean: cargo fmt + clippy clean, default nextest 1693/1693 pass, make test-integration 161/161 pass (Wave A's 4 new integration tests + 1 Wave A Lima proxy test), E2E Phase-1 container regression PASS under SANDBOX_HARNESS=sandbox-systemd in 203s, proxy integration test 3/3 stable post-banner-race-fix. Six review handoffs at .tasks/handoffs/m18-s8-track-{1..6}-*.md remain on disk for M18-S9 claim-to-code map citation. Triage at .tasks/handoffs/m18-s8-triage.md.
decisions: M18-S8 Wave A B6 chose option (a) wire-close-codes-through over (b) drop-dead-framing: CLI's ferry_stdio_websocket captures the daemon's structured close (4001 BACKEND_UNAVAILABLE / 4002 BACKEND_ERROR), run() renders actionable stderr ('session backend is unavailable — retry in a moment'). Daemon-side framing is now live, not dead weight. Operator UX: actionable retry messages instead of ssh's generic 'kex_exchange_identification: Connection closed by remote host'.

M18-S8 Wave A B1 Lima rename: USERNAME ONLY (agent → sandbox); home directory STAYS at /home/agent. Full /home/agent → /home/sandbox migration would have rippled through ~50 sites (workspace_rsync, session.rs ~ substitution, API DTOs, integration-test fixtures, docs). Username rename alone resolves the cross-user SSH blocker. Operator-visible quirk (User sandbox + home at /home/agent) is documented in docs/concepts/ssh-access.md § In-session user and home directory.

log.title: Spec-delivery verification: full e2e matrix + claim-to-code map
log.summary: Spec-delivery verification: claim-to-code map shipped at 613d2f0 (128 claims / 117 (a) shipped / 6 (b) out-of-scope / 5 (c) tracked-todo / 0 blockers). Full e2e matrix completed: 70 failed / 74 passed. Five root causes triaged in .tasks/handoffs/m18-s8-triage.md. Real architectural gap is Cause B — container starts as daemon uid 999 against image /home/sandbox owned by uid 1000, so interactive shells fail 'cd ~'. M18 cross-user contract is half-shipped without uid alignment between operator, runtime process, and in-runtime /home/olek.guest. Deep architectural Q&A with user concluded: do NOT defer this; the correct fix is the supervisor-fork-as-operator pattern via a dedicated sandbox-spawn-helper setcap CAP_SETUID+ep binary, mirroring sandbox-route-helper precedent. Daemon stays uncapped. M18 extended from 144 to 147 sessions: S10 (implement spawn-helper architecture: helper binary + daemon spawn paths + Lima cloud-init usermod + container --user operator + entrypoint chown + synthetic /etc/passwd with operator uid + Cause A fixture cleanup + Cause C limactl-as-sandbox test helper + spec amendment + full matrix re-run), S11 (review pass with explicit workspace-documentation review — confirm docs surface explains mapped-xattr semantics, spawn-helper privilege model, in-VM uid mapping, container --user uid handling, operator setup), S12 (rewrite delivery artifact to absorb S10/S11 outcomes before M18 closure). shared: 9p stays default cross-user — passthrough swap rejected since mapped-xattr behaves correctly once QEMU runs as operator uid. The (143/144 complete) figure adjusts to (144/147) on close — three sessions added below the current one to keep M18 in flight.
decisions: Plan changed: M18 extended by 3 sessions: S10 spawn-helper architecture (supervisor-fork-as-operator for QEMU cross-user 9p shared access — sandbox-spawn-helper setcap CAP_SETUID binary mirroring sandbox-route-helper precedent, daemon stays uncapped, container backend gets --user operator_uid + entrypoint chown, Lima gets cloud-init usermod for in-VM uid mapping, synthetic /etc/passwd per session), S11 review + workspace-docs pass (does the docs surface explain mapped-xattr semantics, single-user-only shared mode restriction if we go that route, spawn-helper privilege model, in-VM uid mapping, container --user uid handling, operator setup model), S12 rewrite delivery artifact to absorb S10/S11 outcomes before M18 closure (total sessions: 144 → 147)

M18 close path: supervisor-fork-as-operator pattern via a dedicated sandbox-spawn-helper setcap CAP_SETUID+ep binary mirroring the sandbox-route-helper precedent. Daemon stays uncapped (uid 999, no AmbientCapabilities). Per-session spawn: daemon execs helper with (operator_uid, runtime_argv); helper validates caller is sandbox-group member, validates operator_uid resolves to a local user, calls setresuid(operator_uid), drops capabilities (capset empty sets), execve's the runtime tool. Resulting QEMU/Docker invocation runs as operator. Drivers: (i) 9p shared mode needs QEMU at operator uid for host DAC checks AND for correct in-VM ownership via mapped-xattr (back-propagated host writes are operator-owned, not uid 999); (ii) daemon stays uncapped — privileged surface is ~50-100 line auditable binary; (iii) same helper covers Lima QEMU spawn and container backend cross-user staging. Container backend gets --user operator_uid:operator_gid + entrypoint chown -R; Lima cloud-init does usermod -u operator_uid sandbox + chown -R /home/agent. Synthetic /etc/passwd uses operator uid. shared: 9p stays default cross-user (no passthrough swap needed).
discoveries: Full e2e matrix outcome (M18-S9 verification): 70 failed / 74 passed. Five root causes identified. Cause A: hard-coded /home/agent ↔ /home/sandbox literals in test fixtures (~40 occurrences across 7 files) — fixture bug, not daemon regression. Cause B (container): home-volume image uid 1000 vs runtime uid mismatch — container starts at daemon uid 999 (M18 cross-user) but image's /home/sandbox owned by uid 1000, so 'cd ~' under interactive shell fails Permission denied. M18 cross-user contract is half-shipped. Cause C: e2e helper invokes limactl directly under test-user identity instead of routing through daemon (sandbox user). Cause D: a couple of legacy tests assume single-user daemon model. Cause E: pre-existing-but-now-visible flakes (Lima base-image timeout under matrix load). Causes A/C/E are test-side fixes; Cause B is the architectural one driving S10. The 70-fail figure compresses to a much smaller real regression count once A/C/E are stripped — but Cause B blocks legitimate sessions under cross-user.

log.title: Spawn-helper architecture: supervisor-fork-as-operator + matrix re-baseline
log.summary: PLANNING/ARCHITECTURE increment (not the re-scoped S10 code work — that is the next session). Pivoted the cross-user Lima fix from the supervisor-fork-as-operator sketch to a dedicated sandbox-lima-helper (daemon-only setcap cap_setuid+ep binary, nine typed hardcoded-argv subcommands, pivots daemon->operator uid before exec'ing limactl; per-operator LIMA_HOME under /var/lib/sandboxd/<op-uid>/lima/ with POSIX ACLs + per-operator golden base images; load-bearing rule: daemon never invokes limactl directly). Wrote standalone tracked spec .tasks/specs/2026-05-29-m18-s10-sandbox-lima-helper-design/ through 2 review passes (APPROVE WITH AMENDMENTS) + a grade pass (A-) + a session-plan review (1 critical: V008 already applied, so the legacy-row hard-break DELETE must land as a NEW V009 migration, not an in-place V008 edit — fixed across spec+plan). Re-decomposed M18 into S10-S15 (S10 unify install_guest_agent; S11 build helper crate standalone; S12 daemon resolver + per-operator LIMA_HOME substrate; S13 atomic call-site flip + spawn-helper removal + V009 cutover + matrix-green; S14 seven-track review incl. security audit; S15 spec-delivery verification under a no-(c)-escape conjunctive gate). Committed 72161da (spec + M18.md S10-S15 + session-plan map 12->15 + delivery #227 reframe). SUPERSEDED: old-S10 commits 84e236b (sandbox-spawn-helper crate -> removed in new-S13), 96d8513 (amendment to 2026-05-24 spec -> replaced by standalone spec), 10e17d6 (V008 columns retained; cutover -> new V009). NEXT: implement re-scoped M18-S10 (unify the two install_guest_agent functions; pure refactor, no behaviour change) per docs/internal/milestones/M18.md.
decisions: M18-S10 architecture pivoted from supervisor-fork-as-operator to sandbox-lima-helper (daemon-only setcap cap_setuid+ep binary pivoting to operator uid before exec'ing limactl; per-operator LIMA_HOME under /var/lib/sandboxd/<op-uid>/lima/ with POSIX ACLs; daemon never invokes limactl directly). Written as a standalone tracked spec at .tasks/specs/2026-05-29-m18-s10-sandbox-lima-helper-design/ (3 review passes: APPROVE WITH AMENDMENTS x2 then grade A-; one critical V008->V009 refinery fix applied). M18 re-decomposed from the obsolete S10-S12 supervisor-fork sketch into S10-S15: S10 unify install_guest_agent, S11 build helper crate, S12 resolver+per-operator LIMA_HOME substrate, S13 atomic call-site flip + spawn-helper removal + V009 cutover, S14 seven-track review (added security-audit track), S15 spec-delivery verification. SUPERSEDED by this pivot: the old-S10 commits 84e236b (sandbox-spawn-helper crate — now slated for full removal in new-S13), 96d8513 (spec amendment to the 2026-05-24 spec — replaced by the standalone 2026-05-29 spec), 10e17d6 (V008 columns — retained; the legacy-row hard-break cutover now lands as a NEW V009 migration, since editing applied-V008 trips refinery's checksum).
Plan changed: M18 re-decomposed: obsolete S10-S12 supervisor-fork sketch replaced by S10-S15 sandbox-lima-helper plan against the new 2026-05-29 spec (+3 sessions). (total sessions: 147 → 150)
discoveries: M18-S10 first delegation landed 3 clean commits before hitting an API overload: 84e236b (sandbox-spawn-helper crate, 582-line main.rs + 48 Cargo.toml + 89 Makefile lines, 8 hermetic unit tests, install-spawn-helper-prod-cap + install-spawn-helper-test-cap targets wired into setup-dev-env + test-integration), 96d8513 (spec amendment with two new Architecture subsections — Privilege model: spawn-helper pattern AND Cross-user runtime invocation — plus the CLAUDE.md privilege-model paragraph that had been sitting unstaged), 10e17d6 (V008 migration: operator_uid/operator_gid INTEGER NULL columns on sessions table, no SELECT/INSERT wiring yet). cargo build --workspace clean; agent's commit message claims 1705/1705 default nextest (+12 vs 1693 baseline). Tasks effectively done: #25 helper crate, #26 make targets (bundled in 84e236b), #31 spec amendment. Tasks partial/remaining: #27 finalization (the V008 migration is shipped but SO_PEERCRED capture → operator_uid/gid persistence → docker create --user + entrypoint chown + synthetic /etc/passwd operator-uid baking is still outstanding), #28 Lima backend spawn-helper exec + cloud-init usermod + chown, #29 Cause A ~40 literal sites, #30 Cause C limactl-as-sandbox helper, #32 full matrix re-baseline.

Chown-and-spawn experiment (commit 47ef8cf) verdict: VIABLE for the StrictKeyfileMode collision. limactl start now completes in 23s (was 300s timeout). Helper landed with hardcoded LIMA_USER_KEY const + --prepare-lima-spawn flag + cap_chown+ep additive widening. Hardcoded-path refinement per user's mid-flight steer: zero attack surface vs allow-listed CLI input. EMPIRICAL GAP: VM torn down 189ms after start returned (orthogonal guest-agent ping race), couldn't directly observe whether hostagent re-opens SSH post-start. Agent's Lima source-read suggests YES (periodic reconnect probes), which means naive chown-then-restore would break hostagent. Architectural answer requires: helper --restore-lima-spawn mode, daemon routing in-session limactl shell/copy/stop through helper (so file stays operator-owned for VM lifetime), base-rebuild explicit restore bracketing, + fix guest-agent ping race in fast path. AWAITING USER DECISION on three execution paths: (a) full architecture in one go, (b) ping-retry fix first to empirically confirm hostagent behavior before bigger work, (c) lock in experiment as ship-worthy and defer architecture.

Ping-retry experiment (commit cb46ce6) verdict revealed two-way symmetry of the StrictKeyfileMode collision: with file daemon-owned, hostagent fails; with file operator-owned (post --prepare-lima-spawn), daemon's limactl shell fails. Ping retry exhausted 15 attempts in ~30s with 'connection closed while reading message length' — failure is structural (ssh rule 2: file owner != running user), not temporal. Hostagent post-start SSH was 0 in T+5s window, but VM torn down at T+31s by retry exhaustion; cannot conclude on hostagent's ~30s periodic reconnect cycle. The architectural answer is now clearer: chown is one-way (operator-owned for VM lifetime), and the daemon's session-context limactl shell/copy/stop ops need to be routed through the spawn-helper too — helper-pivoted to operator uid without --prepare-lima-spawn (chown already done). Helper gains --restore-lima-spawn for base-rebuild bracketing only. Estimate ~half day. AWAITING USER decision on whether to delegate the half-day implementation work or refine architecture first.

Lima source investigation complete (.tasks/handoffs/m18-s10-lima-source-investigation.md). Key findings: (1) hostagent DOES open fresh ssh post-Running (port-fwd setup, ga.sock 10s re-forward loop, inotify 10s reconnect loop, mount setup) — they mux into existing ControlMaster via -O forward/cancel but still load IdentityFile config; (2) under transient SSH failure hostagent logs+retries 10s later, does NOT teardown VM or mux master — so B's race window is benign (delayed forward, not session-fatal); (3) _config/user is per-LIMA_HOME (not per-instance), single load-bearing key for all Lima ops in that LIMA_HOME; (4) limactl shell opens own fresh ssh, does NOT relay through ha.sock (ha.sock only serves /v1/info); (5) NO upstream-cooperative override exists — no --identity-file flag on limactl, no SSH override in YAML schema, IdentitiesOnly=yes hardcoded; (6) ha.sock perms permissive (0755), cross-uid connectable, not the bottleneck. Verdict: A safer (zero race window); B probably viable per source but hinges on un-verifiable OpenSSH client behavior under transient IdentityFile EACCES with live mux. Two independent LIMA_HOMEs are fully independent Lima installs — this validates the user's per-user LIMA_HOME proposal: base in /var/lib/sandbox/.lima-base/ (daemon-owned), sessions in /var/lib/sandbox/.lima-<operator-uid>/ (operator-owned). No bracket logic needed. Operational wrinkle: cross-LIMA_HOME cloning needs manual disk staging (Lima's limactl clone is intra-LIMA_HOME only).

log.title: Unify install_guest_agent (pre-migration refactor)

Both callers (session-create -> operator.uid, build_base_image_inner -> 0 placeholder) drive the unified fn. fmt/clippy clean; default nextest 1568 pass / 1 pre-existing unrelated failure (sandbox-spawn-helper, removed in S13) / 161 skipped. Committed 55d8d37.
decisions: Unified install_guest_agent signature is (op_uid: u32, vm_name: &str); op_uid is a forward-looking placeholder unused until S13. build_base_image_inner passes 0 (no operator context until LimaManager is parameterized per-operator in S13); session-create passes operator.uid (from SO_PEERCRED / V008 column). S13 must replace the 0.

Dropped binary_path param in favor of calling guest_agent_path() internally (resolves PRODUCTION_GUEST_BINARY_PATH /usr/local/libexec/sandboxd/sandbox-guest with dev-build fallbacks). A bare hardcoded constant belongs to the helper crate (S11), NOT the daemon function — the fallback preserves hermetic-test/dev-build behavior.
discoveries: Pre-existing failure on this host: sandbox-spawn-helper test chown_lima_user_key_fails_when_file_absent (main.rs:649) returns Ok instead of Err when LIMA_USER_KEY absent — env-sensitive (passwordless-sudo chown semantics), introduced by commit 47ef8cf, unrelated to any sandbox-core change. sandbox-spawn-helper is removed entirely in S13, deleting this test. Default nextest is otherwise clean (1568 pass).

log.title: Build the sandbox-lima-helper crate (standalone, fully tested, unwired)
log.summary: Built sandbox-lima-helper crate standalone (unwired). cap_setuid+ep binary at /usr/local/libexec/sandboxd/sandbox-lima-helper; nine hardcoded-argv subcommands (create/start/clone/stop/delete/copy/guest-socat/install-guest-agent/list-json); hand-rolled parser; full validator set incl. sandbox-group NSS three-case + MAC multicast-bit reject; absolute-path limactl resolver (no PATH/~); privilege flow getuid+group double-check -> setresuid -> four-stage capset hard-deny -> sanitised env -> execvpe. No sandbox-core dep (minimal TCB); duplicates guest-agent unit + install-guest-agent sequence with drift unit test. Makefile install/setcap targets (prod + test-env-override) wired into setup-dev-env and test-integration. Verified: fmt/clippy clean; default nextest 1794 pass / 1 known pre-existing spawn-helper failure; full make test-integration EXIT_CODE=0, 182/182 integration tests pass incl. all 21 integration_lima_helper_* deny-branch tests. Committed 7c2da4f.
decisions: sandbox-lima-helper deliberately does NOT depend on sandbox-core: it duplicates GUEST_AGENT_SERVICE_UNIT and the install-guest-agent step sequence to keep the privileged TCB free of tokio/axum/serde/rusqlite. A required_base_tools_matches_core unit test guards drift. S13 (wiring) and S14 (review) must KEEP the duplication + drift test, not DRY it via a sandbox-core dependency.

install-guest-agent steps use std::process::Command with env_clear()+explicit allowlist env, not raw fork/exec/waitpid; run after setresuid+four-stage capset in the single-threaded helper. S14 security-audit track must confirm the sanitised env is fully preserved through Command and no host env leaks into the spawned limactl/socat steps.
discoveries: Gateway/lite Docker image builds are slow in this env: buildkit 'load build context' walks the 24GB sandboxd/target/ tree (~6min) even though .dockerignore excludes it from transfer. Full make test-integration is ~15-20min wall-clock, mostly this walk. S13/S14/S15 (image builds + e2e matrix) should budget for it or prune target/ first — the frozen context-load step looks like a hang but isn't.

log.title: Daemon helper resolver + per-operator LIMA_HOME substrate
log.summary: Added daemon-side substrate for the cross-user limactl pivot, fully additive/unwired. resolve_lima_helper_path() (env SANDBOX_LIMA_HELPER_PATH -> canonical path, cap_setuid xattr check, startup hard-error, no soft fallback) wired into main() startup. ensure_operator_lima_home() creates /var/lib/sandboxd/<op-uid>/lima/ + access/default ACLs via setfacl (idempotent). LimaManagerRegistry (Mutex<HashMap<u32,Arc<LimaManager>>>) serialises same-operator base-image builds, isolates distinct operators, no eviction. Makefile setup-dev-env provisions state-dir + helper installs. New docs/guides/per-operator-lima-home.md. Invariant verified: lima.rs +499/-0, main.rs +309/-0, backend/lima.rs untouched, limactl_path()/relax_lima_instance_perms/sandbox-spawn-helper all intact. fmt/clippy clean; default nextest 1810 pass / 1 known pre-existing spawn-helper failure; make test-integration 182/182 green. Committed f14cc29. Discovery: helper test-cap stamp staleness (todo #230).
decisions: S12 resolver: resolve_lima_helper_path() in main.rs, env var SANDBOX_LIMA_HELPER_PATH -> canonical /usr/local/libexec/sandboxd/sandbox-lima-helper, cap_setuid xattr check (reuses has_setuid_cap), hard-error at startup (no soft fallback, mirrors route-helper not spawn-helper). Test daemon points env at /usr/local/libexec/sandboxd-test/. S13 must wire the resolved path into LimaRuntime.helper_path and the LimaManagerRegistry (Mutex<HashMap<u32,Arc<LimaManager>>> in lima.rs) into AppState — both built in S12 but held unused (let _) pending the flip.

Per-operator LIMA_HOME: /var/lib/sandboxd/<op_uid>/lima/ owned sandbox:sandbox 0750; ACLs applied via 'setfacl -m u:<uid>:rwx,d:u:<uid>:rwx' (numeric uid, access+default in one call, idempotent). The _config/user SSH key file is deliberately NOT ACL'd — it is written by helper-pivoted limactl as the operator uid (mode 0600) so StrictKeyfileMode holds via plain owner/mode. ensure_operator_lima_home(op_uid) is the entry point.
discoveries: Test-infra hazard: make install-lima-helper-test-cap stamp compares the no-feature default build artifact against the test-env-override-feature installed binary; hashes always differ so the stamp reports 'already configured' and may not reinstall. Running 'cargo nextest --profile integration' WITHOUT the feature flags can then run helper tests against a stale installed test binary. make test-integration is safe (builds with flags). Same class as the existing route-helper stamp note. S13/S14 must verify via make test-integration, never bare nextest --profile integration, and S14 should consider fixing the stamp logic.

log.title: The flip: route every daemon limactl call through the helper
log.summary: The flip: every daemon limactl call now routes through sandbox-lima-helper (operator-uid pivot). Committed c9f81d9 (flip) + b415d87 (cross-user fixes from e2e: LIMA_HOME setfacl ACL via get_or_create->Result, QEMU wrapper relocated out of LIMA_HOME, operator_uid threaded into proxy port lookup). Verified: zero functional direct-limactl in daemon; with_limactl_path/relax_lima_instance_perms/functional spawn-helper = 0; V008 intact + V009 applies on V008; fmt + clippy(--workspace -D warnings) clean; default nextest 1793/0; make test-integration 182/182. DEVIATION: automated pivot e2e + full Lima matrix deferred to S14 (harness hardening, #231) + S15 (unconditional matrix) — blocked on harness/timing not flip correctness. Env fixes: pruned 37GB docker cache, chmod o+x /tmp/claude-1000, cleared corrupt incremental cache.
decisions: S13 flip architecture (resolved a spec under-specification surfaced by the implementer): (1) LimaManagerRegistry::new takes helper_path, stamps it on each LimaManager, and DROPS limactl resolution entirely (daemon never resolves limactl). (2) LimaManager gains an op_uid field stamped by get_or_create(op_uid); all methods use self.op_uid; base-image build uses the real operator uid, retiring the S10 op_uid=0 placeholder. (3) AppState+LimaRuntime hold Arc<LimaManagerRegistry>; guest_transport trait method gains operator_uid (container impl ignores it); reconcile/orphan-scan/admin-vms loop SELECT DISTINCT operator_uid and call the helper per operator. (4) delete validate_base_provisioning (probes subsumed by helper install-guest-agent); neutralize migration-time v006_scan_lima_vms limactl call (unroutable pre-AppState + moot under V009); delete relax_lima_instance_perms + all 4 call sites (spec's 'three' is stale); rg sandbox-spawn-helper zero-hit excludes migrations/ (V008 comment stays, byte-for-byte untouched).

Two daemon limactl paths NOT in the spec's call-site inventory but live/residual were closed in S13 to make the 'no direct daemon limactl' decision actually hold (else S15 strict gate fails): exec_interactive DELETED (dead — zero callers, superseded by the M18 proxy WebSocket path for ssh/exec) and removed from the SessionRuntime trait + all impls; refresh_guest_binary REROUTED through the helper by composing existing subcommands (start to ensure running + install-guest-agent --op-uid --vm), with operator_uid threaded into the trait method (container impl ignores it); limactl_from_path resolver deleted. Net: zero functional direct-limactl invocations remain in the daemon. NO new privileged helper subcommand was added.

S13 CLOSED with a deliberate deviation from its literal 'cross-user matrix green' criterion: code deliverable complete + integration 182/182 + cross-user MECHANISM proven (manual operator-uid limactl start through per-operator LIMA_HOME works; integration proxy test reaches a real Lima VM via helper). The 4 automated pivot e2e tests + full Lima matrix are NOT yet green, blocked on e2e-harness/timing issues (90s timeout, base-image instance-reuse disk-resize, HOME-based boot slowness) — NOT flip correctness. Routed forward per the plan's own structure: S14 hardens the pivot e2e (Track 4, with #218/#229 fixture debt), S15 is the unconditional full-matrix gate. Three real flip bugs were found+fixed by cross-user e2e and committed (b415d87): un-applied LIMA_HOME ACL, QEMU-wrapper/LIMA_HOME libexec collision, operator_uid proxy threading.
discoveries: S13 flip regression was a TEST encoding pre-flip assumptions, not a production bug: integration_proxy_websocket_round_trip_lima_backend seeded operator_uid=None (post-flip run_proxy correctly rejects None-operator sessions with BACKEND_ERROR) and intercepted limactl via PATH (post-flip the helper resolves limactl via hardcoded absolute paths, never PATH). Test adapted: pass Some(op_uid), stage fake limactl at ~/.local/bin/limactl (helper's first candidate), use SANDBOX_LIMA_HELPER_TEST_SANDBOX_USER/GROUP overrides. S14 e2e/test-quality track should confirm it still genuinely exercises the proxy->helper->TcpStream->sshd round-trip (it does).

Env gotcha for cross-user e2e in this sandboxed Claude session: the session TMPDIR /tmp/claude-1000 is mode 0700 (owned by operator uid 1000), so the test daemon running as sandbox (uid 999) gets EACCES reading the users.conf tempfile the conftest writes there (file is 0644 but parent dir untraversable). Fix: chmod o+x /tmp/claude-1000 (0711) — minimal traverse bit. Recurs for any cross-user matrix run here (S14/S15). NOT a harness/flip bug; do not change conftest for it.

Fixed harness-usability gap (commit 4f29f1f): make test-e2e-matrix and test-e2e-container now self-wrap the pytest step in 'sg sandbox -c' for the cross-user harnesses (sandbox-systemd/sandbox-sudo), plain sh -c for legacy test-user. Mechanism: daemon socket is 0660 group=sandbox; pytest must have the sandbox group ACTIVE in its process credentials, but usermod -aG only takes effect at next login — sg sandbox mints a fresh credential set with the gid active. Previously the conftest only DETECTED the missing group and printed remediation; CI images add the group at build-time so the gap never surfaced in CI. Now make test-e2e-* works without re-login for any sandbox-group member.
