Root-cause for why S13's cross-user matrix never went green (beyond the 3 test-side #231 blockers): the e2e cross-user harness (SANDBOX_HARNESS=sandbox-systemd, the DEFAULT) points the daemon at the TEST-cap'd helper /usr/local/libexec/sandboxd-test/sandbox-lima-helper (conftest.py:1057), but test-e2e-matrix (Makefile:121) does NOT depend on install-lima-helper-test-cap — so the cross-user Lima path ran against a missing/stale helper. Compounded by #230 stamp staleness (installer can report 'already configured' without reinstalling current binary). Dispatched background verify-gate agent af5ed439: STEP0 force-reinstall fresh test helpers; STEP1 make test-integration (canonical gate, Rust agent only ran nextest default); STEP2 add install-lima-helper-test-cap dep to test-e2e-matrix; STEP3 run pivot file alone under sg sandbox (3 tests pass, 2 9p tests skip since op_uid==1000); STEP4 full make test-e2e-matrix green; STEP5 commit Makefile fix. Monitoring for stall/CLARIFY.

ROOT CAUSE of cross-user Lima SSH hang (an S13 bug, commit b415d87): the per-operator LIMA_HOME setfacl (u:op:rwx,d:u:op:rwx) leaves a DEFAULT GROUP+OTHER ACL that propagates read bits into the operator SSH key _config/user, so it lands 0644 (-rw-r--r--+) regardless of umask. OpenSSH StrictKeyfileMode rejects the group/world-readable private key -> hostagent never authenticates -> 600s 'waiting for ssh' timeout -> base-image build fails. The 0f4eeab/3b3cf32 churn band-aided the same vector. Lead decision: fix at the ACL level in ensure_operator_lima_home (daemon/sandbox-core) — NOT cap_chown (spec Non-goal), NOT a timeout bump. Zero BOTH default-group and default-other in the ACL so files in the tree are operator-private 0600; operator access stays via the named-user default entry; daemon (dir owner, uid 999) and operator (named-user) both retain access, everyone else denied; daemon never reads the key directly (goes through helper). Keep helper umask 0o077 as defense-in-depth. Must be grounded in actual getfacl output + verified empirically (key 0600 op-owned, SSH up <600s) before the matrix.

Deeper root cause: OpenSSH ignores POSIX ACLs and reads st_mode. When _config/user inherits the default named-user ACL entry (default:user:op:rwx) from the per-operator LIMA_HOME, the kernel maintains an ACL MASK, and ls/stat show that mask in the GROUP bits. So the key reads 0640 (g+r) and ssh StrictKeyfileMode rejects it ('bad permissions') -> hostagent never auths -> 600s ssh-wait hang. Zeroing d:g::/d:o:: is insufficient while a named-user entry forces mask>=r. Fix direction: the operator OWNS everything it creates via the helper pivot (owner bits suffice); it only needs to TRAVERSE the daemon-created top dir. So use a NON-default u:op:rwx on the top LIMA_HOME dir for traversal and DROP the default named-user propagation (d:u:op), so child files (incl the SSH key) carry no named-user ACL, no mask, and land plain 0600. Verify no daemon-owned (uid 999) files live below the top dir that the operator must read; if any do, handle those narrowly. cap_chown remains a Non-goal; helper runs AS operator so it can chmod operator-owned files without it.

make test-integration = 182/182 PASSED with all S14 changes (canonical integration gate green). Cross-user pivot MECHANISM proven end-to-end: operator SSH key now plain 0600 operator-owned, SSH up 45s, golden base image builds within 600s, guest-agent path wired. Remaining pivot-file failures (5) are a pytest FIXTURE-ORCHESTRATION bug, NOT pivot correctness: the agent's manual base-image prewarm (a workaround for the now-fixed 600s SSH hang) runs, then the session fixture's _reset_sandbox_state_dir (which #231(b) made wipe the per-operator base VM) deletes the prewarmed image and the daemon is SIGTERM'd before test bodies -> sandbox create returns exit 2. Fix: REMOVE the manual prewarm (no longer needed; base builds in ~3min<600s), let conftest's normal session-scoped base-image fixture build once and keep the daemon up across tests.

CROSS-USER PIVOT FILE GREEN: test_cross_user_helper_pivot.py = 3 passed, 2 skipped in 327s (the 2 skips are op_uid==1000 9p mapped-xattr no-ops, correct). The #231 core goal is met: daemon-as-sandbox pivots to operator via sandbox-lima-helper, verified end-to-end (key ownership 0600, session reachability, per-operator LIMA_HOME). Root causes fixed this session: (1) ACL default named-user entry forced a mask that OpenSSH reads as group-read -> key rejected (fix: non-default ACL, key lands plain 0600); (2) guest-binary path test wiring; (3) test used non-existent create --wait flag. HOWEVER full make test-e2e-matrix shows BROAD failures on BOTH backends (test_presets npm/cargo/github, test_workspace cp, test_vm_lifecycle, policy_persistence[lima]) — pattern (both lima AND container, network/workspace/lifecycle subsystems) points to environmental/load or pre-existing preset flakes (known #39/#55/#37), NOT the Lima-only cross-user pivot. Container tests don't touch the helper. Needs triage: re-run failed subset in isolation (unloaded) to separate flakes/env from real S14 regressions per project guidance.

Full matrix = 90 failed / 57 passed / 2 skipped in 1h47m. Failure signatures (from matrix.log): dominant 'propagated=false ... actual=-' (policy never reaches gateway) + '500 Internal Server Error' (17) = gateway/policy-propagation subsystem degraded (long-known-flaky #37/#38/#40/#55); 'rsync error code 11' on workspace incl CONTAINER backend (where lima-helper/umask cannot be involved); test_policy_persistence::test_policy_survives_daemon_restart failing = known #218 (restart not adapted to systemd harness). Pattern = cascade/load/flake + legacy suite not cross-user-harness-adapted, on BOTH backends, after 2h on 8GB host — NOT S14 pivot regressions (pivot file + integration are GREEN; S14 commits touch only 6 files, none of the failing subsystems). Verifying via isolation re-run before concluding (not accepting agent's pre-existing claim on faith).

TRIAGE VERDICT (4 representative matrix failures, clean sequential isolation + source-diff proof): the 90 matrix failures are NOT cross-user pivot regressions. Breakdown: (1) CONTAINER failures (scp 'Connection closed'/socat pump exit137; npm-preset curl -w arg-relay rc=2) are in code byte-identical across b415d87..HEAD (container never uses lima-helper) -> PRE-EXISTING/ENVIRONMENTAL, reproduce in clean isolation (stable host issues, not flake/load). (2) Bulk of LIMA failures = TEST-HARNESS not adapted to per-operator LIMA_HOME: helpers like test_vm_lifecycle.py limactl_shell() run bare 'limactl shell <vm>' with no LIMA_HOME -> wrong registry; daemon side succeeds. This is #229-class harness-adaptation debt across the legacy suite (large). (3) ONE genuine in-scope M18 bug: daemon ssh-config/cp path reads old hardcoded /var/lib/sandbox/.lima/_config/user instead of per-operator /var/lib/sandboxd/<uid>/lima -> sandbox cp on Lima broken cross-user. Pivot file + integration GREEN; proxy-websocket (guest-socat via helper) works, but cp/ssh-config path was missed by the pivot. Baseline b415d87 run inconclusive (daemon rc=203 + on-disk DB already at v009) — source-diff proof used instead. Escalating scope decision to user.

Container in-VM home is /home/sandbox (lite Dockerfile useradd sandbox + /home/sandbox/workspace; container.rs mounts home volume at /home/sandbox, comment 'container: /home/sandbox, Lima: /home/agent'). Lima in-VM home is /home/agent. CROSS-BACKEND tests (test_workspace, test_git_remote, etc., parametrized [lima,container]) originally hardcoded /home/agent for BOTH -> container halves fail since the M18-S2 rename (this IS the #229 root, pre-existing since S2). Harness agent e97e9a2 flattened literals to LIMA_VM_HOME=/home/agent (preserved the container bug) and wrongly set CONTAINER_HOME=/home/agent. CORRECT fix: backend-aware in-VM home — guest_home(backend): lima->/home/agent, container->/home/sandbox; cross-backend tests must select by the backend fixture. Sending harness agent back to correct.

Round 1 measurement (isolation): GREEN now = test_cp_host_to_vm[lima] (read-user-key cp fix), test_level0_denied[lima] (so the 26 test_policy matrix fails were CASCADE under 2h load, not real bugs), git_push[lima], lite validation. TWO remaining root causes: (A) HARNESS limactl_cmd runs 'sudo -u sandbox limactl' but per-operator LIMA_HOME is owned by operator uid 1000 -> sandbox(999) gets permission-denied on lima.yaml. Fix: run limactl as the OPERATOR (test user, no sudo -u sandbox) with LIMA_HOME set — matches the proven S13 manual path; high-leverage (unblocks Lima tests that shell in to verify). (B) CONTAINER cp: 'scp: Connection closed' + container proxy socat exits 137 (SIGKILL) mid-transfer — container-backend cp/proxy bug, pre-existing (proxy source unchanged across range), reproduces in isolation. Round 2: fix A (conftest, edit-only) + investigate/fix B (container proxy).

Round 2: (A) DONE — conftest limactl_cmd + teardown limactl_argv_prefix now run limactl as the OPERATOR (env LIMA_HOME=OP_LIMA_HOME limactl, no sudo -u sandbox); matches proven manual path; unblocks Lima tests that shell in to verify. (B) container cp root cause FOUND: lite image sshd_config was MISSING 'Subsystem sftp' — modern scp uses SFTP protocol, so scp got 'Connection closed' and the socat pump died with 137 on teardown. Genuine pre-existing container bug (scp never worked on lite backend). Fix: add Subsystem sftp to lite image sshd_config + rebuild lite-image; verifying both container cp directions in isolation.

CORRECTION: my live 'failed=0' readings during matrix run 2 were a grep artifact — pytest ANSI color codes sit between the space and FAILED so 'grep " FAILED"' counted zero. ALWAYS strip ANSI (sed 's/\x1b\[[0-9;]*m//g') before counting. Actual matrix run 2 result: 56 failed / 91 passed / 2 skipped (1:54:53), DOWN from 90/57 pre-fix. Round 1+2 fixes worked where applied: test_workspace 17->3, test_git_remote 4->0, test_vm_lifecycle 2->0, test_guest_agent/policy_persistence ->0. STILL FAILING: test_policy 17 (likely cascade — passes in isolation), test_workspace_local 11 (no change — unaddressed sync path), test_presets 7 (curl -w arg-relay), test_lite 4, workspace_lock 3, networking 3, golden_image 3, shared_guest_path 2, discovery 2, hardening 1. Not green; continuing per user 'drive whole matrix green'.

Wave 3A landed: (Rust a099720) new sandbox-lima-helper 'run-rsync' subcommand pivots to operator uid and execvpe's rsync for the daemon initial local-workspace push — both backends (lima -e 'limactl shell', container -e 'docker exec -i'); fixes the change_dir-Permission-denied (~16 workspace_local/lock/shared tests); nextest 1817/1817, clippy/fmt clean. (Python) curl -w arg-relay fixed via shlex.quote on all 7 'sandbox ssh -- bash -c' callsites; lite stderr advisory stripped in 2 assertions. NOTE: cargo/github preset tests may still fail on real outbound network reachability (index.crates.io, github clone) — environmental, watch in next run.

Wave 3B classification (isolation, helper w/ run-rsync reinstalled): 3A CONFIRMED — workspace_local rsync FIXED both backends, lite-stderr FIXED, curl-arg-relay works (curl reaches host). Remaining classified: REAL CODE: #5/#8 lima 'sandbox ssh' UDP/socat relay drops stdin bytes (one root cause, breaks udp policy + denied_traffic); #3 mitmproxy CA not trusted in container/lite curl (arg-relay fix unmasked it); #9 QEMU not under sandbox.slice (likely helper uid-pivot dropped systemd-run --user cgroup placement). TEST/HARNESS: #7 golden_image hardcodes old base-image-meta.json path; #6 policy-L3 curl needs bash -c wrap like presets; #10 lite-restart opens daemon's private 0700 sessions.db directly; #11 daemon(999) can't traverse pytest 0700 /tmp/pytest-of-olek for container shared-mount (real home 0755 wouldn't hit it). The earlier policy '500s' were cascade — gone in isolation. Round 4a dispatched: Python test fixes (#6,#7,#10,#11) + Rust UDP relay (#5/#8); Round 4b: CA-trust #3 + cgroup #9.

Round 4a-py DONE (059aeeb): #7 golden_image now reads per-operator base-image-meta.json via OP_LIMA_HOME; #6 shlex.quote applied to 15 bash -c sites in test_policy (the 2>&1/pipe/format mangling, same class as presets); #10 lite orphan-restart test reads daemon db via 'sudo -u sandbox sqlite3' (was failing on 0700 db); #11 added _grant_traverse_to_sandbox() helper (chmod o+x ancestors up to /tmp) so daemon(999) can traverse pytest 0700 tmp to the shared-workspace host dir. Round 4a-udp (lima UDP) NOT converged — agent deep in Lima SLIRP interface Metric internals (248k tokens); hypothesis shifting from stdin-relay to UDP egress routing via wrong NIC. Reining in with time-box.

CONFIRMED: policy UDP failures were the shell-quoting bug, NOT SLIRP routing — test_udp_bidirectional_echo[lima] PASSES with 059aeeb (no Rust/product change). Redirecting the UDP agent off the routing hunt was correct. test_networking test_denied_traffic has the same bare-bash-c bug (059aeeb only covered test_policy+test_presets) — Python comprehensive sweep (a718389) now fixing test_networking + auditing all e2e files. Round 4b: #3 CA-trust (mitmproxy CA not trusted in lite container curl — preset TLS fail, was masked by the arg bug; likely pre-existing container gap) dispatched; #9 cgroup (QEMU not under sandbox.slice) to follow sequentially.

Quoting sweep complete (715d7f0): fixed 6 more sandbox-ssh-login-shell sites (test_networking:498 UDP /dev/udp send, test_discovery:136 ||true, test_policy_persistence:92 curl, test_lite 507/630/675 curl+echo-redirects). KEY insight: only 'sandbox ssh -- ' relays through a LOGIN SHELL (word-splits unquoted shell syntax); 'sandbox exec -- bash -c' (guest-agent execvp), 'limactl shell', and 'docker exec -- sh -c' do NOT split — safe. Final grep confirms no remaining unquoted shell-syntax via sandbox ssh. This should clear the quoting-class failures suite-wide (policy/networking/discovery/policy_persistence/lite curl). Awaiting CA-trust (a2f8531) then #9 cgroup, then final matrix.

CA-trust #3 FIXED+verified: lite sshd had UsePAM no, so the docker --env CURL_CA_BUNDLE (set on container init) never reached sshd session children and PAM never read /etc/environment -> curl in-session didn't trust the mitmproxy CA. Fix: SetEnv directives baked into lite image sshd_config (OpenSSH 8.0+, injects CA env into every session regardless of PAM). test_npm_preset_allows_npm_install[container] PASSED. Pre-existing, unmasked by the arg-relay fix. Requires lite-image rebuild (matrix stamp handles). REMAINING known-unfixed: #9 cgroup (test_cgroup_limits: QEMU not under sandbox.slice in operator --user manager — likely helper uid-pivot dropped systemd-run --user scope placement; investigate product-vs-harness). All other categories fixed: rsync a099720, test-bugs 4524894, quoting 059aeeb+715d7f0, golden/lite-db/traverse 059aeeb, CA-trust now. cargo/github presets may still fail on network reachability (env, watch in matrix).

#9 cgroup ROOT CAUSE = real M18 regression: helper's sanitized env block dropped XDG_RUNTIME_DIR, so the QEMU wrapper's 'systemctl --user show-environment' probe fails ('No medium found') -> wrapper falls to the no-limits branch -> session VMs launch with NO systemd-run scope and NO cgroup CPU/mem limits. Same family as the HOME-drop. Fix: set XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/run/user/<op_uid> in build_env_block (deterministic from op_uid, operator's own runtime dir, no host leak). Caveat: assumes operator has a user manager (linger/session) at /run/user/<uid> — test operator does; service-account operators without it would still skip limits (follow-up consideration). Agent implementing+verifying test_cgroup_limits.

#9 cgroup FIXED (74fd5bd): XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/run/user/<op_uid> in helper env block; test_cgroup_limits PASSED; nextest 1818/1818. ALL KNOWN matrix failure categories now fixed across Round 1-4. Commits: 70b892d/1b5e5b1 (S14 review fixes+HOME), e97e9a2/f7297fb/c47f572 (harness LIMA_HOME/backend-home/operator-uid), ae8d383 (cp read-user-key), 5fe0e40 (Makefile dep), 0871a3a (pivot test fixes), 748cf77 (lite sftp), a099720 (ws rsync pivot), 4524894 (curl-arg+lite-stderr), 059aeeb (golden-path/policy-quote/lite-db/traverse), 715d7f0 (quoting sweep), CA-trust commit, 74fd5bd (cgroup XDG), plus Makefile sg-wrap 4f29f1f + color da37ba2 + docs. Only wildcard: cargo/github presets depend on real outbound network reachability (crates.io/github via gateway) — env, will show in matrix. Launching confirming full matrix run 3 via make test-e2e-matrix (self-wraps sg + --color=no).

Matrix run 3 mid-run (64/149): 6 stragglers NOT covered by Round 1-4 fixes — test_golden_image x3 (rebuild_from_scratch, session_uses_clone_path, staleness_detection) STILL fail despite #7 per-operator-meta-path fix (so 059aeeb #7 was incomplete — these tests do more than read the meta path, likely limactl/clone ops); test_lite blocks_docker_in_docker (never addressed) + workspace_uid_alignment (despite #11 traverse-grant — incomplete); test_networking stop_start_with_networking[container] (never addressed). 'All categories fixed' was premature — matrix is the arbiter. Letting run finish for complete list, then Round 5 triage. cargo/github network presets still pending in back half.

golden_image x3 FIXED+verified by node-id (5a39312, test-only): two cross-user-harness gaps — (1) base-image-meta.json written 0600 sandbox:sandbox; LIMA_HOME default-ACL mask::--- strips ACE propagation so olek test process can't read/write it -> route meta I/O via sudo -u sandbox cat|tee|rm; (2) daemon logs go to journal under systemd (_stdout_log empty placeholder) -> read via journalctl -u sandboxd-test --since. All 3 PASS in isolation (212/214/284s). Next: container stragglers (lite blocks_docker_in_docker [never targeted], lite workspace_uid_alignment [#11 incomplete], networking stop_start_with_networking[container] [never targeted]) — verify each by node-id.

All 6 matrix3 stragglers FIXED+verified by node-id: golden_image x3 (5a39312); lite blocks_docker_in_docker (same sh -c quoting bug in harness assert_no_dind — product was correct), lite workspace_uid_alignment (#11 was incomplete: pytest tmp is GROUP-owned by sandbox, group bits checked before other for group members -> needed g+x not just o+x), networking stop_start[container] (hardcoded /home/agent -> guest_home(backend)). All test-harness. Now verifying the BACK-HALF previously-failing tests (matrix3 only reached ~91/149) that were fixed by-class/by-claim but not re-verified by node-id: vm_lifecycle, workspace, workspace_local, workspace_lock, shared_guest_path, cargo/github presets — isolation batch before the full-matrix gate, per the verify-by-node-id discipline.

Back-half verification: 7/8 PASS (cargo[container], github[container], shared_guest_path[container], shared_mount[container], workspace_local pull[container], workspace_lock blocks_stop, vm_lifecycle stop_start). 1 REAL PRODUCT straggler: test_shared_mount[lima] — limactl create (run as operator via helper) fails 'open /var/lib/sandboxd/1000/lima/sessions/<id>/template.yaml: permission denied'. Root: the ACL fix 0d5659e (dropped default named-user ACL to keep SSH key mask-free for OpenSSH) had a SIDE EFFECT — daemon(999)-created files in the per-operator LIMA_HOME are no longer operator(1000)-readable. The shared-mount Lima path writes template.yaml as the daemon then reads it as the operator via limactl. Container shared_mount passes (Lima-specific). Need: make daemon-created non-sensitive Lima session files operator-readable (e.g. 0644) WITHOUT regressing the 0600 no-mask SSH key. Dispatching Rust investigation+fix; verify by node-id. Then full matrix to completion (will catch any other ACL-side-effect paths).

shared_mount[lima] FIXED+verified (root cause: shared-mount uses slow create_vm path writing template.yaml into daemon-owned sessions/<id>/ which the ACL fix left operator-inaccessible; normal create uses clone_vm so no template file). Fix: write template as 0644 OUTSIDE LIMA_HOME, deleted after limactl create — no ACL change, SSH-key 0600/no-mask invariant intact (verified). shared_mount lima+container+create_and_destroy PASS, nextest 1818/1818, sibling audit clean. ALL KNOWN failures now fixed+verified by node-id across Rounds 1-5. Building daemon fresh + reinstalling test helper, then full matrix to COMPLETION as the final gate (no kill).

matrix4 fast-failed (149 errors/83s) = session-fixture base-image build failure, NOT a fix regression: transient host-DNS i/o timeout (127.0.0.53) on limactl's last-modified HEAD check during base rebuild -> 'open sandbox-test-base/image: no such file' fatal. Root: transient systemd-resolved blip under image-build load; DNS stable now (3/3), cache intact, base dir clean. The 2 host nft rules are benign Lima LIMADNS (VM SLIRP DNS 192.168.5.3->resolver, not host DNS). Base build works reliably in isolation (create_and_destroy + golden_image rebuild passed recently). Retrying full matrix (matrix5). If base build fast-fails again, will pre-warm + investigate limactl digest-less-cache behavior.

matrix5 base built OK (DNS stable). At 64/149: 62 passed, 2 failed = test_gateway_traffic_flow[lima]+[container] (the 'errors=1' was a false-grep on a non-result ERROR string). gateway_traffic_flow PASSED in run2 AND matrix3, fails only in matrix5 — and it does NOT use send_cmd (line 494, that's denied_traffic) or any line I edited; nothing in Rounds 3-5 deterministically affects both-backend gateway flow (lite-image/cgroup/Lima-helper changes are backend-specific). Pattern = likely FLAKE in the gateway/DNS-propagation pipeline (known-flaky #39/#55/#37), possibly same transient-DNS class as the base-build blip. Will confirm by isolation re-run (flake->passes alone). Letting matrix5 finish for the complete failure list, then triage each by node-id.

Real product bug found+fixed in container --repo clone: daemon hardcoded clone target '/home/agent/workspace/' (Lima home) for the container/lite branch too, but container home is '/home/sandbox' on a --read-only rootfs -> clone failed. Fixed main.rs container branch to '/home/sandbox/workspace/'; Lima branch stays '/home/agent/workspace/'. Backend-split literals, no shared home constant.

install-e2e on this bg host (nested-virt, 8GB, run as olek.guest under ~/.lima) fails 6/13 with ONE shared root cause: /run/sandbox/sandboxd.sock does not appear within 60s after 'systemctl enable --now sandboxd' even though wait_for_systemd_active passes (daemon active but socket-bind lags >60s under nested-virt load). PROVEN not a regression from the home-unification: commit 2177ce2 touches zero service/install.sh/systemd/socket files (main.rs diff is pure home-path strings), and the same socket-wait fails peercred-isolation tests which cannot be home-related. Environmental/pre-existing fixture-timeout, not code.

REAL M18 release-integration regression caught by install-e2e: daemon hard-requires sandbox-lima-helper (cap_setuid+ep) at /usr/local/libexec/sandboxd/ on startup, but the helper is NOT packaged or installed by ANY install path. Missing from: build-local-tarball.sh (build loop+staging+manifest), .github/workflows/release.yml (PRODUCTION release - install+manifest), scripts/install.sh (install+setcap step), scripts/uninstall.sh (removal). So fresh installs AND real shipped releases produce a daemon that crash-loops on startup (exit 1) and never binds its socket. Not a timing/ordering issue; not caused by home-unification. Cap string: cap_setuid+ep (matches Makefile install-lima-helper-prod-cap).

getcap on libcap 2.66 (target distros incl. Ubuntu 22.04 install-e2e VMs) renders 'setcap cap_setuid+ep' as 'cap_setuid=ep' (= not +). install.sh setcap verification must normalize +/= or accept both, else exact-match dies despite success. Route-helper unaffected (already uses =eip syntax).

Pre-existing test bug surfaced by the all-tests re-run (NOT my regression — signature byte-identical pre/post my batch, get_ssh_config + this test file untouched by my commits): integration_get_ssh_config_lima_backend passed owner_uid into the guest_proto slot and None for operator_uid, so the daemon (correctly) 500'd 'Lima session has no operator_uid'. Product behavior is correct (cross-user Lima ssh-config needs operator_uid to locate the per-operator key); fixed the test call to guest_proto=0, operator_uid=Some(owner_uid), operator_gid=Some(gid). Likely broke at the M18-S13 V009 cutover (operator_uid hard-check), pre-this-session. Re-running full integration to confirm + surface any other fail-fast-hidden failures.

log.title: Spec-delivery verification + M18 closure
log.summary: M18-S15 spec-delivery verification complete; M18 CLOSED. Claim-to-code delivery map committed (5bf6b44): 154 claims -> 140 (a) + 14 (b) + 0 (c) + 0 blockers (no-(c)-escape gate). Lone BLOCKER was a stale spec example (setfacl default named-user ACL d:u:<op>:rwx breaks OpenSSH StrictKeyfileMode via the POSIX mask) — impl was correct, spec amended in-branch. Audited load-bearing claim: zero limactl invocations outside the helper crate. Non-goals 13/13 absent; open-questions empty. Gates green: e2e matrix 147/0/2, install-e2e 33/33, integration 182/182, nextest 1822, clippy -D warnings + fmt. M18 flipped completed in both plan files. Terminal milestone for the 2026-05-24 + 2026-05-29 specs. 12 commits b9bcdc2..5bf6b44, local/unpushed.
discoveries: S15 claim-to-code map produced (139 claims: 123(a)/14(b)/0(c)/1 BLOCKER). BLOCKER root cause: spec's setfacl example prescribes d:u:<op>:rwx (default named-user ACL) but code uses u:{op}:rwx,d:g::---,d:o::--- (lima.rs:252) — code is CORRECT (default named-user ACL forces mask into group bits -> 0640 key -> OpenSSH StrictKeyfileMode rejects -> 600s hang; this was the M18-S13/S14 fix). Resolution: SPEC is stale/wrong; amend the setfacl example + Privilege-model/LIMA_HOME prose to match the correct impl with the OpenSSH rationale (sanctioned spec-amendment per BLOCKER->(b) lifecycle; delta 'fixed in-branch' = fix the spec, NOT the code which would reintroduce the bug). Also: impl ships 2 extra subcommands (read-user-key, run-rsync) beyond spec's 9 - forward work, not blockers.

log.title: Faithful cross-uid usermod e2e test (sandbox-e2e-test operator)
log.summary: Replaced the 2 vacuous op_uid==1000-skipped 9p tests with one faithful distinct-operator usermod-realignment e2e test. Operator = dedicated sandbox-e2e-test (uid 4099, != daemon 999, != baked 1000) provisioned ONCE at setup: make setup-e2e-test-operator (explicit WARNING, sandbox-group, passwordless sudoers grant for sudo -n -u; wired into setup-dev-env), removed by make clean (userdel + sudoers-rm). No provisioning sudo in the test body. conftest: operator added to the daemon users.conf allow_users (subnet allocation) + e2e_test_operator fixture (missing-prereq skip only, never op_uid==1000). Test asserts in-VM 'id -u sandbox'==4099 (realignment fired) + host-side 9p file st_uid==4099. Two issues found+fixed during verify-by-node-id: (1) subnet allocation needed the operator in allow_users; (2) shared dir had to be operator-traversable (/tmp 0777, not runner's 0700 pytest tmp) and the operator-owned file is checked via stat (st_uid+st_size), not read_text (runner can't read a 4099-owned 0600 file). PARALLEL=N broken in E2E tests: sandbox-base Lima VM is a single user-global resource (~/.lima/sandbox-base, BASE_VM_NAME hardcoded), so each pytest-xdist worker's _ensure_base_image fixture races on concurrent rebuild-image. Fix: filelock on base image path, skip rebuild when fresh. Also audit Docker subnet pool contention and concurrent gateway nftables injection.

Add nft -c validation for compiled nftables output in an integration test (catches syntax errors before E2E). Requires privileged container; hook into sandbox-core::gateway_integration.

Replace string-fragment assertions in PolicyCompiler unit tests with exact-shape or parser-validated checks (e.g. parse compiled nftables with nft -c, Envoy config with envoy --mode validate, mitmproxy JSON against a schema). Add apply_policy-through-real-gateway integration test in sandbox-core::gateway_integration. Covers compile → inject → nft/envoy/coredns acceptance. Catches syntax bugs, addon/daemon wire drift, and silent-failure swallowing before E2E.

Move LISTENER_HOST_ROOT from /tmp/sandboxd-listeners to $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/sandboxd/listeners/ (fallback ~/.local/share/sandboxd/listeners/); optionally expose SANDBOX_LISTENER_DIR override. Convention alignment with socket-path precedent (CLAUDE.md).

Wrap PolicyDistributor::distribute call sites in tokio::task::spawn_blocking (pre-existing violation of CLAUDE.md 'all std::process::Command in async handlers wrapped in spawn_blocking'). Now also covers std::fs::rename via AtomicListenerWriter. Not M9-S18 scope but worth a dedicated follow-up.

Harden test_gateway_lds_listener_and_atomic_rewrite: also observe listener_manager.listener_create_success and total_listeners_active across rewrite to directly assert 'no drain, no recreate' (stronger than gateway_status()==Healthy). Consider /stats?format=json for robustness over text-parsing.

macOS/Lima cross-host bind-mount inotify MovedTo semantics for the Envoy listener file are unverified — tests only run on Linux. If macOS host support matters, add a platform-portability note or test for listener-file rename events across virtiofs/sshfs.
make docs-build regenerates site/package-lock.json as a side effect (uncommitted diff after every build). Make the build reproducible or .gitignore the churn.

Envoy version-bump gate: add comment to networking/gateway/Dockerfile pointing at .tasks/verification/20260420-connect-tunneling/run-verification.sh — rerun before trusting %DOWNSTREAM_LOCAL_ADDRESS% on a new Envoy version.

Defense-in-depth against a silent mitmproxy-log format regression. Out of M9-S20 scope per explicit agent-C deferral.

Spec correction: amend 2026-04-21-...-design.md Part 3 'Liveness posture' section - deny-logger healthcheck probes GATEWAY_IP:10003 (bridge IP via $(hostname -i)), NOT 127.0.0.1:10003. Empirically verified in Phase 4: listener bound to bridge IP does not answer on loopback. Should ride into Phase 9 /review or a docs-only follow-up.

BUG M10-S3 Phase 8 discovery: UDP deny events carry post-DNAT destination (gateway_ip:10002) instead of pre-DNAT (user's real target). Root cause: IP_ORIGDSTADDR cmsg does not expose pre-DNAT destination for conntrack-DNAT'd UDP like TCP's SO_ORIGINAL_DST does. Fix path: add conntrack netlink lookup in deny-logger UDP handler. Blocks useful M10-S4 discovery workflow for UDP traffic (TCP is fine, works via SO_ORIGINAL_DST). Reproduction: Phase 8 test 2 in sandboxd/sandboxd/tests/m10_s3_end_to_end.rs which currently asserts post-DNAT tuple with explanatory comment.

M10-S3 I1 follow-up: pin Rust toolchain for deny-logger Dockerfile build. Options: (a) add rust-toolchain.toml at workspace root with channel='1.85.0', (b) pin Docker stage to specific rust:1.85-bookworm digest. Supply-chain hygiene + build reproducibility. Parallels the golang:1.22 pinning on CoreDNS builder.

M10-S3 I3 follow-up: surface flush_ticker panics in sandbox-deny-logger. Options: (a) include flush_ticker JoinHandle in main's tokio::select! so panic takes process down, (b) install process-level std::panic::set_hook that logs and aborts. Current design hides panics silently; liveness posture should propagate listener-task exits to process termination consistently.

M10-S3 I4 follow-up: extract transition-detection core of poll_and_emit_component_health into a pure helper in sandbox-core. Currently Phase 8 test 4 re-implements ~150 lines of monitor-loop logic inline with a 500ms poll override and forced last_healthy reset. Shared helper removes double-maintenance risk when production monitor evolves (extra component, transition predicate, restart-gated policy reapply). Non-blocking - current structure is a pragmatic trade-off with good explanatory comments.

Flaky: sandbox-deny-logger tcp::tests::tcp_respects_concurrency_cap fails under load (passed in isolation + on re-run). Likely a timing/ordering bug in the concurrency-cap test setup. Investigate and either stabilize (stricter barriers, longer sleeps, #[ignore] with explicit rationale, or redesign) or pin as known-flaky. Pre-existing from M10-S3 Phase 3.

daemon: propagate git clone failure from create --repo handler. Currently sandboxd/src/main.rs:805-820 logs a warning but returns CREATED even when the in-guest clone exited non-zero. Session ends up Running with empty /home/agent/workspace — test_m5_workspace::test_clone_repo observes this under full-suite load. Either fail the create call with SessionError::CloneFailed or leave session in a distinct Degraded state. Pre-existing since 2fd260c; surfaced during M10-S4 full E2E run.

M10-S6 docs sweep: spec text uses 'sandbox start' for session creation; implementation uses 'sandbox create'. Reconcile across spec + user-facing docs. Not a code change (flags land on create in M10-S5).

M10-S5 Phase 5a' Option B: live cargo fetch trace in a sandbox VM to empirically verify the cargo preset host list. Agent chose Option A (documented endpoints) to save the 30+ min VM boot cost. Fixture at sandbox-cli/tests/fixtures/cargo_fetch_trace.json currently matches documented endpoints; spec cites 'verified against documented endpoints'. Live verification can update the fixture if the observed host set differs. Non-blocking.

M10-S6: introduce durable fix for policy propagation cold-boot race. M10-S5 band-aided with POLICY_PROPAGATION_S=15s. Real fix: either sandbox policy status --wait CLI subcommand + daemon endpoint reporting 'all rules propagated to all enforcement layers', or per-batch 'propagated' confirmation logs in sandbox_core::dns_propagation. Cold-boot race currently fires on first E2E run after make gateway-image; runs 2+ pass. Not blocking but fragile.

M11: durable Envoy LDS/listener-ack in PropagationState. sandboxd/src/main.rs:2235 mark_propagated flips when listener YAML write returns, NOT when Envoy acks the new config. M10-S6 E2E surfaced: cargo curl Connection refused 53ms after --wait returns propagated=true, because cycle-2 listener rewrite may still be in flight. Fix: poll Envoy admin /config_dump (or /stats last_update_duration) for listener snapshot version before flipping propagated. Handoff 20260423-m10-s6-e2e-regression.md hypothesis 1.

M11: stabilize test_github_repo_preset_scopes_to_one_repo. Pre-existing ~80%-pass-rate flake. DNS rotation (github.com → rotating IPs 140.82.112.x/121.x/113.x/114.x) vs nftables allow-set causes torvalds/linux fetch to land on IP not in the github-repo preset set → deny at L3 (deny-logger) instead of L7 (mitmproxy) where the assertion expects. Handoff 20260423-m10-s6-e2e-regression.md failure 2 + prior 20260423-070500-* + 20260423-074500-*. Fix path: expand allow-set with suffix/CIDR matching for github.com pool, or add rule-level 'expand to common pool' semantics to the github-repo preset. Non-blocking for now.

Investigate real multi-host policy propagation race: curl TCP-RST within ~100ms post-propagated=true on presets with >1 host. Envoy listener_manager.lds.update_success stays at 0 (filesystem-watch no-op on content-identical rewrites). Candidates: (a) Envoy cluster STRICT_DNS endpoint resolution lag, (b) nftables set enforcement delay after add-element, (c) Envoy worker-thread filter-chain swap not atomic. See progress log discovery (post-fix rerun analysis) and .tasks/handoffs/20260423-m10-s6-e2e-regression.md. M11-S1 work — blocks M10-close.

M11+: Git LFS preset (github-lfs / lfs=true on github-repo and github-pr). Spec 'Known gaps' bullet. No partial leak in M10 tree; file when user hits it. Files: sandboxd/sandbox-cli/src/presets/builtin.rs (new entry).

M11-S1: user-preset versioning scheme. Spec 'Known gaps' calls out that user-authored presets need a version field so schema evolution doesn't silently break. No code yet; design-review task. Files: sandboxd/sandbox-cli/src/presets/ (user preset loader + schema).

M11+: daemon-level (non-session-scoped) lifecycle events endpoint. Spec 'Known gaps' item — currently events are per-session; operator-level events (daemon up/down, gateway-image-rebuild, CA-rotation) have no stream. Surface: GET /events on the daemon socket. Non-blocking.
Add Python-version sentinel to tests/e2e/.venv/.installed Makefile rule so venv rebuilds when interpreter version changes (e.g., embed python-X.Y in stamp filename).
Add clap/env to sandboxd's direct deps so 'cargo check -p sandboxd' works in isolation. Currently builds only via workspace feature unification through sandbox-deny-logger.

Integration test for #16: trigger a real PolicyDistributor::distribute failure end-to-end (syntactically-valid policy the gateway rejects) to exercise the fatal-failure path through the full create flow. Hermetic unit test currently pins the contract; this would catch regressions in the trigger path itself.
EVENTS_HOST_ROOT (/tmp/sandboxd-events) is the next candidate for XDG migration (same pattern as #20 listener path). Forward-pointing doc comment left in events/mod.rs for the next reader.

cargo fmt drift on m10-s8-polish-sweep: atomic_listener_writer.rs, main.rs, m10_s3_end_to_end.rs need fmt — accumulated across Groups 2-3 commits. Fold into the final session-close cleanup commit before opening a PR.

Investigate LDS in-place vs warm-restart-with-drain mismatch: design comment at sandbox-core/src/policy.rs:1158 claims filter-chain-only rewrites don't drain, but runtime shows listener_modified ticks + total_listeners_draining 0→1 even on valid-→valid rewrites. Hypothesis: Envoy in-place path applies only to changes within filter_chains: (named match list), not default_filter_chain: (catch-all). PolicyCompiler currently emits the latter for L1 passthrough. Either fix the compiler to use filter_chains: where in-place semantics matter, or correct the design comment. Discovered during Group 5 #22 investigation; full evidence in handoff 20260425-101213-m10-s8-group5-lds-test-hardening.md.

Gateway health check witnesses processes running, not an active listener. integration_gateway_lds_listener_and_atomic_rewrite shows gateway_status() == Healthy holds even when bootstrap listener is rejected by Envoy (total_listeners_active == 0). Health check should require at least one active listener after first successful LDS update. Discovered during Group 5 #22 investigation.

M10-S8 Group 5 follow-up: investigate in-place-vs-drain mismatch — design comment at sandbox-core/src/policy.rs:1158 says filter-chain-only rewrite is in-place; runtime stats show warm-restart-with-drain (listener_modified ticks, total_listeners_draining 0→1) for the test's emit shape (uses default_filter_chain:). Verify whether populated filter_chains: shape behaves the same. If yes, connection preservation across rule edits is not actually achieved.

M10-S8 Group 5 follow-up: gateway health check should witness an active listener, not just running processes. Current Healthy state passes when bootstrap listener is rejected by Envoy (total_listeners_active=0, lds.update_rejected=1). Production risk: a policy-compiled listener rejected at runtime would still report Healthy in GatewayManager.

Symmetry follow-up to #34: boot_cmd block in create_session (sandboxd/sandboxd/src/main.rs ~line 924-980) still has the warn-and-continue pattern for guest exec failures. If --boot-cmd fails, session ends up Running anyway. Consider extending fail_explicit_repo_clone-style fatal-failure path to boot_cmd for symmetry. Out of scope for #34 (no failing E2E).
M10-S5: pytest cleanup deletes session events on remove — set SANDBOX_KEEP_SESSION_EVENTS or test-side preservation flag for E2E debugging (mitmproxy.jsonl + deny-logger.jsonl)

M11: close the DNS-rotation propagation race architecturally. CoreDNS resolves new IP and answers VM in <1ms, but sandboxd dns_propagation_loop polls resolved.json every 2s — VM's TCP arrives at gateway nft set before the IP is admitted, deny-logger RSTs. Phase 3's CARGO_FASTLY_CIDR_POOL (9d1dca7) and #39 GITHUB_INTERACTIVE_CIDR_POOL (874a9bb) work around this for known CDN supernets but don't generalize. Two viable architectural fixes: (C) inline nft add element from sandboxpolicy CoreDNS plugin's WriteMsg interceptor BEFORE returning DNS answer to VM (race-free, cross-language coordination — Go plugin writes nft state managed by Rust daemon, risk of divergence with rebuild-from-cache model); (D) synchronous gating where CoreDNS plugin holds DNS response until sandboxd acks nft update (file/fifo barrier, adds DNS latency but architecturally clean). Both require redesigning the propagation contract; out of scope for polish session. Investigation handoff in M10-S8 progress log.

Lima SSH essential-requirement startup timeout (~22s default) caused a 1-in-3 E2E flake during M10-S8 Group 7 verification on 8GB host. If pattern recurs across delegations, tune sandbox-core::lima ssh.essentialRequirement retry budget upward (~45s) so VM has headroom under post-teardown IO pressure. Single occurrence so far — file only after second sighting. Evidence: .tasks/handoffs/20260425-135200-m10-s8-group7-e2e-lima-ssh-flake.md run 2.

nft set-injection duplicate-element noise: when two allowed hosts share an IP (e.g. archive.ubuntu.com + security.ubuntu.com both resolving to 91.189.91.81), nft batch logs 'File exists' for the second add even though policy still propagates correctly. Cleanup options: (a) dedupe IPs before nft batch insertion, (b) use 'add element ... { ... }' with idempotent semantics. Low-priority log-noise item, orthogonal to correctness. Discovered during M10-S8 Group 10 live-trace VM run.

Promote live-cargo-trace methodology to an opt-in slow E2E test for periodic toolchain-bump re-verification. Current state: one-off harness was deleted post-todo-#36 verification; test_cargo_preset_allows_cargo_fetch remains as lighter ongoing coverage. Methodology: custom-policy VM + apt-install cargo + capture mitmproxy events filtered by RFC3339-ms timestamp marker between phases. Mark with pytest.mark.slow/manual gate so make test-e2e doesn't run it by default. Out of M10-S8 scope; useful when cargo upstream changes its sparse-protocol or registry topology.

Drift-detection unit test cargo_preset_matches_frozen_trace currently only compares hosts[].host strings against preset domains — does not catch drift in methods/purpose/notes fields. If a future cargo version introduces a new HTTP method or changes a host's role, the test stays green even though the fixture has rotted. Consider extending the assertion to compare methods sets too, or accept the gap as documentation-only and add a comment. Low-priority. Surfaced during M10-S8 Group 10 review.

Bump nix dep when 0.30+ exposes pidfd_open: drop libc::syscall fallback in sandbox-route-helper, drop direct libc dep, switch to wrapped form. Currently in-tree at sandbox-route-helper/src/main.rs. No urgency — syscall is stable.

Enrich NetworkManager::restore_from_infos error message to name the configured pool CIDR and offending session id alongside the offending subnet base. Currently surfaces only 'subnet X does not map to a valid block in the base range'. Out of scope for M11-S2 (would touch sandbox-core/src/network.rs which Phase 2C handoff explicitly forbids); good candidate for a small M11 follow-up before merge to main.

M11-S2 polish: lower RUST_LOG to warn (or unset) in tests/integration_users_conf_startup.rs to reduce stderr-pipe pressure on slow CI. Substring assertions are on eprintln output, not log output; info-level messages are unnecessary and risk pipe-fill if daemon emits chatty init logs. Trivial change, sandboxd/sandboxd/tests/integration_users_conf_startup.rs:67. From kio-reviewer pass.

M11-S2 polish: i32::try_from(raw).expect(...) instead of raw as i32 for pidfd_open return cast in sandbox-route-helper/src/main.rs:191-209. Documents the kernel-fd-in-i32-range assumption inline; cannot fire in practice since pidfd values are kernel-allocated fd numbers. From kio-reviewer pass — fold into the next route-helper touch (likely todo #60's nix bump).

M11-S2 polish: add // must come after stderr read comment near drop(tmp); in tests/integration_users_conf_startup.rs:103. Future refactors that move the stderr read could break the explicit-early-drop pattern silently. From kio-reviewer pass.

M11 commit disentanglement: working tree contains both M11-S1 (backend/, V005__session_backend_column.sql, session_backend column, sandbox-core/tests/migrations.rs, lima_integration backend tests) and M11-S2 (users_conf, sandbox-route-helper, daemon startup validation, install docs) uncommitted on master. M11-S1's session closed without committing; M11-S2 layered changes on top. Before merging to main, untangle into two commits (or a logical sequence) so attribution maps to the originating session. Hermetic + integration + full E2E gates all green on the combined working tree, so the bundle is verified — only the git layout is the concern. Files mixed across both sessions: sandboxd/Cargo.toml, sandboxd/Cargo.lock, sandboxd/sandbox-core/Cargo.toml, sandboxd/sandbox-core/src/lib.rs, sandboxd/sandboxd/Cargo.toml, sandboxd/sandboxd/src/main.rs.

Spec patch: .tasks/specs/2026-04-22-lite-mode-container-backend-design/2026-04-22-lite-mode-container-backend-design-spec.md § Hardening line 542 lists 'seccomp=default' which is INVALID Docker CLI syntax. Correct spelling is 'seccomp=builtin'. Implementation already uses builtin; spec text needs to match. Discovered in M11-S3 Phase 3A. Tiny edit; fold into M11 spec-cleanup pass before close.

M11-S3 Phase 3D: normalize cpus precision at HTTP boundary. BackendSpecific::Container { cpus: u32 } but spec § Resource defaults wants 1-decimal precision (0.8, 1.5). 3A mitigated with as f64 in resource_ceilings; the proper fix lives at the request-parsing layer in sandboxd/src/main.rs (or the type itself in sandbox-core/src/backend/capabilities.rs). Surface during 3D wiring.

Gap #5 (workspace bind plumbing for lite container): re-advertise WorkspaceMode::Shared in container capability matrix (sandbox-core/src/backend/container.rs:362) and thread workspace_host_path from SessionConfig.workspace_mode through to ContainerNetwork at sandboxd/src/main.rs:813. Currently empty() per M11-S3 review fix #2 (deferred). Test 3.9 (workspace UID alignment) in tests/e2e/test_lite.py is skipped with this reason. Schedule into M11-S5 alongside parametrization.

Cosmetic: 'sandbox describe/inspect' on a container session created without --cpus/--memory shows CPUs: 0, Memory: 0 MB (Phase 4D-pre sentinel display). Either flip SessionConfig.cpus/memory_mb to Option<u32> end-to-end, or render '0 (host-80% default)' for container backend in describe/inspect. Surface fix only; runtime behavior is correct.

Wire gateway container into container backend create_session: setup_session_networking equivalent for lite sessions (attach sandbox-gw-<id> to sandbox-net-<id>, point lite container DNS at gateway IP, integrate nft policy enforcement). Spec E2E orphan-cleanup test: kill daemon mid-create, restart, assert orphan reaped — Rust integration test landed in Phase 5B but the spec also calls for an E2E pytest equivalent. Defer to M11-S6 verification or a follow-on phase.

test_m3_networking.py agnostic refactor: 3 tests (gateway_traffic_flow, stop_start_with_networking, concurrent_sessions) currently skip in-body for container backend due to hardcoded Lima 10.209.x.x/28 subnet pool. Refactor to query gateway IP via 'sandbox info' instead of hardcoding subnet — would unlock these 3 tests for container backend. Out of M11-S5 Phase 5C scope per handoff constraint.

Provision self-hosted KVM-capable GitHub Actions runner with labels [self-hosted, kvm]. Until done, e2e-matrix queues on every push-to-main without scheduling. Reqs: KVM hardware/nested-virt, Docker, limactl, qemu-bridge-helper, 8 CPU/8GB floor. See .tasks/handoffs/20260427-051634-implementer-m11-s5-phase5d-runner-provisioning.md.

Wire nightly perf-benchmarks GitHub Actions job (spec § CI policy: 'Nightly | Matrix + perf benchmarks | longer'). Blocked on KVM-runner provisioning. Add schedule: trigger and reuse e2e-matrix scaffolding.

M11 polish: 'sandbox describe/inspect' on a container session created without --cpus/--memory currently shows 'CPUs: 0, Memory: 0 MB' (sentinel display). Either render the actual host-80% defaults the daemon would apply, OR omit the field entirely when no explicit value was set. Never show 0 — that misleads the reader into thinking the session has no CPU/memory budget. Connects to todo #67 (cpus precision normalization) since the display path needs to match whatever the HTTP boundary normalizes to.

Drop the leading underscore from SubnetAllocator._prefix_len in sandbox-core/src/network.rs. The field is now actively read by NetworkManager::restore_from_infos's enriched error message (todo #61); the underscore-as-unused marker is misleading post-edit. Trivial rename; fold into the next pass that touches network.rs.

M11-S7 follow-up: --boot-cmd symmetry on container backend. The Lima path runs bash -c <cmd> after the --repo clone; container branch still doesn't dispatch req.boot_cmd. Pattern is identical to the new repo-clone block (different state.guest.exec args, fail_explicit_boot_cmd instead of fail_explicit_repo_clone). Mirror the symmetry pattern that --repo just got. Out of original S7 scope; small follow-up.

Cosmetic: sandboxd/sandbox-cli/tests/integration_no_cache_rejection.rs:95 hand-rolls a fake /backends mock with workspace_modes: []. Spec-correct post-S7 value is ['shared', 'clone']. Test still passes (assertion is about --no-cache rejection, not workspace_modes). Opportunistic cleanup whenever someone next touches the file.

Pre-existing flake on master: integration_create_session_container_first_use_warning_surfaces fails in raw nextest runs because sandbox-core::lima::guest_agent_path() resolves via current_exe().parent().join('sandbox-guest') — under nextest, current_exe() is target/debug/deps/<test-binary>, not target/debug. Fix: search both target/debug and target/debug/../ for the binary, or fall back to the workspace-root canonical path. Affects local-nextest reliability; CI may or may not. Surface for next sandbox-guest provisioning touch.

Refactor: expose CONTAINER_CA_BUNDLE_PATH and home_volume_name(SessionId) via sandbox-core's public API. Today they are private to sandbox-core/src/backend/container.rs; the daemon (sandboxd/src/main.rs::session_mount_info_for) mirrors them verbatim because M11-S7 Bundle Y constraints forbade exposing new accessors. The mirror creates drift risk if either value changes. Either pub the constant + free function, or expose them through the SessionRuntime trait (alongside Capabilities) so the daemon never duplicates literals. The orphan-reaper's parse_home_volume_session_id test should catch volume-name drift but not constant drift.

Future: validate or warn on fractional --cpus for Lima sessions. Today sandboxd/src/main.rs:929 truncates 1.5 → 1 silently for Lima (Lima uses QEMU -smp integers). Either reject Lima fractional cpus at the daemon boundary with a 400, or surface a daemon warn! log + an operator-visible warning string in SessionDto.warnings. Surfaced by kio-reviewer over M11-S7.

Add gateway nftables-integration coverage for cross-session L4 isolation (container backend). M11-S7's e2e _probe_gateway_tcp can only verify positive reachability on container — gateway DNAT prerouting (sandbox-core/src/gateway.rs:1462-1486) rewrites every TCP/UDP packet from session A's saddr before it can leave the bridge, so cross-session 'A cannot reach B's gateway' is invisible at L4 from inside the session. The structural disjoint-subnet check in test_concurrent_sessions covers the architectural contract on both backends, but the runtime traffic-isolation property is currently only behaviourally tested on Lima (via ICMP). Add an in-tree integration test in sandbox-core that asserts: a packet from session A's vm_subnet targeting session B's vm_subnet is dropped by the forward chain. No spec change required.

Add fifth M11-S8 integration test exercising probe-Err(_) propagation through the daemon's create_session handler and out to the CLI. Currently the unit test (probe_against_path_stub_round_trip Phase 3) confirms DockerInfoBehavior::Fail surfaces as SandboxError::Gateway, but no integration test drives the HTTP path: stub set to Fail → daemon returns 500 → CLI relays the spec-shaped error body. Should be named integration_rootless_docker_probe_failure_surfaces_as_gateway_error and follow the same harness pattern as the four existing tests in sandboxd/sandboxd/tests/integration_rootless_docker.rs. Surfaced by kio-reviewer over M11-S8. Non-blocking; M11.md plan only listed 4 tests, so this is genuinely additional scope.

CIDR-scoped reaper: filter sandbox networks/containers/volumes by daemon's allocator pool (sandbox-core/src/network.rs:197 default 10.209.0.0/24) before reaping. Today the reaper trusts only the 'sandbox-' name prefix, so two daemons sharing a host (or a daemon + a misconfigured second instance, or dev tests using the production prefix) can mass-delete each other's resources. Defense-in-depth — name says 'sandboxd's', CIDR says 'this sandboxd's'. Implementation: extend DockerOps to fetch IPAM via 'docker network inspect' and filter networks whose IPv4 subnets fall outside the daemon's pool; containers + home volumes are owned by-network transitively, so out-of-pool networks gate them. New test surface: CIDR-mismatch fixtures asserting cross-daemon isolation. Separate session — has its own design + test scope.

Mode-arm coverage gap in sandbox-core::users_conf::validate_users_conf_security (line ~498-510). The world-writable/group-writable refusal arm is unreachable from non-root unit tests because the uid-0 arm fires first. Test defensive_check_refuses_world_writable_canonical_file asserts InsecureFile but cannot distinguish which arm fired. Fix: introduce validate_users_conf_security_with_meta(meta: &Metadata, ...) seam so a unit test can synthesize uid-0-but-mode-666 metadata. Surfaced by kio-reviewer over M11-S9. Non-blocking — current tests pass.

S2 scope expansion: UDP allow-path is structurally dead. nft DNATs to Envoy:10000 but Envoy has no UDP listener AND gateway input chain at gateway.rs:1547 rejects udp dport 10000. Pre-S2 design decision required: (a) add Envoy UDP listener, (b) reshape nft so allow-path UDP skips Envoy, or (c) explicitly remove dead path. M12-S2's 'fully working UDP at transport' bullet inherits this decision — see docs/internal/m12-s1-udp-audit.md sections b1/b2.

S2 doc reconciliation: networking-design.md:1395 claims 'UDP policy is enforced purely by nftables (IP/port allow/deny) with no userland proxy' but the deny path runs through the userland sandbox-deny-logger binary. Spec drift — fix wording or change implementation.
S2 spec/impl reconciliation: networking-design.md:1221 mandates ICMP unreachable for UDP rejects, but sandbox-deny-logger drops silently. Either spec or impl needs to give. Decide as part of M12-S2.

S9 candidate: no per-source UDP rate limit in sandbox-deny-logger. TCP path enforces a per-connection cap; UDP only has a per-process cap. A noisy sender from a single VM can starve the deny-event channel for other sessions.

S9 candidate: UDP recvmsg buffer fixed at 128B in sandbox-deny-logger silently truncates with no MSG_TRUNC accounting. Larger UDP datagrams lose payload bytes invisibly. Either bump buffer or surface MSG_TRUNC counts in deny events.
S9 candidate: http+udp policy validation is unit-tested only. No e2e/integration test boundary check that http+udp combinations are rejected at the daemon API level.

S9 candidate (links to S2 todo above): policy_allow_udp set populated by DNS propagation is dead-code scaffolding until allow-path is real. If S2 chooses 'remove dead path' option, this scaffolding should also be torn out. If S2 chooses 'fix allow-path', this is correct.

S9 candidate: wire CONNTRACK_LOOKUP_MISSES atomic into /health JSON shape next to events_emitted_60s. Today operators only see lookup misses via tracing::warn! log lines; numeric signal would be useful for alerting without log scraping. Low priority — warn is sufficient until production load shows non-zero misses.

S9 candidate: conntrack netlink recv buffer fixed at 4096 bytes. Adequate for IPv4/UDP entries but a CT entry with rich extension NLAs (mark, timeout, helper) could exceed it. Today surfaces as LookupError::Protocol (deny event still ships via post-DNAT fallback). Tighten with MSG_TRUNC accounting or dynamic alloc if real workloads ever hit it.

S9 candidate (review m3): in sandbox-deny-logger UDP receive loop, conntrack lookup runs unconditionally before rate_cap.try_admit(). Under a deny storm at >cap rate, every dropped datagram still pays the netlink send+recv cost. Cheap fix: invert order so lookup only runs on admitted datagrams (rate_limited summary doesn't need per-datagram tuples). Pairs naturally with #108 UDP rate-cap hardening.

S9 candidate (review m4): conntrack netlink Socket is in blocking mode (netlink_sys::Socket::new doesn't set SOCK_NONBLOCK) and lookup runs inside tokio UDP task without spawn_blocking. Microsecond latency in practice and UDP task is single-purpose, but technically deviates from CLAUDE.md async/blocking convention. Either (a) add a comment explaining why blocking is acceptable here, or (b) wrap socket in tokio::io::unix::AsyncFd (the impl AsRawFd is already there). conntrack.rs:236-258, udp.rs:107-141.

S9 candidate (review m5): iter_nlas in conntrack.rs:489-516 allocates Vec<(u16, &[u8])> per call, called 3-4x per kernel reply (top-level, IP sub-tuple, proto sub-tuple). Convert to lazy iterator (impl Iterator<Item = Result<(u16, &[u8]), LookupError>>) for zero-allocation walk. Tracking-only; not load-bearing today.

S9 nit (review m6): in sandbox-deny-logger (binary crate), lookup_misses() and reset_lookup_misses() are pub + #[allow(dead_code)] — pub is misleading because [[bin]] crates have no library surface. Either tighten to pub(crate) or add a TODO(M12-S2)/wire-to-/health comment so the lifecycle is visible to grep. conntrack.rs:169-172.

S2 follow-up: coordinated deny-logger.jsonl -> nft-deny.jsonl rename per spec Resolution 6 (entrypoint env-var default + binary clap default + sandbox-core watcher constant DENY_LOGGER_JSONL -- atomic single change before Phase 7 close)

S9 candidate: nft-loggers SIGTERM clean exit. spawn_blocking netlink-recv not woken by tokio cancel; relies on 10s SIGKILL escalation. Could be fixed with non-blocking socket + poll/cancel-token or signal-arm fd close. Cosmetic, low priority.

S9 candidate: Makefile auto-rebuild gateway image when sandboxd nft-loggers change. Stamp dependency from sandbox-nft-deny-logger + sandbox-nft-allow-logger build outputs to gateway-image stamp. Currently silent staleness can mask integration test correctness.

S5 candidate: drop unused hasECHParam helper from networking/coredns-plugin/strip.go. After M12-S3 strip-the-param rewrite, only containsECHKey is called in production. M12-S3 in-scope bullet explicitly preserved hasECHParam. Trivial cleanup for the reviewer-nit round-up.

LimaRuntime::ip should source IP from a per-session side map, not 'limactl shell'. sandbox-core/src/backend/lima.rs:303 currently shells out via 'limactl shell <vm> -- ip -4 addr show eth1' and parses the dotted-quad — requires VM running with eth1 up (neither holds during create / post-stop windows) and costs a limactl round-trip per call. ContainerRuntime (sandbox-core/src/backend/container.rs:245) already implements the right pattern: sessions: Mutex<HashMap<SessionId, ContainerNetwork>> populated by register_session before SessionRuntime::create, ip() returns from that map. Mirror it for Lima: add register_session(session_id, vm_ip) to LimaRuntime, daemon populates from NetworkInfo.vm_ip at create time, replace shell-out with side-map lookup. Original M11-S1 Phase 1C deferred work item (b).

Base image build silently accepts failed provision.system scripts. Lima's cc_scripts_per_boot allows non-zero exit without aborting cloud-init; the daemon's build_base_image_inner relies on 'limactl start' returning success, which doesn't check provisioning. Need to either grep the serial.log for all expected 'step=X done' markers post-start, or invoke a guest probe (e.g. 'command -v docker') to validate the image before declaring it golden. Without this, cloned VMs re-run provisioning on first boot and the same flakiness recurs per-session.
