Group D flagged in their M16-S4 report — none of the existing update e2e tests exercise the cosign verify-blob path because they all use --from <dir>.

integration_guest_refresh_container_backend flakes under full-suite parallel load (Docker container-lifecycle interference: docker cp fails with RWLayer-unexpectedly-nil or No-such-container, non-deterministic). Passes solo in ~16s. Candidate fixes: (a) retry/wait around docker cp at integration_guest_refresh.rs:358; (b) audit other integration tests for stale-name container cleanup races; (c) move into a serial nextest group.

E2E test_update_with_recreate_session_classification missing from tests/install-e2e/ — runtime-arm covered by integration_guest_refresh.rs in sandbox-core but no end-to-end test for sandbox update --dry-run's per-session 'recreate' classification path. CLI surface for per-session classification is wired (M16-S4 added --dump-proto-version + classify_stopped_sessions); the test gap is the only outstanding piece.

Code-side milestone-tag drift: sandboxd/sandbox-cli/src/update/mod.rs:187 has comment 'M16-S3 wires that' which violates CLAUDE.md's no-milestone-IDs-in-code rule. Strip the milestone tag — see if a wider audit catches similar tags via 'grep -rn -E "M1[0-6]-S[0-9]+" sandboxd/'.

Skip-on-match (idempotency shortcut) optimization in 'sandbox update --dry-run' renderer still hardcoded — every step renders 'would execute' regardless of pre-applied state (sandbox-cli/src/update/mod.rs:717). Affects operator-visible dry-run accuracy; not a correctness bug since the full update still does step-level skip checks at runtime.

Verify whether files created inside a VM end up owned by sandbox:sandbox on host when shared-workspace mount is in use; if uid/gid mapping is wrong, fix it. Likely points to investigate: Lima's mount config (idmap vs reverse-sshfs), 9p/virtiofs uid mapping, and the in-VM guest agent's umask/owner stamping when writing back into the shared mount.

Pre-existing latent flake: integration_create_session_container_* tests create sandbox-<sid> containers but are NOT in nextest's docker-sandbox-namespace test-group filter in sandboxd/.config/nextest.toml. Future tests in the same namespace will race the orphan-reaper. Audit the integration_create_session_container family and add them to the group filter.

integration_lite_image_build_concurrent_calls_serialize (sandbox-core/tests/integration_lite_image_build.rs:207) hangs indefinitely under memory-constrained VM (folio_wait_bit_common page-cache wait, no docker activity, no progress 25+ min). Pre-existing M11/M12-vintage test. Candidates: (a) add slow-timeout { terminate-after = 3 } to sandboxd/.config/nextest.toml for this test; (b) investigate whether ensure_image's container_image_lock mutex can deadlock when underlying docker build wedges.

M16-S4 triage MF27 + MF28: extend backup module test coverage with in-process apply_migration / atomic-write tests parallel to MF24 (cfg_migrations). Same pattern as dd58e36's apply_migration_in_memory triple, but in backup.rs with larger setup cost.

M16-S4 triage MF38: install-e2e Fedora coverage. Add distro_template='fedora-41' to update test parameterization; verify the chain of distro-conditional prereq paths (rpm vs deb, systemd unit deltas, etc.) on Fedora.
M16-S4 triage MF22 + MF23: route-helper integration tests that need root or user-namespace harness. Out of scope for unit-test pass — needs a dedicated privileged-test harness design.

Real correctness gap: rollback recipe at docs/guides/rollback.md step 7 restores only sessions.db.bak; commit 36530a9 (M16-S7) extended the backup set to include sessions.db-wal + sessions.db-shm but the rollback path doesn't restore them. Operators following the documented recipe lose in-flight transactions. Spec § 7.2 is also silent on WAL/SHM. Tri-way reconciliation needed: either (a) recipe + spec restore WAL/SHM (likely correct since WAL holds load-bearing in-flight tx), or (b) backup drops them. Probably (a). Touches: docs/guides/rollback.md, Spec 5 § 7.2 example block, and test_update_then_manual_rollback assertions.

M16-S4 triage MF44: Spec 5 § 7.2 example block is stale relative to docs/guides/rollback.md (the docs were updated to the sudo-wrapped glob shape during M16-S3 but the spec example block wasn't synced). Spec edit, not docs edit. Future spec-vs-docs reconciliation pass.

#151 daemon-side bug: bob's request via peercred-connector returns FIN+0bytes; 'sudo -u bob curl --unix-socket' returns correct 404; 'sudo -u bob sandbox ps' fails 'operation was canceled' on the 2nd keep-alive request. Alice works fine. Daemon journal silent. Full diagnosis + 3 hypotheses + reproduction shell scripts in .tasks/handoffs/20260516-153431-subagent-151-daemon-side-bob-request-bug.md. Investigation likely: tokio Unix socket peercred listener vs axum keep-alive vs hyper Connection:close+half-close handling. Real production-relevant bug — multi-operator workflows are partially broken.

Spec 2 § 4.1 daemon close-without-response: implementation closes via drop(stream) → kernel delivers RST when bytes are queued. Some operators/load balancers prefer graceful shutdown(SHUT_WR) then close (FIN). Surfaced during M16-S10 test #150 — assertion now accepts both arms (commit 4d8ae67). Spec says 'close the connection' without specifying RST vs FIN. Decide whether to pin one shape in the spec or keep both as acceptable behaviors.

Two integration_guest_refresh tests are Docker-race flaky under parallel nextest (integration_guest_binary_shared_inode_across_sessions, integration_guest_binary_swap_picked_up_by_new_sessions). Pre-existing; both pass on retry. Surfaced during M16-S10 libexec fix verification. Candidate fixes: small wait/retry on runtime.delete OR move to docker-sandbox-namespace group. Related to #173 and #178.

Replace Rekor's --rekor_server.signer=memory with persistent on-disk signer in tests/install-e2e/sigstore-stack/docker-compose.yml so the rekor signing key survives container restarts. Eliminates the bundle-staleness class of failures entirely (M16-S12 b04fbdc works around it at the test-fixture layer via mtime-tracked re-signing, but a persistent signer is the proper fix). Rekor supports file-based keys via --rekor_server.signer=<path>.

Investigate why sudo dd of= on Lima VMs hits EACCES even after sudo chmod 0666  (with sudo chmod itself succeeding). Workaround in M16-S12 commit 075c260 routes tampered-byte injection through limactl-cp from host. Possible cause: Lima overlay /tmp interaction with dd's open-flag combination (O_CREAT|O_RDWR vs O_WRONLY|O_TRUNC). Not actionable without a reproducer outside the test harness.

Drop the daemon-side guest-binary staging step (M16-S6 vestige): bind-mount /usr/local/libexec/sandboxd/sandbox-guest directly into containers instead of staging a copy at /var/lib/sandbox/guest/sandbox-guest. Remove stage_guest_binary_at (sandbox-core/src/guest.rs:211), STAGED_GUEST_FILE_RELPATH / STAGED_GUEST_SUBDIR constants, the guest/ subdir in ensure_base_dir_layout, and the daemon-startup staging call. Container backend's bind-mount source becomes guest_agent_path() output directly. sandbox update atomically renames the libexec file; live containers keep their old inode (same as today), new sessions see the new file. Tests' SANDBOXD_GUEST_BINARY_PATH env override already points the resolver at a built binary, so the test path becomes the bind-mount source directly — no harness changes needed. Update Spec 2 § 3.8.1 amendment to reflect the simpler design (the include_bytes! framing was abandoned in S10; staging was kept as an artifact). Rootless Docker concern (root:root 0755 libexec file would not be bind-mountable by a rootless daemon) is out of scope per M11-S8 contract.

M16-S4 triage MF12 and MF39 unaccounted for: every other MF number from .tasks/handoffs/m16-s4-triage-plan.md was either closed in an S7/S8/S9 commit (mentioned by name in the log) or rolled into a deferred todo (#180 for MF27+MF28, #181 for MF38, #182 for MF22+MF23, #184 for MF44). MF12 and MF39 are not referenced in any S6-S12 commit message and not in any deferred todo. Re-read the triage plan entries for MF12 and MF39: either close them in a follow-up commit if still applicable, mark them stale in the triage plan, or capture as their own deferred todos with concrete scope.

Feature-gate SANDBOX_INSTALL_TEST_* and SANDBOX_UPDATE_TEST_* Sigstore env-var redirects so they compile out of production builds. Today the env reads at sandbox-cli/src/update/fetch.rs:252-272 (and the parallel install.sh injection points) are unconditional — an operator running a release-built sandbox can set SANDBOX_UPDATE_TEST_FULCIO_ROOT + SANDBOX_UPDATE_TEST_REKOR_URL + SANDBOX_UPDATE_TEST_REKOR_PUBLIC_KEY + SANDBOX_UPDATE_TEST_CT_LOG_PUBLIC_KEY and substitute their own cosign trust root. The comment at fetch.rs:250 claims to 'mirror the route-helper's test-env-override Cargo feature pattern' but that pattern uses #[cfg(feature = "test-env-override")] to strip the code out of production binaries; this one does not. Fix: gate the env-read block under #[cfg(feature = "test-env-override")] (or a sibling feature), enable the feature in the install-e2e harness build, and verify a release-profile build no longer carries the env-var names as strings. install.sh shell injection is a separate concern (shell scripts can't be feature-gated like Rust); evaluate whether to leave-as-is, hardcode-prod, or add a build-time substitution.

Workspace cargo fmt sweep: ~29 pre-existing fmt-check failures on master across sandbox-cli/core/sandboxd + several integration tests. 'make build' invokes 'fmt-check' first and fails on this. Either land a one-shot 'cargo fmt --all' commit or pin failing files via rustfmt::skip. Surfaced during M16-S13.

M16-S17 docs sweep: update verification-matrix commands in milestone files to use 'make test-integration' (which encodes --features sandbox-route-helper/test-env-override) rather than the bare 'cargo nextest run --workspace --profile integration'. The bare form fails with sha-mismatch panics in route-helper tests. Affects future agents running per-session verification matrices.

install-e2e tarball-cache staleness vector: tests/install-e2e/conftest.py's release_tarball_* fixture invalidates only on sandboxd/**/*.rs mtime (skipping target/), missing changes to build-local-tarball.sh, install.sh, Cargo.toml, and Cargo.lock. A future session touching only the build pipeline (no .rs change) will silently reuse a stale tarball and test stale code. Fix: extend mtime walk to include build-local-tarball.sh, install.sh, and Cargo manifests; or hash the tarball-relevant source set into the cache key.

Daemon observability gap: hyper aborts in-flight HTTP responses silently when the client half-closes read side before response flush. No tracing, no journal entry. Add a tracing seam on the response-abort path — e.g. wrap the Unix listener's accept loop with a connection-level observer that logs when a connection drops with response_in_flight=true. Surfaced during M16-S14 diagnosis of the peercred-connector half-close bug. Defensive observability for future production debugging.

install-e2e _run helper at tests/install-e2e/conftest.py:80 uses subprocess.run(..., text=True) for every call, which universal-newline-translates CRLF to LF. Any future test that needs to inspect raw wire-shape HTTP bytes from a guest-side process will hit the same masking bug M16-S14 found in _parse_http_status. Fix: add a capture_bytes=True option to _run that switches to bytes mode and skips the text decoder; OR add a prominent docstring warning. Surfaced during M16-S14 Phase 2.

Cross-operator keep-alive regression test gap: no install-e2e test currently exercises the two-request keep-alive pattern under a non-owner uid (e.g. bob sandbox ps + bob sandbox describe over the same hyper connection). The handoff's reported 'operation was canceled on 2nd request' symptom did not reproduce after M16-S14's fix, but no test pins the post-fix behavior. Add a targeted test that issues two sequential requests over a kept-alive peercred-connector connection (or via the CLI's keep-alive sender) under a non-owner uid and asserts both complete.

Trillian tree-ID stability for local sigstore stack: rekor's --trillian_log_server.tlog_id defaults to 0 which creates a new tree on every Rekor start, even with persistent MySQL and persistent signing key (M16-S15's fix). Cached .sigstore bundles referencing the old tree's logID trip 'signature not found in transparency log'. Fix: pin tlog_id via Trillian admin pre-provisioning OR a stateful first-run probe that writes the assigned id to disk and reads it back on subsequent boots. Surfaced during M16-S15; out of scope for #188's key-persistence fix.

integration_cp_lima_* 'operation was canceled' flake under load: surfaced once during M16-S15 verification matrix run #2. axum/hyper cancellation on a fake-daemon unix-socket request. Separate flake class from #178/#187. Passed cleanly on next run. Worth a stand-alone investigation if it surfaces again — possibly related to the keep-alive symptoms M16-S14 didn't fully diagnose, or a fake-daemon harness lifetime issue.

Hermetic CLI unit tests for fetch_session_counts and classify_stopped_sessions (sandbox-cli/src/update/mod.rs ~lines 318, 431) that pin the snake_case wire-format contract. The M16-S16 install-e2e test catches regression via end-to-end behavior, but a CLI-layer unit test would catch it locally without needing the install-e2e harness. Approach: stage a wire-shaped Vec<SessionDto> response over an in-process Unix-socket fixture (similar to sandboxd/sandboxd/tests/integration_* daemon harness), or add a small http_get_override seam and run inline. Cover: (a) lowercase 'stopped' counted into stopped bucket, (b) SessionState round-trip wire format matches struct field name expectations, (c) classify_stopped_sessions filters by lowercase. Scope: ~150 LOC new tests file OR 2 tests inline + small seam. Effort: 30-60 min.

Stale rustdoc on sandbox-cli/src/main.rs Command::Doctor variant (~line 353-355): comment says 'Full check implementation lands in a follow-up session — this stub is the dispatch arm', but doctor.rs has shipped the full 13-check implementation (~2200 lines, C1-C13 with parallel execution) for several sessions. One-line edit. Surfaced during M16-S17 docs sweep.

docs/operate/update.md stateful-step table off-by-one with spec § 3.2 sub-headings: the doc promotes daemon_reload to its own row (it's a logged sub-step inside § 3.2.23 install_unit with its own step=daemon_reload log line at update/mod.rs:2022), shifting step indices 26-30 against spec § 3.2.26-30. M16-S17 left this as-is because renumbering would either need to drop the daemon_reload row or rephrase the surrounding prose. Operator impact is minimal (table is a flow summary, not a spec citation). Worth resolving in a future docs-cycle.

docs/guides/lite-mode.md :::note block claims docs/lite.md resolves to lite-mode.md on the published site. The container backend's first-use warning (networking/.../container.rs) hard-codes docs/lite.md as a URL fragment. Verify the docs-site renderer's alias config redirects docs/lite.md → docs/guides/lite-mode.md; if not, either fix the static-site config or change the code-side URL to match what actually publishes. Surfaced during M16-S17; out-of-scope as it's a site-config question.

Reverse M16-S13 decision: feature-gate (or hardcode-prod) the SANDBOX_INSTALL_TEST_* env-var reads in scripts/install.sh:777-806 + _DEBUG_COSIGN_STDERR + _SKIP_SIGSTORE so they cannot affect a production install. Non-negotiable rule: zero test env-var names must reach a production binary or production shell artifact. Shell scripts can't be Cargo-feature-gated, so the path is build-time substitution (m4/envsubst-style) OR a sed pass in the release pipeline that strips the test-env blocks before tarball packaging. Verify with: grep -E 'SANDBOX_(INSTALL|UPDATE)_TEST_' on the released install.sh returns zero matches; install-e2e harness builds from source-tree install.sh and still honors the env vars. The comment at install.sh:755 ('mirrors the route-helper test-env-override pattern') becomes accurate once the shell side is also stripped. Spec 4 delivery row P4.36 needs updating to reflect the new gate.

Tear out the layered cache-staleness defenses for the local Sigstore stack, replaced by #195's always-rebuild policy. Specifically: (1) revert tests/install-e2e/sigstore-stack/docker-compose.yml's '--rekor_server.signer=/etc/rekor/signing.key' to '--rekor_server.signer=memory' and drop the signing-key volume mount; (2) drop the ECDSA P-256 keygen block in tests/install-e2e/sigstore-stack/init.sh and the state/rekor/signing.key material it produces; (3) delete tests/install-e2e/test_sigstore_stack_smoke.py::test_rekor_public_key_survives_container_restart (becomes meaningless under always-rebuild); (4) delete the mtime-tracked re-sign branches in tests/install-e2e/conftest.py's release_tarball_* fixtures (around lines 329-352 and 467-478) plus the state/rekor.pub.cached.pem cache file. Rationale: bundles never outlive the Rekor instance that signed them under #195's always-rebuild, so neither persistent signing key nor Trillian tree-ID pinning has a job to do. Single staleness defense (always-rebuild) replaces three layered ones. Supersedes #199. Verification: docker compose down/up sigstore-stack between pytest runs no longer affects test results; install-e2e suite green; no SANDBOX_*_TEST_REKOR_PUBLIC_KEY mtime drift in test logs.

sandbox-cli/src/update/mod.rs:208 references https://Koriit.github.io/sandboxd/docs/migrate-dev-to-system but no such page exists under docs/. Same class of issue as #204 (stale published URL). Decide canonical slug under the published site (likely 'guides/migrate-dev-to-system' or similar) and rewrite. Surfaced during M16-S18 bundle 6.

site/astro.config.mjs:48 still uses placeholder 'site: "https://example.github.io"'. TODO comment flags it for the future repo-slug-known session. Replace with real published origin once the GitHub Pages URL is finalized; absolute-URL generation in the published site is currently nonsensical. Surfaced during M16-S18 bundle 6.

tests/e2e/conftest.py preflight calls 'docker image inspect sandbox-gateway' (implicit :latest), but 'make gateway-image' tags 'sandbox-gateway:<workspace-version>' (currently 0.1.0). On any clean host the matrix skips all 119 tests with misleading 'build it with: make gateway-image' message. Fix: read version from sandboxd/sandbox-core/Cargo.toml or match the daemon's gateway_image_tag_for_version helper. Latent since commit 92cbd6d. Surfaced during M16-S19.

Add '--durations=20' to test-e2e-container and test-e2e-matrix Makefile targets so future post-arc verification runs capture per-test reference timings without rerunning. One-line Makefile change per target. Surfaced during M16-S19 — the run produced only aggregate '119 passed in 5525.76s' which doesn't help with future timing-regression triage.

Lift the in-session '_schema_version:1' literal in tests/e2e/conftest.py into a more durable harness shape: import the constant from a generated bindings module (or read sandbox-core/users_conf.rs's DAEMON_MIN_SUPPORTED_USERS_CONF_SCHEMA at test-collection time). Goal: future schema-version bumps fail at test-collection, not at daemon-startup time mid-matrix. Surfaced during M16-S19.

Fix pre-existing E2E tests broken by M17-S1 breaking default: test_lite.py::test_lite_workspace_uid_alignment and test_workspace.py::test_shared_mount[container] both rely on the now-removed implicit /home/agent/workspace mount point. Update to pass explicit :<guest_path> token. Best raised in M17-S4 six-track review.

M17-S5 manual-replay artefact captures: render Lima YAML, docker create argv text, host-side stat -c %F + getfattr -d output, verbatim 409 response bodies for stop+delete-when-locked, rsync happy-path stderr. Reference M17.md:237-249. Deferred from S1-S4 via M17.md exit-criteria deferrals.

Fix parse_remote_helper_url default at sandbox-cli/src/main.rs:5384 — defaults slash-less sandbox::<session> URLs to /home/agent/workspace, which the M17-S1 breaking default no longer implicitly provides. Pre-existing pre-M17 but operator-visible now. Either error 'specify a path' or fall back to session's actual host_path via daemon API.

Consolidate parallel rsync argv builders: sandbox-core/src/workspace_rsync.rs:62 build_argv (daemon initial push) + sandbox-cli/src/main.rs:4538 plan_workspace_sync_argv (CLI push/pull). Wire shape shared by convention; no live drift; both well-tested. Refactor into one sandbox-core helper with tagged options struct (Direction, MkPath, DryRun, SymlinkPolicy, Filter).

Matrix-resilience hardening: make test-e2e-matrix exhibits cumulative-state flakes on long sequential runs (8/137 failed in 1h42m run, all 8 passed in isolation post-matrix). Investigate per-test cleanup gaps (Docker network pool exhaustion, rsync fixture image staleness, port allocations, test daemon base-dir GC). Goal: matrix passes clean end-to-end so M17-style verification sessions don't have to interpret 'matrix clean' as 'all isolated passes'.

BLOCKS M18-S9: Root-cause daemon-spawned QEMU exit-1 under sandbox uid. Works from interactive 'sudo -u sandbox' shell; fails when daemon spawns. Likely fd-table / sigmask / controlling-terminal / setsid difference. Investigate before M18-S9 full Lima matrix run. See diff-the-outcomes.md A1/A2 failure mode and M18-S1 log discovery.

Rework test_daemon_restart_recovery (and matching test_lite.py restart test) for systemd harness: they currently swap sandbox_daemon['process'] for a fresh Popen, incompatible with _SystemdDaemonHandle. Should drive 'systemctl restart sandboxd-test' instead. Not in M18-S1 acceptance set so OK to land later, but needed before any session that runs the full e2e matrix under sandbox-systemd.

systemd journal capture for failure dumps under sandbox-systemd: _dump_daemon_log_on_failure autouse fixture currently dumps empty _stdout_log/_stderr_log placeholders because daemon output goes to journal. Improve _SystemdDaemonHandle teardown / fixture to 'journalctl -u sandboxd-test --since=<test-start>' on failure.

Cosmetic: move StartLimitIntervalSec from [Service] to [Unit] in sandboxd/contrib/systemd/sandboxd.service (modern systemd warns the [Service] placement is deprecated since systemd 230; unit still loads but logs a warning at every load).

BLOCKS M18-S3/S4 cross-user contract: resolve the in-container uid alignment so sshd can start when daemon-uid != 1000. Three candidate approaches: (a) always --user 1000:1000 inside the lite container, picking a different mechanism for workspace bind-mount ownership; (b) bind-mount a synthetic /etc/passwd alongside /run/sandbox/authorized_keys so getpwuid(daemon_uid) returns a usable entry; (c) image-build-time uid parameterisation so in-image sandbox uid matches daemon uid at runtime. M18-S3 should pick one before keypair injection — without this, the proxy in M18-S4 opens tunnels that fail on the sshd side. See M18-S2 discovery log entries for the load-bearing OpenSSH getpwuid constraint.

Daemon-observable sshd-readiness state on the session row so M18-S4's proxy endpoint can short-circuit when sshd never started (best-effort start failure under cross-user uid), instead of opening a WebSocket tunnel that 401s or hangs. Probe approach: after container start, poll 'docker exec <ctr> ss -tlnp' or 'sshd -t' once, set a boolean column on the session record. Lazy proxy uses that to refuse with a structured error.

M18-S8 e2e-test-quality review track: investigate integration_proxy_websocket_round_trip_container_backend flake (container exits before sshd banner observed; reproducible by retry). Surfaced during M18-S7 E2E verification — pre-existing race in container lifecycle / sshd startup ordering inside the test. Either add a wait-for-sshd-ready probe before the banner read, or use the M18-S2 best-effort sshd start log line as a readiness gate.

Pre-M18 milestone-tag cleanup pass: networking/gateway/Corefile:14,23 (M10-S2/M10-S10) and sandboxd/sandbox-core/migrations/V004__port_explicit_policy_rules.sql:25 (M10-S2). Both violate CLAUDE.md no-milestone-tags rule; were out-of-M18-S8-scope. SQL migrations CANNOT be modified due to refinery checksum constraint (see remember item #18) — needs a follow-up migration approach if pursued. Corefile is freely editable. Candidate for a general-purpose convention-violation sweep ticket.

Drift-recovery retry-path test at unit/integration/e2e layers. M18-S6's run_with_drift_recovery wrapper has hermetic state-machine tests but no full retry-path coverage that actually drives a stale local entry → daemon refresh → retry. Flagged by M18-S8 tracks 3+4 as SHOULD-FIX. Bundle with related M18-S8 docs SHOULD-FIX leftovers if convenient: (a) drift-recovery troubleshooting paragraph in docs/guides/troubleshooting.md, (b) install.sh Step 12 cross-link in docs/start/installation.md, (c) lite-mode bundled-sshd docker-exec-socat subsection in docs/guides/lite-mode.md.

Daemon proxy: container-path non-zero-exit BACKEND_ERROR emission is currently un-emittable because the ferry has already half-closed the WebSocket by the time the docker-exec child's exit status is observable. Would require restructuring bidirectional_ferry to return its WebSocket halves on early backend exit so the daemon can send a structured close frame. Reasonable post-M18 follow-up. The 4001 BACKEND_UNAVAILABLE close (pre-ferry dial failure) handles the most common operator-actionable signal already — this 4002 gap only affects late-detected backend exits.

Manual VS Code Remote-SSH connect verification (gate G9 from M18 delivery map): point VS Code Remote-SSH (or JetBrains Gateway) at sandbox-<id> alias from ~/.ssh/sandbox/, confirm the IDE connects through the ProxyCommand shim to the in-container/in-VM sshd. Operator-facing walkthrough at docs/guides/external-ssh-tools.md. User-elected to defer at M18-S9 close; underlying SSH+ProxyCommand wire is CI-covered. Risk is limited to GUI-side walkthrough accuracy.

M18-S10 deferred: lite-image entrypoint chown for the non-1000 operator case. Container --user <operator_uid> is wired (c1d4782); for operator uid 1000 (the common dev + CI case) this matches the lite-image's /home/sandbox uid 1000 and works without any chown. For operator uid != 1000, the container runs as that uid but /home/sandbox is owned by 1000 mode 0700 → EACCES on home-dir writes. Fix paths considered: (a) drop Dockerfile 'USER 1000:1000' + bundle gosu/su-exec, container starts as root and entrypoint chowns + drops to operator — bigger image surface; (b) bind-mount a host-side /home/sandbox dir pre-chowned to operator_uid by the daemon — refactor of image's /home/sandbox semantics; (c) chmod 0777 /home/sandbox in the image — security regression. None of (a)/(b)/(c) is M18 critical-path. File for M18-S11 review or M19. Surfaces only when operator uid != 1000.

M18-S10 deferred: Cause A test-fixture literal sweep (~116 /home/agent + /home/sandbox occurrences across 13 files — 9 Python e2e + 4 Rust integration). Estimate from M18-S9 triage was ~40 across 7 files; actual count is ~3x larger. Each literal needs semantic verification (Lima /home/agent vs container /home/sandbox are distinct paths post-M18-S2 rename, not interchangeable). Plan: add LIMA_HOME and CONTAINER_HOME constants in tests/e2e/conftest.py (Python) and a sandbox-core test-utility module (Rust); route literals through the constants. NOT a daemon regression — fixture-hygiene work. Re-scope from M18-S10 to M18-S11 review pass where fixture cleanup naturally lives. Matrix run will show these as known fixture failures; classify in matrix handoff.

Fix install-lima-helper-test-cap (and audit route/spawn-helper) stamp staleness: the stamp compares the default (no-feature) build artifact against the test-env-override-feature installed binary, so hashes always differ and the stamp can report 'already configured' without reinstalling the current feature-gated binary. Risk: bare 'cargo nextest --profile integration' runs helper integration tests against a stale installed test binary. Make the stamp depend on the feature-gated artifact, or force reinstall. Surfaced M18-S12; candidate for S14 test-infra track.

S14/S15: get the 4 cross-user pivot e2e tests (test_cross_user_helper_pivot.py) + Lima e2e matrix GREEN. Flip MECHANISM is proven (manual operator-uid limactl start through per-operator LIMA_HOME works; integration proxy test reaches a real Lima VM via helper). Automated pivot tests blocked on e2e-harness/timing issues, NOT flip correctness: (a) 90s SSH-connect timeout vs ~44s manual — bump/profile; (b) base-image instance REUSE re-runs disk-resize on a used disk (possible corruption) — ensure fresh-instance-per-run or proper teardown; (c) HOME=/var/lib/sandbox boot slower than operator HOME. This is S14 e2e-test-quality (Track 4) territory; S15 is the unconditional full-matrix gate.

S14: base_image_lock uses a single coarse AppState Mutex<()> serializing base-image builds across ALL operators, instead of the spec's per-operator serialization via the LimaManagerRegistry Mutex<HashMap>. Safe but not matching spec intent (distinct operators needlessly block). Code-quality/impl-vs-spec item for S14.

S14: extend the test sudoers fragment env_keep to include SANDBOX_LIMA_HELPER_PATH so the 'sudo -u sandbox' e2e harness fallback exercises the cross-user Lima path (currently only the systemd-unit harness path carries it). Same gap the old spawn-helper had.

S14: cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings has ~7 uninlined_format_args lints in sandbox-core test code (the official S13 criterion without --all-targets is clean). Also a cfg(test) branch in LimaManagerRegistry::get_or_create that diverts ensure_operator_lima_home to a tmpdir override — code-quality review item. Clean these in S14 Track 2/3.

Lima cgroup limits silently skipped for service-account operators lacking a systemd user manager (/run/user/<uid>): the QEMU wrapper's 'systemctl --user' probe fails without linger/active-session, so session VMs boot without MemoryMax/CPUQuota/TasksMax. Fix XDG_RUNTIME_DIR (74fd5bd) restores limits for operators WITH a user manager (interactive/linger). Consider a system-slice fallback (systemd-run --system / a sandbox.slice in the system manager) so limits apply regardless of operator login state, or document loginctl enable-linger as a hard requirement. Surfaced M18-S14.

remember.text: KVM access: /dev/kvm needs chmod 666 or newgrp kvm in this VM. Group membership doesn't take effect without re-login. Fix with: sudo chmod 666 /dev/kvm
Lima does NOT support kernel AF_VSOCK. Using TCP-over-SSH-tunnel instead. Guest agent listens on TCP port inside VM, host connects via Lima SSH port forward. Protocol unchanged (framed JSON).
VM networking uses two NICs: eth0=SLIRP (Lima SSH/management), eth1=TAP on Docker bridge (data, hot-added via QMP at ~/.lima/{vm}/qmp.sock). Guest agent configures eth1 static IP.

Privilege model: sandboxd runs as regular user (docker+kvm groups). nftables via docker exec with CAP_NET_ADMIN in container. TAP devices via qemu-bridge-helper (setuid, ships with QEMU). No sudo/sudoers/root anywhere. Host-side bridge/TAP/veth code in network.rs to be fully removed.
Run failing E2E tests individually during debugging. Only run full suite for final validation.
Host VM has 8 CPUs and 8GB RAM. Session VMs: 2 CPUs / 2 GB. Base image build VM: 4 CPUs / 4 GB (needs more resources for cloud-init + apt). No timeout may exceed 600s (10 min).

Slow cloud-init root cause: flaky HTTP connections to Ubuntu mirrors (apt default 120s timeout per stalled connection). Fix: HTTPS sources + /etc/apt/apt.conf.d/99sandbox with 5s timeout, 5 retries, ForceIPv4. Base image build now ~82s.

Subagent delegation gotcha: subagents may ignore 'do not touch session-tracking' instructions and call the progress CLI. Orchestrator must verify session state after each return. Also: LSP diagnostics can be stale; ground-truth verification is cargo build from sandboxd/ workspace root.

Gateway container image must rebuild whenever networking/ changes. Makefile test-e2e depends on stamp-driven gateway-image target. Host sandboxd ↔ gateway addon version drift is silent when the addon falls back to allow-all on unknown shape — pin both sides with unit tests (see policy.rs mitmproxy_config_matches_e2e_failing_policies and test_policy_addon.py::TestLegacyPreM9S10ShapeIsRejected).

Session plan moved to docs/internal/session-plan.md during M9-S16. progress.json plan field still points at docs/session-plan.md — a future progress CLI update should fix it or ignore when referring to plan.

Spec > plan override: .tasks/specs/2026-04-21-...-design.md line 846 uses 'rate_limited_count' for the summary event field. M10-S3 plan Q5 accidentally renamed it to 'dropped_events_count'; this was landed in Phase 1 commits 0fcc3ca-569f89a then corrected. When spec prose and plan override disagree on a wire field name, spec wins — call the discrepancy out in review rather than silently following the plan.

Default to running continuously to finish the full plan — do not check in between sessions. Open the next session and proceed unless something genuinely requires user input (missing credentials, ambiguous spec, broken environment).
Do NOT push to origin when running with session tracking active. Commits stay local; user pushes manually. Don't ask about pushing.

When delegating parallel sweep agents touching the same workspace (cleanup passes, refactors with broad file impact), explicitly prohibit 'git stash' and 'git stash pop' in the delegation prompts. Instruct agents to use 'git show <commit>:<path>' or 'git log <path>' for pre-existing-state checks. Stash captures the entire dirty tree including sibling work; pop restores to a tree siblings have moved on from, silently dropping edits with only a 'Dropped' message. Discovered M12-S6 cost ~250 sites of cleanup work.

VM has limited memory: do NOT run multiple agents in parallel that both do development work AND run tests (e.g. cargo build/nextest, pytest, full E2E). Serialize implementer subagents; the host can't sustain the combined working sets. OK to parallelize agents that only read or only run a single test process at a time.

Integration tests REQUIRE the 'sandbox-route-helper/test-env-override' Cargo feature flag — running 'cargo nextest run --profile integration' bare will produce ~17 spurious failures (route-helper tests, integration_doctor_*, integration_session_create_refused_*). The canonical invocation is 'make test-integration' which sets the feature + depends on gateway-image, lite-image, install-route-helper-test-cap. Do NOT trust subagent claims of 'pre-existing failures' on integration tests — verify by running 'make test-integration' before accepting.

refinery::embed_migrations! checksums migration file content. Modifying SQL or even narrative comments in an already-deployed migration breaks startup on any host that has applied it. V007's stale 'keys/<id>' narrative cannot be safely fixed in-place — needs a follow-up migration if updated. Schema-narrative drift must live in adjacent source files (ssh.rs, dto.rs docs) instead of in migration files.

Parallel-wave coordination gotcha: when multiple subagents share a working tree, uncommitted in-progress edits in one wave can be reverted by sibling waves doing 'git checkout HEAD -- <path>'. M18-S8 Wave A briefly reverted Wave B's unstaged docs edits this way. Mitigation for future parallel-wave delegations: each wave commits to a temp branch eagerly, or write non-overlapping file sets up front with strict scope boundaries. Wave-isolation rules in the delegation prompt help but aren't ironclad.

append_system_prompt:
We are building sandboxd \u2014 an isolated Linux environment daemon for coding agents (Lima/QEMU VMs and hardened Docker containers).

Current state and active session: docs/internal/session-plan.md
Milestone detail per session: docs/internal/milestones/M13.md \u2026 M18.md
Current spec under implementation: .tasks/specs/2026-05-29-m18-s10-sandbox-lima-helper-design/2026-05-29-m18-s10-sandbox-lima-helper-design-spec.md

Use /session-tracking for progress tracking. Log decisions and discoveries \u2014 they survive compaction. Use progress block to pause if you hit something only the user can resolve.

You are running unsupervised \u2014 make autonomous decisions and do not wait for user input.

Keep context clean: delegate exploration and implementation to subagents. Reserve the main conversation for decisions and coordination. Leverage teams for build/test work \u2014 those produce large logs and benefit from a dedicated teammate.

Environment: Linux host with Docker (rootful), Lima, /dev/kvm accessible, passwordless sudo available. Install packages as needed. Note though that end user runs on a different machine and does not have passwordless sudo.
